1→package com.secman.controller
     2→
     3→import com.secman.dto.CreateAwsAccountSharingRequest
     4→import com.secman.service.AwsAccountSharingService
     5→import com.secman.repository.UserRepository
     6→import io.micronaut.http.HttpResponse
     7→import io.micronaut.http.HttpStatus
     8→import io.micronaut.http.annotation.*
     9→import io.micronaut.security.annotation.Secured
    10→import io.micronaut.security.authentication.Authentication
    11→import io.micronaut.security.rules.SecurityRule
    12→import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable
    13→import jakarta.validation.Valid
    14→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    15→
    16→/**
    17→ * REST controller for AWS Account Sharing management.
    18→ *
    19→ * Feature: AWS Account Sharing
    20→ *
    21→ * - ADMIN users: full access to all sharing rules (list all, create any, delete any)
    22→ * - Non-admin users with AWS accounts: scoped access to their own outgoing sharing rules
    23→ *   (list own, create where they are source, delete their own)
    24→ */
    25→@Controller("/api/aws-account-sharing")
    26→@Secured(SecurityRule.IS_AUTHENTICATED)
    27→open class AwsAccountSharingController(
    28→    private val awsAccountSharingService: AwsAccountSharingService,
    29→    private val userRepository: UserRepository
    30→) {
    31→    private val logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(AwsAccountSharingController::class.java)
    32→
    33→    /**
    34→     * GET /api/aws-account-sharing - List sharing rules.
    35→     * ADMINs see all rules; non-admins see only rules where they are the source user.
    36→     */
    37→    @Get
    38→    fun listSharingRules(
    39→        @QueryValue("page") page: Int?,
    40→        @QueryValue("size") size: Int?,
    41→        authentication: Authentication
    42→    ): HttpResponse<Map<String, Any>> {
    43→        logger.debug("Listing AWS account sharing rules: page=$page, size=$size")
    44→
    45→        return try {
    46→            val result = if (isAdmin(authentication)) {
    47→                awsAccountSharingService.listSharingRules(page ?: 0, size ?: 20)
    48→            } else {
    49→                val userId = getUserIdFromAuthentication(authentication)
    50→                awsAccountSharingService.listSharingRulesForSourceUser(userId, page ?: 0, size ?: 20)
    51→            }
    52→            HttpResponse.ok(result)
    53→        } catch (e: Exception) {
    54→            logger.error("Failed to list AWS account sharing rules", e)
    55→            HttpResponse.serverError(mapOf(
    56→                "error" to "Internal Server Error",
    57→                "message" to "Failed to list sharing rules"
    58→            ))
    59→        }
    60→    }
    61→
    62→    /**
    63→     * POST /api/aws-account-sharing - Create a new sharing rule.
    64→     * ADMINs can create any rule; non-admins can only create rules where they are the source.
    65→     */
    66→    @Post
    67→    open fun createSharingRule(
    68→        @Valid @Body request: CreateAwsAccountSharingRequest,
    69→        authentication: Authentication
    70→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
    71→        logger.info("Creating AWS account sharing rule: source=${request.sourceUserId}, target=${request.targetUserId}")
    72→
    73→        return try {
    74→            val currentUserId = getUserIdFromAuthentication(authentication)
    75→
    76→            // Non-admin users can only share their own accounts
    77→            if (!isAdmin(authentication) && request.sourceUserId != currentUserId) {
    78→                return HttpResponse.status<Any>(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN).body(mapOf(
    79→                    "error" to "Forbidden",
    80→                    "message" to "You can only share your own AWS accounts"
    81→                ))
    82→            }
    83→
    84→            val result = awsAccountSharingService.createSharingRule(request, currentUserId)
    85→            HttpResponse.status<Any>(HttpStatus.CREATED).body(result)
    86→        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
    87→            logger.warn("AWS account sharing creation failed: ${e.message}")
    88→            HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf(
    89→                "error" to "Validation Error",
    90→                "message" to (e.message ?: "Invalid request")
    91→            ))
    92→        } catch (e: IllegalStateException) {
    93→            logger.warn("AWS account sharing creation failed: ${e.message}")
    94→            HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf(
    95→                "error" to "Conflict",
    96→                "message" to (e.message ?: "Sharing rule already exists")
    97→            ))
    98→        } catch (e: NoSuchElementException) {
    99→            HttpResponse.notFound(mapOf(
   100→                "error" to "Not Found",
   101→                "message" to (e.message ?: "User not found")
   102→            ))
   103→        }
   104→    }
   105→
   106→    /**
   107→     * DELETE /api/aws-account-sharing/{id} - Delete a sharing rule.
   108→     * ADMINs can delete any rule; non-admins can only delete rules where they are the source.
   109→     */
   110→    @Delete("/{id}")
   111→    fun deleteSharingRule(
   112→        @PathVariable id: Long,
   113→        authentication: Authentication
   114→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
   115→        logger.info("Deleting AWS account sharing rule: id=$id")
   116→
   117→        return try {
   118→            // Non-admin users can only delete their own rules
   119→            if (!isAdmin(authentication)) {
   120→                val currentUserId = getUserIdFromAuthentication(authentication)
   121→                if (!awsAccountSharingService.isOwnedByUser(id, currentUserId)) {
   122→                    return HttpResponse.status<Any>(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN).body(mapOf(
   123→                        "error" to "Forbidden",
   124→                        "message" to "You can only delete your own sharing rules"
   125→                    ))
   126→                }
   127→            }
   128→
   129→            awsAccountSharingService.deleteSharingRule(id)
   130→            HttpResponse.noContent<Void>()
   131→        } catch (e: NoSuchElementException) {
   132→            logger.warn("AWS account sharing rule not found: $id")
   133→            HttpResponse.notFound<Any>()
   134→        }
   135→    }
   136→
   137→    private fun getUserIdFromAuthentication(authentication: Authentication): Long {
   138→        val userId = authentication.attributes["userId"]
   139→        return when (userId) {
   140→            is Long -> userId
   141→            is Int -> userId.toLong()
   142→            is String -> userId.toLong()
   143→            else -> throw IllegalStateException("Unable to determine user ID from authentication")
   144→        }
   145→    }
   146→
   147→    /**
   148→     * GET /api/aws-account-sharing/users - Lightweight user list for the sharing form.
   149→     * Returns minimal user info (id, username, email) for populating target user dropdowns.
   150→     * Available to all authenticated users so non-admins can select sharing targets.
   151→     */
   152→    @Get("/users")
   153→    fun listUsersForSharing(): HttpResponse<List<SharingUserResponse>> {
   154→        val users = userRepository.findAll().map { user ->
   155→            SharingUserResponse(
   156→                id = user.id!!,
   157→                username = user.username,
   158→                email = user.email
   159→            )
   160→        }
   161→        return HttpResponse.ok(users)
   162→    }
   163→
   164→    private fun isAdmin(authentication: Authentication): Boolean {
   165→        return authentication.roles.contains("ADMIN")
   166→    }
   167→
   168→    @Serdeable
   169→    data class SharingUserResponse(
   170→        val id: Long,
   171→        val username: String,
   172→        val email: String
   173→    )
   174→}
   175→

1→package com.secman.service
     2→
     3→import com.secman.domain.AwsAccountSharing
     4→import com.secman.dto.AwsAccountSharingResponse
     5→import com.secman.dto.CreateAwsAccountSharingRequest
     6→import com.secman.dto.toResponse
     7→import com.secman.repository.AwsAccountSharingRepository
     8→import com.secman.repository.UserMappingRepository
     9→import com.secman.repository.UserRepository
    10→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
    11→import jakarta.transaction.Transactional
    12→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    13→
    14→/**
    15→ * Service for managing AWS Account Sharing rules.
    16→ *
    17→ * Feature: AWS Account Sharing
    18→ *
    19→ * Provides business logic for creating, listing, and deleting sharing rules,
    20→ * as well as resolving shared AWS account IDs for access control.
    21→ */
    22→@Singleton
    23→open class AwsAccountSharingService(
    24→    private val awsAccountSharingRepository: AwsAccountSharingRepository,
    25→    private val userRepository: UserRepository,
    26→    private val userMappingRepository: UserMappingRepository
    27→) {
    28→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(AwsAccountSharingService::class.java)
    29→
    30→    /**
    31→     * List all sharing rules with source/target user details and shared account counts.
    32→     * Manually paginates the eager-fetched results to avoid N+1 and LAZY issues.
    33→     */
    34→    fun listSharingRules(page: Int, size: Int): Map<String, Any> {
    35→        val allRules = awsAccountSharingRepository.findAllWithUsers()
    36→        return paginateRules(allRules, page, size)
    37→    }
    38→
    39→    /**
    40→     * List sharing rules where the given user is the source (outgoing shares only).
    41→     * Used for non-admin users who can only manage their own sharing rules.
    42→     */
    43→    fun listSharingRulesForSourceUser(sourceUserId: Long, page: Int, size: Int): Map<String, Any> {
    44→        val allRules = awsAccountSharingRepository.findAllWithUsersBySourceUserId(sourceUserId)
    45→        return paginateRules(allRules, page, size)
    46→    }
    47→
    48→    private fun paginateRules(allRules: List<AwsAccountSharing>, page: Int, size: Int): Map<String, Any> {
    49→        val totalElements = allRules.size.toLong()
    50→        val totalPages = if (totalElements == 0L) 0 else ((totalElements + size - 1) / size).toInt()
    51→
    52→        val pagedRules = allRules
    53→            .drop(page * size)
    54→            .take(size)
    55→            .map { sharing ->
    56→                val count = userMappingRepository.findDistinctAwsAccountIdByEmail(sharing.sourceUser.email).size
    57→                sharing.toResponse(sharedAwsAccountCount = count)
    58→            }
    59→
    60→        return mapOf(
    61→            "content" to pagedRules,
    62→            "totalElements" to totalElements,
    63→            "totalPages" to totalPages,
    64→            "page" to page,
    65→            "size" to size
    66→        )
    67→    }
    68→
    69→    /**
    70→     * Create a new sharing rule.
    71→     *
    72→     * Validates:
    73→     * - source != target (no self-sharing)
    74→     * - Both users exist
    75→     * - No duplicate rule
    76→     * - Source user has at least one AWS account mapping
    77→     */
    78→    @Transactional
    79→    open fun createSharingRule(request: CreateAwsAccountSharingRequest, adminUserId: Long): AwsAccountSharingResponse {
    80→        if (request.sourceUserId == request.targetUserId) {
    81→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Source and target user cannot be the same")
    82→        }
    83→
    84→        val sourceUser = userRepository.findById(request.sourceUserId)
    85→            .orElseThrow { NoSuchElementException("Source user not found: ${request.sourceUserId}") }
    86→        val targetUser = userRepository.findById(request.targetUserId)
    87→            .orElseThrow { NoSuchElementException("Target user not found: ${request.targetUserId}") }
    88→        val adminUser = userRepository.findById(adminUserId)
    89→            .orElseThrow { IllegalStateException("Admin user not found: $adminUserId") }
    90→
    91→        if (awsAccountSharingRepository.existsBySourceUserIdAndTargetUserId(
    92→                request.sourceUserId, request.targetUserId)) {
    93→            throw IllegalStateException("Sharing rule already exists from ${sourceUser.email} to ${targetUser.email}")
    94→        }
    95→
    96→        val sourceAwsAccounts = userMappingRepository.findDistinctAwsAccountIdByEmail(sourceUser.email)
    97→        if (sourceAwsAccounts.isEmpty()) {
    98→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Source user ${sourceUser.email} has no AWS account mappings to share")
    99→        }
   100→
   101→        val sharing = AwsAccountSharing(
   102→            sourceUser = sourceUser,
   103→            targetUser = targetUser,
   104→            createdBy = adminUser
   105→        )
   106→
   107→        val saved = awsAccountSharingRepository.save(sharing)
   108→        log.info("AUDIT: AWS account sharing created: source={}, target={}, admin={}, sharedAccounts={}",
   109→            sourceUser.email, targetUser.email, adminUser.email, sourceAwsAccounts.size)
   110→
   111→        return saved.toResponse(sharedAwsAccountCount = sourceAwsAccounts.size)
   112→    }
   113→
   114→    /**
   115→     * Delete a sharing rule by ID.
   116→     */
   117→    @Transactional
   118→    open fun deleteSharingRule(sharingId: Long): Boolean {
   119→        val sharing = awsAccountSharingRepository.findById(sharingId)
   120→            .orElseThrow { NoSuchElementException("Sharing rule not found: $sharingId") }
   121→
   122→        awsAccountSharingRepository.delete(sharing)
   123→        log.info("AUDIT: AWS account sharing deleted: id={}, source={}, target={}",
   124→            sharingId, sharing.sourceUser.email, sharing.targetUser.email)
   125→        return true
   126→    }
   127→
   128→    /**
   129→     * Check if a sharing rule is owned by (source user of) the given user.
   130→     * Used for authorization: non-admin users can only delete their own rules.
   131→     */
   132→    fun isOwnedByUser(sharingId: Long, userId: Long): Boolean {
   133→        val sharing = awsAccountSharingRepository.findById(sharingId).orElse(null) ?: return false
   134→        return sharing.sourceUser.id == userId
   135→    }
   136→
   137→    /**
   138→     * Get all shared AWS account IDs for a target user.
   139→     * Used by access control services to determine asset visibility.
   140→     *
   141→     * Returns distinct AWS account IDs from all source users sharing with this target user.
   142→     */
   143→    fun getSharedAwsAccountIds(targetUserId: Long): List<String> {
   144→        return awsAccountSharingRepository.findSharedAwsAccountIdsByTargetUserId(targetUserId)
   145→    }
   146→
   147→    /**
   148→     * Get shared AWS account IDs by resolving the target user's email to their ID.
   149→     * Convenience method for services that have the email but not the user ID.
   150→     */
   151→    fun getSharedAwsAccountIdsByEmail(targetUserEmail: String): List<String> {
   152→        val user = userRepository.findByEmailIgnoreCase(targetUserEmail).orElse(null) ?: return emptyList()
   153→        return getSharedAwsAccountIds(user.id!!)
   154→    }
   155→}
   156→

package com.secman.repository

import com.secman.domain.AwsAccountSharing
import io.micronaut.data.annotation.Query
import io.micronaut.data.annotation.Repository
import io.micronaut.data.jpa.repository.JpaRepository
import io.micronaut.data.model.Page
import io.micronaut.data.model.Pageable
import java.util.Optional

/**
 * Repository for AwsAccountSharing entity.
 *
 * Feature: AWS Account Sharing
 */
@Repository
interface AwsAccountSharingRepository : JpaRepository<AwsAccountSharing, Long> {

    fun findByTargetUserId(targetUserId: Long): List<AwsAccountSharing>

    fun countByTargetUserId(targetUserId: Long): Long

    fun findBySourceUserId(sourceUserId: Long): List<AwsAccountSharing>

    fun deleteBySourceUserId(sourceUserId: Long)

    fun deleteByTargetUserId(targetUserId: Long)

    fun deleteByCreatedBy_Id(createdById: Long)

    fun existsBySourceUserIdAndTargetUserId(sourceUserId: Long, targetUserId: Long): Boolean

    fun findBySourceUserIdAndTargetUserId(sourceUserId: Long, targetUserId: Long): Optional<AwsAccountSharing>

    fun deleteBySourceUserIdAndTargetUserId(sourceUserId: Long, targetUserId: Long)

    override fun findAll(pageable: Pageable): Page<AwsAccountSharing>

    /**
     * Find all sharing rules with eager-loaded user associations for admin listing.
     */
    @Query("""
        SELECT s FROM AwsAccountSharing s
        LEFT JOIN FETCH s.sourceUser
        LEFT JOIN FETCH s.targetUser
        LEFT JOIN FETCH s.createdBy
        ORDER BY s.createdAt DESC
    """)
    fun findAllWithUsers(): List<AwsAccountSharing>

    /**
     * Find sharing rules where the given user is the source, with eager-loaded associations.
     * Used for non-admin users who can only see their own outgoing sharing rules.
     */
    @Query("""
        SELECT s FROM AwsAccountSharing s
        LEFT JOIN FETCH s.sourceUser
        LEFT JOIN FETCH s.targetUser
        LEFT JOIN FETCH s.createdBy
        WHERE s.sourceUser.id = :sourceUserId
        ORDER BY s.createdAt DESC
    """)
    fun findAllWithUsersBySourceUserId(sourceUserId: Long): List<AwsAccountSharing>

    /**
     * Find all AWS account IDs accessible to a target user via sharing rules.
     * Joins aws_account_sharing -> users -> user_mapping to resolve shared accounts.
     *
     * This is the critical query for access control integration.
     * Non-transitive by design: only joins user_mapping (direct mappings),
     * not recursively computed visibility.
     */
    @Query(
        value = """
            SELECT DISTINCT um.aws_account_id
            FROM aws_account_sharing acs
            JOIN users u_source ON u_source.id = acs.source_user_id
            JOIN user_mapping um ON um.email = u_source.email AND um.aws_account_id IS NOT NULL
            WHERE acs.target_user_id = :targetUserId
        """,
        nativeQuery = true
    )
    fun findSharedAwsAccountIdsByTargetUserId(targetUserId: Long): List<String>
}

content.text:
Everything looks good. Let me summarize the complete change set.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
**Key design decisions in this implementation:**

1. **Role-based data scoping at the API level**: Rather than creating separate endpoints for admin vs non-admin, the same endpoints adapt their behavior based on the caller's role. The `GET` endpoint checks `isAdmin(authentication)` and either returns all rules or filters by `sourceUserId`. This is a common pattern in multi-tenant systems — the authorization logic lives in the controller, not duplicated across separate routes.

2. **Ownership-based authorization for mutations**: Non-admins can only `POST` with themselves as source, and only `DELETE` rules they own. The backend enforces this independently of the frontend — even if someone crafts a manual API call, the server rejects unauthorized operations.

3. **Lightweight user listing endpoint**: Since `/api/users` is admin-only, I added `/api/aws-account-sharing/users` that returns minimal user info (id, username, email). This avoids exposing sensitive admin data (roles, workgroups) to regular users while still letting them pick sharing targets.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

## Summary of all changes

### Backend (4 files)

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `AwsAccountSharingController.kt` | Changed `@Secured("ADMIN")` to `@Secured(IS_AUTHENTICATED)`. All 3 endpoints now check `isAdmin()` to decide behavior: admins see/manage all rules, non-admins are scoped to their own. Added `GET /users` endpoint for target user dropdown. |
| `AwsAccountSharingService.kt` | Added `listSharingRulesForSourceUser()` for scoped listing, extracted `paginateRules()` helper, added `isOwnedByUser()` for delete authorization. |
| `AwsAccountSharingRepository.kt` | Added `findAllWithUsersBySourceUserId()` JPQL query with eager-loaded joins. |

### Frontend (4 files)

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `AwsAccountSharingManager.tsx` | Detects admin vs non-admin. Non-admins: source user is fixed (disabled field), "Source User" column hidden, description text personalized, empty state message adjusted, target user dropdown filters out self. |
| `Sidebar.tsx` | Added "AWS Sharing" link under Vulnerability Management for non-admin users with `awsAccountCount > 0`. |
| `aws-account-sharing.astro` | **New file** — non-admin route at `/aws-account-sharing` (no `/admin/` prefix). |
| `awsAccountSharingService.ts` | Updated error messages to remove "ADMIN role required" wording. |

    18→import jakarta.inject.Inject

    21→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory

    23→import java.time.Instant
    24→import java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentHashMap
    25→import java.util.*
    26→
    27→@Controller("/api/auth")
    28→open class AuthController(
    29→    private val userRepository: UserRepository,
    30→    private val userMappingRepository: UserMappingRepository,
    31→    private val awsAccountSharingRepository: AwsAccountSharingRepository,
    32→    private val tokenGenerator: TokenGenerator,
    33→    private val inputValidationService: InputValidationService,
    34→    private val authCookieService: AuthCookieService
    35→) {
    36→
    37→    private val passwordEncoder = BCryptPasswordEncoder()
    38→    private val logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(AuthController::class.java)
    39→
    40→    // Login rate limiting: track failed attempts per username
    41→    private data class LoginAttemptRecord(val count: Int, val firstAttemptAt: Instant)
    42→    private val loginAttempts = ConcurrentHashMap<String, LoginAttemptRecord>()
    43→    private val maxLoginAttempts = 5
    44→    private val lockoutDurationSeconds = 900L // 15 minutes
    45→
    46→    private fun isLoginLocked(key: String): Boolean {
    47→        val record = loginAttempts[key] ?: return false
    48→        if (record.count >= maxLoginAttempts) {
    49→            val elapsed = java.time.Duration.between(record.firstAttemptAt, Instant.now()).seconds
    50→            if (elapsed < lockoutDurationSeconds) {
    51→                return true
    52→            }
    53→            // Lockout expired, reset
    54→            loginAttempts.remove(key)
    55→        }
    56→        return false
    57→    }
    58→
    59→    private fun recordFailedLogin(key: String) {
    60→        loginAttempts.compute(key) { _, existing ->
    61→            if (existing == null) {
    62→                LoginAttemptRecord(1, Instant.now())
    63→            } else {
    64→                val elapsed = java.time.Duration.between(existing.firstAttemptAt, Instant.now()).seconds
    65→                if (elapsed >= lockoutDurationSeconds) {
    66→                    LoginAttemptRecord(1, Instant.now()) // Reset window
    67→                } else {
    68→                    LoginAttemptRecord(existing.count + 1, existing.firstAttemptAt)
    69→                }
    70→            }
    71→        }
    72→    }
    73→
    74→    private fun clearFailedLogins(key: String) {
    75→        loginAttempts.remove(key)
    76→    }

    79→    data class LoginRequest(
    80→        val username: String,
    81→        val password: String
    82→    )

    87→        val username: String,
    88→        val email: String,
    89→        val roles: List<String>,
    90→        val workgroupCount: Long = 0,
    91→        val awsAccountCount: Long = 0,
    92→        val domainCount: Long = 0

    95→    @Serdeable
    96→    data class StatusResponse(
    97→        val id: Long,
    98→        val username: String,
    99→        val email: String,
   100→        val roles: List<String>,
   101→        val workgroupCount: Long = 0,
   102→        val awsAccountCount: Long = 0,

   106→    @Post("/login")
   107→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_ANONYMOUS)
   108→    @Transactional
   109→    open fun login(@Valid @Body loginRequest: LoginRequest): HttpResponse<*> {
   110→        if (loginRequest.username.isBlank() || loginRequest.password.isBlank()) {
   111→            return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf("error" to "Username and password are required"))
   112→        }
   113→        
   114→        // Validate username format
   115→        val usernameValidation = inputValidationService.validateName(loginRequest.username, "Username")
   116→        if (!usernameValidation.isValid) {
   117→            return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf("error" to usernameValidation.errorMessage))
   118→        }
   119→        
   120→        // Validate password length (basic check, don't reveal too much)
   121→        if (loginRequest.password.length > 200) {
   122→            return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf("error" to "Invalid credentials"))
   123→        }
   124→        
   125→        // Check for potential injection attempts
   126→        if (inputValidationService.containsCommandInjection(loginRequest.username) ||
   127→            inputValidationService.containsPathTraversal(loginRequest.username)) {
   128→            // Log the attempt but return generic error
   129→            return HttpResponse.unauthorized<Any>().body(mapOf("error" to "Invalid credentials"))
   130→        }
   131→
   132→        // Rate limiting: check if login is locked for this username
   133→        val rateLimitKey = loginRequest.username.lowercase()
   134→        if (isLoginLocked(rateLimitKey)) {
   135→            logger.warn("Login rate limit exceeded for user: {}", loginRequest.username)
   136→            return HttpResponse.status<Any>(io.micronaut.http.HttpStatus.TOO_MANY_REQUESTS)
   137→                .body(mapOf("error" to "Too many failed login attempts. Please try again later."))
   138→        }
   139→
   140→        val userOptional = userRepository.findByUsername(loginRequest.username)
   141→        
   142→        if (userOptional.isEmpty) {
   143→            recordFailedLogin(rateLimitKey)
   144→            return HttpResponse.unauthorized<Any>().body(mapOf("error" to "Invalid credentials"))
   145→        }
   146→
   147→        val user = userOptional.get()
   148→        
   149→        if (!passwordEncoder.matches(loginRequest.password, user.passwordHash)) {
   150→            recordFailedLogin(rateLimitKey)
   151→            return HttpResponse.unauthorized<Any>().body(mapOf("error" to "Invalid credentials"))
   152→        }
   153→
   154→        // Successful login - clear failed attempts
   155→        clearFailedLogins(rateLimitKey)
   156→
   157→        // Generate JWT token
   158→        val userDetails = mapOf(
   159→            "sub" to user.username,
   160→            "username" to user.username,
   161→            "email" to user.email,
   162→            "roles" to user.roles.map { it.name },
   163→            "iss" to "secman-backend-ng",
   164→            "userId" to user.id.toString()
   165→        )
   166→        
   167→        val tokenOptional = tokenGenerator.generateToken(userDetails)
   168→        
   169→        if (tokenOptional.isEmpty) {
   170→            return HttpResponse.serverError<Any>().body(mapOf("error" to "Failed to generate token"))
   171→        }
   172→
   173→        // Update last login timestamp
   174→        user.lastLogin = Instant.now()
   175→        userRepository.update(user)
   176→
   177→        val token = tokenOptional.get()
   178→
   179→        val directAwsCount = userMappingRepository.countDistinctAwsAccountsByEmail(user.email)
   180→        val sharedAwsCount = awsAccountSharingRepository.countByTargetUserId(user.id!!)
   181→
   182→        val response = LoginResponse(
   183→            id = user.id!!,
   184→            username = user.username,
   185→            email = user.email,
   186→            roles = user.roles.map { it.name },
   187→            workgroupCount = userRepository.countWorkgroupsByUsername(user.username),
   188→            awsAccountCount = directAwsCount + sharedAwsCount,
   189→            domainCount = userMappingRepository.countDistinctDomainsByEmail(user.email)
   190→        )
   191→
   192→        // Set JWT in HttpOnly secure cookie (primary auth mechanism)
   193→        return HttpResponse.ok(response).cookie(authCookieService.createAuthCookie(token))
   194→    }
   195→
   196→    @Post("/logout")
   197→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_AUTHENTICATED)
   198→    fun logout(): HttpResponse<*> {
   199→        // Clear the auth cookie by setting an expired cookie
   200→        return HttpResponse.ok(mapOf("message" to "Logged out successfully"))
   201→            .cookie(authCookieService.createLogoutCookie())
   202→    }
   203→
   204→    /**

   207→     */
   208→    @Post("/clear-session")
   209→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_ANONYMOUS)
   210→    fun clearSession(): HttpResponse<*> {
   211→        return HttpResponse.ok(mapOf("message" to "Session cleared"))
   212→            .cookie(authCookieService.createLogoutCookie())
   213→    }
   214→
   215→    @Get("/status")
   216→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_AUTHENTICATED)
   217→    fun status(authentication: Authentication): HttpResponse<*> {
   218→        val username = authentication.name
   219→        val userOptional = userRepository.findByUsername(username)
   220→
   221→        if (userOptional.isEmpty) {
   222→            return HttpResponse.unauthorized<Any>().body(mapOf("error" to "User not found"))
   223→        }
   224→
   225→        val user = userOptional.get()
   226→        val directAwsCount = userMappingRepository.countDistinctAwsAccountsByEmail(user.email)
   227→        val sharedAwsCount = awsAccountSharingRepository.countByTargetUserId(user.id!!)
   228→
   229→        val response = StatusResponse(
   230→            id = user.id!!,
   231→            username = user.username,
   232→            email = user.email,
   233→            roles = user.roles.map { it.name },
   234→            workgroupCount = userRepository.countWorkgroupsByUsername(user.username),
   235→            awsAccountCount = directAwsCount + sharedAwsCount,
   236→            domainCount = userMappingRepository.countDistinctDomainsByEmail(user.email)
   237→        )
   238→
   239→        return HttpResponse.ok(response)
   240→    }
   241→
   242→    @Serdeable
   243→    data class RefreshResponse(
   244→        val token: String,

   254→    /**
   255→     * Refresh the current JWT token.
   256→     * Issues a new token with extended expiration if the current token is still valid.
   257→     */
   258→    @Post("/refresh")
   259→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_AUTHENTICATED)
   260→    fun refresh(authentication: Authentication): HttpResponse<*> {
   261→        val username = authentication.name
   262→        val userOptional = userRepository.findByUsername(username)
   263→
   264→        if (userOptional.isEmpty) {
   265→            return HttpResponse.unauthorized<Any>().body(mapOf("error" to "User not found"))
   266→        }
   267→
   268→        val user = userOptional.get()
   269→
   270→        // Generate a fresh JWT token
   271→        val userDetails = mapOf(
   272→            "sub" to user.username,
   273→            "username" to user.username,
   274→            "email" to user.email,
   275→            "roles" to user.roles.map { it.name },
   276→            "iss" to "secman-backend-ng",
   277→            "userId" to user.id.toString()
   278→        )
   279→
   280→        val tokenOptional = tokenGenerator.generateToken(userDetails)
   281→
   282→        if (tokenOptional.isEmpty) {
   283→            return HttpResponse.serverError<Any>().body(mapOf("error" to "Failed to generate token"))
   284→        }
   285→
   286→        val token = tokenOptional.get()
   287→        // Update both the cookie and return the token in response
   288→        return HttpResponse.ok(RefreshResponse(token = token))

   296→    @Get("/heartbeat")

   298→    fun heartbeat(authentication: Authentication): HttpResponse<HeartbeatResponse> {

   300→        return HttpResponse.ok(HeartbeatResponse())

     3→import io.micronaut.http.cookie.Cookie
     4→import io.micronaut.http.cookie.SameSite
     5→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
     6→import java.time.Duration
     7→
     8→/**
     9→ * Service for creating authentication cookies.
    10→ *
    11→ * Provides consistent cookie creation for both local login (AuthController)
    12→ * and OAuth login (OAuthController) to ensure the same HttpOnly secure cookie
    13→ * is used across all authentication methods.
    14→ */
    15→@Singleton
    16→class AuthCookieService(
    17→    @io.micronaut.context.annotation.Value("\${secman.auth.cookie-secure:true}")
    18→    private val cookieSecure: Boolean
    19→) {
    20→    companion object {
    21→        const val AUTH_COOKIE_NAME = "secman_auth"
    22→        private val AUTH_COOKIE_MAX_AGE = Duration.ofHours(8)
    23→    }
    24→
    25→    /**
    26→     * Create HttpOnly secure cookie for JWT token.
    27→     * Security properties:
    28→     * - HttpOnly: Prevents JavaScript access (XSS protection)
    29→     * - Secure: Only sent over HTTPS (configurable for development)
    30→     * - SameSite=Lax: CSRF protection while allowing navigation
    31→     * - Path=/: Available for all API endpoints
    32→     *
    33→     * Note: Set SECMAN_AUTH_COOKIE_SECURE=false for local development over HTTP
    34→     */
    35→    fun createAuthCookie(token: String): Cookie {
    36→        return Cookie.of(AUTH_COOKIE_NAME, token)
    37→            .httpOnly(true)
    38→            .secure(cookieSecure)
    39→            .sameSite(SameSite.Lax)
    40→            .maxAge(AUTH_COOKIE_MAX_AGE)
    41→            .path("/")
    42→    }
    43→
    44→    /**
    45→     * Create expired cookie to clear authentication.
    46→     */
    47→    fun createLogoutCookie(): Cookie {
    48→        return Cookie.of(AUTH_COOKIE_NAME, "")
    49→            .httpOnly(true)
    50→            .secure(cookieSecure)
    51→            .sameSite(SameSite.Lax)
    52→            .maxAge(Duration.ZERO)
    53→            .path("/")
    54→    }
    55→}

1→package com.secman.security
     2→
     3→import com.secman.service.AuthCookieService
     4→import io.micronaut.core.util.StringUtils
     5→import io.micronaut.http.HttpRequest
     6→import io.micronaut.security.token.reader.TokenReader
     7→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
     8→import java.util.Optional
     9→
    10→/**
    11→ * Custom TokenReader that supports JWT authentication via HttpOnly cookie.
    12→ *
    13→ * **Security Benefits**:
    14→ * - HttpOnly cookies are not accessible via JavaScript, preventing XSS token theft
    15→ * - Secure flag ensures cookie is only sent over HTTPS
    16→ * - SameSite=Lax provides CSRF protection while allowing navigation
    17→ *
    18→ * **Use Case**:
    19→ * - Primary authentication mechanism for browser-based clients
    20→ * - Works alongside Authorization header for backward compatibility
    21→ *
    22→ * **Priority**:
    23→ * - Micronaut tries all TokenReaders, so both cookie and Authorization header work
    24→ * - Order is determined by @Order annotation if needed
    25→ *
    26→ * Feature: Security Hardening - JWT in HttpOnly Cookies
    27→ */
    28→@Singleton
    29→class CookieTokenReader : TokenReader<HttpRequest<*>> {
    30→
    31→    /**
    32→     * Read JWT token from HttpOnly cookie.
    33→     *
    34→     * This reader checks for the authentication cookie set during login.
    35→     * If not found, returns empty to allow other TokenReaders (Authorization header,
    36→     * query parameter for SSE) to be tried.
    37→     *
    38→     * @param request The HTTP request
    39→     * @return Optional containing token string from cookie, or empty if not found
    40→     */
    41→    companion object {
    42→        /** Paths where cookie-based auth must be skipped to prevent stale-cookie login loops */
    43→        private val SKIP_COOKIE_PATH_PREFIXES = listOf("/oauth/", "/api/auth/clear-session")
    44→    }
    45→
    46→    override fun findToken(request: HttpRequest<*>): Optional<String> {
    47→        // Skip cookie reading for OAuth and session-clearing paths.
    48→        // Stale/expired cookies would cause Micronaut's security pipeline to reject
    49→        // these requests before the controller (marked IS_ANONYMOUS) can execute.
    50→        val path = request.uri.path
    51→        if (SKIP_COOKIE_PATH_PREFIXES.any { path.startsWith(it) }) {
    52→            return Optional.empty()
    53→        }
    54→
    55→        val cookies = request.cookies
    56→        val authCookie = cookies.get(AuthCookieService.AUTH_COOKIE_NAME)
    57→
    58→        if (authCookie != null && StringUtils.isNotEmpty(authCookie.value)) {
    59→            return Optional.of(authCookie.value)
    60→        }
    61→
    62→        return Optional.empty()
    63→    }
    64→}
    65→

input.prompt:
Very thorough security-focused exploration of this codebase. Focus on:

1. **Broken Access Control (OWASP #1)**: 
   - Search for ALL controllers: find every @Controller class and check their @Secured annotations
   - Look for endpoints that might be missing authorization (SecurityRule.IS_ANONYMOUS on sensitive endpoints)
   - Search for IDOR vulnerabilities: endpoints that take user IDs or resource IDs as path/query parameters without verifying ownership
   - Check if there are any endpoints where role checks are done inconsistently
   - Read the AssetFilterService.kt fully to understand access control logic
   - Search for any endpoint that returns data without filtering by user context

2. **Sensitive Data Exposure**:
   - Search for password handling (how passwords are stored, hashed, compared)
   - Check if sensitive data (passwords, tokens, API keys) appears in logs (search for log statements near auth operations)
   - Search for any hardcoded secrets, API keys, or credentials in the codebase
   - Check what user data is returned in API responses (are password hashes ever returned?)
   - Search for any endpoint that exports or lists sensitive configuration

3. **Security Misconfiguration**:
   - Read application.yml fully for security-related settings
   - Check HTTP security headers configuration
   - Check if debug/development endpoints are exposed
   - Search for any /actuator, /health, /info, /memory endpoints and their security
   - Check error handling - do error responses leak stack traces or internal details?

4. **Mass Assignment**:
   - Check if DTOs properly restrict which fields can be set by users
   - Look for cases where request bodies are directly mapped to entities without field restrictions
   - Search for @Body annotations and check if the bound DTOs could allow privilege escalation (e.g., setting roles)

Read every file you find completely.

    10→@Repository
    11→interface AssetRepository : JpaRepository<Asset, Long> {
    12→
    13→    // Memory Optimization - Feature 073
    14→
    15→    /**
    16→     * Find asset by ID with workgroups eagerly loaded
    17→     * Used for detail/update operations when LAZY loading is enabled
    18→     *
    19→     * Feature: 073-memory-optimization
    20→     * Task: T006
    21→     *
    22→     * @param id The asset ID
    23→     * @return Optional containing the asset with workgroups loaded
    24→     */
    25→    @io.micronaut.data.annotation.Query("""
    26→        SELECT a FROM Asset a
    27→        LEFT JOIN FETCH a.workgroups
    28→        WHERE a.id = :id
    29→    """)
    30→    fun findByIdWithWorkgroups(id: Long): Optional<Asset>
    31→
    32→    /**
    33→     * Find all assets accessible to a user using unified access control query
    34→     * Combines all access criteria in a single database round trip:
    35→     * 1. Assets in user's workgroups
    36→     * 2. Assets manually created by user
    37→     * 3. Assets discovered via user's scan upload
    38→     * 4. Assets with cloudAccountId matching user's AWS mappings
    39→     * 5. Assets with adDomain matching user's domain mappings
    40→     * 6. Assets where owner matches the user's username
    41→     *
    42→     * Feature: 073-memory-optimization
    43→     * Task: T031
    44→     *
    45→     * @param userId The user's ID (for workgroup, creator, uploader checks)
    46→     * @param userEmail The user's email (for AWS account and domain mapping lookups)
    47→     * @param username The user's username (for owner-based access)
    48→     * @return List of distinct accessible assets, ordered by name
    49→     */
    50→    @io.micronaut.data.annotation.Query(
    51→        value = """
    52→            SELECT DISTINCT a.* FROM asset a
    53→            WHERE
    54→                a.id IN (
    55→                    SELECT aw.asset_id FROM asset_workgroups aw
    56→                    JOIN user_workgroups uw ON aw.workgroup_id = uw.workgroup_id
    57→                    WHERE uw.user_id = :userId
    58→                )
    59→                OR a.manual_creator_id = :userId
    60→                OR a.scan_uploader_id = :userId
    61→                OR a.cloud_account_id IN (
    62→                    SELECT um.aws_account_id FROM user_mapping um
    63→                    WHERE um.email = :userEmail AND um.aws_account_id IS NOT NULL
    64→                )
    65→                OR LOWER(a.ad_domain) IN (
    66→                    SELECT LOWER(um.domain) FROM user_mapping um
    67→                    WHERE um.email = :userEmail AND um.domain IS NOT NULL
    68→                )
    69→                OR a.cloud_account_id IN (
    70→                    SELECT DISTINCT um2.aws_account_id
    71→                    FROM aws_account_sharing acs
    72→                    JOIN users u_source ON u_source.id = acs.source_user_id
    73→                    JOIN user_mapping um2 ON um2.email = u_source.email AND um2.aws_account_id IS NOT NULL
    74→                    WHERE acs.target_user_id = :userId
    75→                )
    76→                OR a.owner = :username
    77→            ORDER BY a.name ASC
    78→        """,
    79→        nativeQuery = true
    80→    )
    81→    fun findAccessibleAssets(userId: Long, userEmail: String, username: String): List<Asset>
    82→
    83→    fun findByNameContainingIgnoreCase(name: String): List<Asset>
    84→
    85→    fun findByType(type: String): List<Asset>
    86→
    87→    fun findByOwner(owner: String): List<Asset>
    88→
    89→    fun findByIp(ip: String): List<Asset>
    90→
    91→    fun findByTypeAndOwner(type: String, owner: String): List<Asset>
    92→
    93→    /**

    95→     * Used for hostname lookup during vulnerability import
    96→     * Related to: Feature 003-i-want-to (Vulnerability Management System)
    97→     *
    98→     * @param name The asset name (hostname)

   100→     */

   112→
   113→    /**

content.text:
Very thorough security-focused exploration of this codebase. Focus on:

1. **Broken Access Control (OWASP #1)**: 
   - Search for ALL controllers: find every @Controller class and check their @Secured annotations
   - Look for endpoints that might be missing authorization (SecurityRule.IS_ANONYMOUS on sensitive endpoints)
   - Search for IDOR vulnerabilities: endpoints that take user IDs or resource IDs as path/query parameters without verifying ownership
   - Check if there are any endpoints where role checks are done inconsistently
   - Read the AssetFilterService.kt fully to understand access control logic
   - Search for any endpoint that returns data without filtering by user context

2. **Sensitive Data Exposure**:
   - Search for password handling (how passwords are stored, hashed, compared)
   - Check if sensitive data (passwords, tokens, API keys) appears in logs (search for log statements near auth operations)
   - Search for any hardcoded secrets, API keys, or credentials in the codebase
   - Check what user data is returned in API responses (are password hashes ever returned?)
   - Search for any endpoint that exports or lists sensitive configuration

3. **Security Misconfiguration**:
   - Read application.yml fully for security-related settings
   - Check HTTP security headers configuration
   - Check if debug/development endpoints are exposed
   - Search for any /actuator, /health, /info, /memory endpoints and their security
   - Check error handling - do error responses leak stack traces or internal details?

4. **Mass Assignment**:
   - Check if DTOs properly restrict which fields can be set by users
   - Look for cases where request bodies are directly mapped to entities without field restrictions
   - Search for @Body annotations and check if the bound DTOs could allow privilege escalation (e.g., setting roles)

Read every file you find completely.

input.prompt:
Very thorough security-focused exploration of this codebase. Focus on:

1. **XSS (Cross-Site Scripting)**:
   - Read the frontend components that display user-provided data: search for dangerouslySetInnerHTML
   - Check if user input is rendered unescaped in any template (Astro files, React components)
   - Search for innerHTML usage in any frontend file
   - Check if CSP (Content Security Policy) headers are configured
   - Look at email templates (src/backendng/src/main/resources/email-templates/) for HTML injection vectors
   - Check if API responses include user-supplied strings that could contain scripts

2. **SSRF (Server-Side Request Forgery)**:
   - Search for any HTTP client usage in the backend (HttpClient, RestTemplate, WebClient, OkHttp, fetch)
   - Check if any endpoint accepts URLs from user input and makes server-side requests
   - Look at OAuth callback handling for open redirect vulnerabilities
   - Check CrowdStrike API integration for SSRF vectors
   - Search for any URL/hostname validation

3. **Insecure Dependencies & Configuration**:
   - Read build.gradle or build.gradle.kts for dependency versions
   - Check for known vulnerable library versions
   - Look at the Dockerfile or deployment configuration for security issues
   - Check if TLS/HTTPS is enforced

4. **Frontend Security**:
   - Read src/frontend/src/utils/auth.ts and auth-init.ts fully
   - Check localStorage usage for sensitive data storage
   - Check how JWT tokens are handled client-side
   - Search for any eval() usage in frontend code
   - Check if CORS is properly configured
   - Look at how the frontend communicates with the backend (credentials, headers)

5. **MCP Security**:
   - Read any MCP-related controller or filter files
   - Check how X-MCP-User-Email header is validated (is it trusted without verification?)
   - Search for MCP authentication/authorization patterns

Read every file you find completely.

    /**

     */
    @io.micronaut.data.annotation.Query(
        value = """
        SELECT v.vulnerable_product_versions as product,
--
        nativeQuery = true
    )
    fun findMostVulnerableProductsForAssets(assetIds: Set<Long>): List<MostVulnerableProductRow>

     * Groups by severity level and counts occurrences

     * Task: T022 [US2]
     * Spec reference: spec.md FR-003, FR-004
     *

Very thorough security-focused exploration of this codebase. Focus on:

1. **XSS (Cross-Site Scripting)**:
   - Read the frontend components that display user-provided data: search for dangerouslySetInnerHTML
   - Check if user input is rendered unescaped in any template (Astro files, React components)
   - Search for innerHTML usage in any frontend file
   - Check if CSP (Content Security Policy) headers are configured
   - Look at email templates (src/backendng/src/main/resources/email-templates/) for HTML injection vectors
   - Check if API responses include user-supplied strings that could contain scripts

2. **SSRF (Server-Side Request Forgery)**:
   - Search for any HTTP client usage in the backend (HttpClient, RestTemplate, WebClient, OkHttp, fetch)
   - Check if any endpoint accepts URLs from user input and makes server-side requests
   - Look at OAuth callback handling for open redirect vulnerabilities
   - Check CrowdStrike API integration for SSRF vectors
   - Search for any URL/hostname validation

3. **Insecure Dependencies & Configuration**:
   - Read build.gradle or build.gradle.kts for dependency versions
   - Check for known vulnerable library versions
   - Look at the Dockerfile or deployment configuration for security issues
   - Check if TLS/HTTPS is enforced

4. **Frontend Security**:
   - Read src/frontend/src/utils/auth.ts and auth-init.ts fully
   - Check localStorage usage for sensitive data storage
   - Check how JWT tokens are handled client-side
   - Search for any eval() usage in frontend code
   - Check if CORS is properly configured
   - Look at how the frontend communicates with the backend (credentials, headers)

5. **MCP Security**:
   - Read any MCP-related controller or filter files
   - Check how X-MCP-User-Email header is validated (is it trusted without verification?)
   - Search for MCP authentication/authorization patterns

Read every file you find completely.

1→package com.secman.service
     2→
     3→import com.secman.config.MemoryOptimizationConfig
     4→import com.secman.domain.Asset
     5→import com.secman.domain.Scan
     6→import com.secman.domain.User
     7→import com.secman.domain.Vulnerability
     8→import com.secman.repository.AssetRepository
     9→import com.secman.repository.ScanRepository
    10→import com.secman.repository.UserMappingRepository
    11→import com.secman.repository.UserRepository
    12→import com.secman.repository.VulnerabilityRepository
    13→import io.micronaut.security.authentication.Authentication
    14→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
    15→import org.hibernate.Hibernate
    16→
    17→/**
    18→ * Centralized service for unified access control filtering
    19→ * Feature: 008-create-an-additional (Workgroup-Based Access Control)
    20→ * Feature: 013-user-mapping-upload (AWS Account Mapping)
    21→ * Feature: 073-memory-optimization (LAZY loading support)
    22→ *
    23→ * Implements filtering logic for assets, vulnerabilities, and scans based on:
    24→ * - ADMIN role: Full access to all resources
    25→ * - VULN role: Respects workgroup restrictions like regular users
    26→ * - USER role: Access to resources from their workgroups + personally created/uploaded items + AWS account mappings
    27→ *
    28→ * Related Requirements:
    29→ * - FR-013: Filter assets by workgroup membership + ownership
    30→ * - FR-014: Filter vulnerabilities by asset accessibility
    31→ * - FR-015: Filter scans by uploader workgroup membership
    32→ * - FR-016: ADMIN has universal access
    33→ * - FR-017-019: Workgroup-based filtering for regular users and VULN role
    34→ * - AWS Account Mapping: Users can access assets based on UserMapping.awsAccountId
    35→ */
    36→@Singleton
    37→open class AssetFilterService(
    38→    private val assetRepository: AssetRepository,
    39→    private val vulnerabilityRepository: VulnerabilityRepository,
    40→    private val scanRepository: ScanRepository,
    41→    private val userRepository: UserRepository,
    42→    private val userMappingRepository: UserMappingRepository,
    43→    private val memoryConfig: MemoryOptimizationConfig,
    44→    private val awsAccountSharingService: AwsAccountSharingService
    45→) {
    46→
    47→    /**
    48→     * Get assets accessible to the authenticated user
    49→     * FR-013, FR-016, FR-017: Filter by workgroup + ownership + AWS account mapping + AD domain mapping, ADMIN has full access
    50→     *
    51→     * Users can access assets if ANY of the following is true:
    52→     * 1. User is ADMIN (universal access)
    53→     * 2. Asset belongs to a workgroup the user is a member of
    54→     * 3. Asset was manually created by the user
    55→     * 4. Asset was discovered via a scan uploaded by the user
    56→     * 5. Asset's cloudAccountId matches any of the user's AWS account mappings
    57→     * 6. Asset's adDomain matches any of the user's domain mappings (case-insensitive)
    58→     * 7. Asset's owner matches the user's username
    59→     *
    60→     * Feature 073: When lazyLoadingEnabled=true, uses unified query for single DB round trip.
    61→     * Otherwise, falls back to original multi-query approach for stability.
    62→     *
    63→     * @param authentication Current user authentication
    64→     * @return List of accessible assets (deduplicated and sorted by name)
    65→     */
    66→    fun getAccessibleAssets(authentication: Authentication): List<Asset> {
    67→        // ADMIN has universal access
    68→        if (hasRole(authentication, "ADMIN")) {
    69→            return assetRepository.findAll()
    70→        }
    71→
    72→        val userId = getUserId(authentication)
    73→        val userEmail = getUserEmail(authentication)
    74→        val username = authentication.name
    75→
    76→        // Feature 073: Use unified query when memory optimization is enabled
    77→        if (memoryConfig.lazyLoadingEnabled && userEmail != null) {
    78→            return getAccessibleAssetsUnified(userId, userEmail, username)
    79→        }
    80→
    81→        // Fallback: Original multi-query approach
    82→        return getAccessibleAssetsMultiQuery(userId, userEmail, username)
    83→    }
    84→
    85→    /**
    86→     * Get accessible assets using unified single-query approach
    87→     * Feature 073: Combines all access criteria in one database round trip.
    88→     *
    89→     * @param userId The user's ID
    90→     * @param userEmail The user's email
    91→     * @return List of accessible assets (already distinct and sorted)
    92→     */
    93→    private fun getAccessibleAssetsUnified(userId: Long, userEmail: String, username: String): List<Asset> {
    94→        return assetRepository.findAccessibleAssets(userId, userEmail, username)
    95→    }
    96→
    97→    /**
    98→     * Get accessible assets using original multi-query approach
    99→     * Retained for stability and feature flag rollback.
   100→     *
   101→     * @param userId The user's ID
   102→     * @param userEmail The user's email (nullable)
   103→     * @return List of accessible assets (deduplicated and sorted)
   104→     */
   105→    private fun getAccessibleAssetsMultiQuery(userId: Long, userEmail: String?, username: String): List<Asset> {
   106→        // Regular users and VULN: filter by workgroup membership + ownership
   107→        val workgroupAssets = assetRepository.findByWorkgroupsUsersIdOrManualCreatorIdOrScanUploaderIdOrderByNameAsc(
   108→            userId = userId,
   109→            manualCreatorId = userId,
   110→            scanUploaderId = userId
   111→        )
   112→
   113→        // Get assets accessible via AWS account mapping
   114→        val awsAccountAssets = if (userEmail != null) {
   115→            val awsAccountIds = userMappingRepository.findDistinctAwsAccountIdByEmail(userEmail)
   116→            if (awsAccountIds.isNotEmpty()) {
   117→                assetRepository.findByCloudAccountIdIn(awsAccountIds)
   118→            } else {
   119→                emptyList()
   120→            }
   121→        } else {
   122→            emptyList()
   123→        }
   124→
   125→        // Get assets accessible via AD domain mapping (case-insensitive)
   126→        // OPTIMIZATION: Use database query instead of loading all assets into memory
   127→        val domainAssets = if (userEmail != null) {
   128→            val userDomains = userMappingRepository.findDistinctDomainByEmail(userEmail)
   129→            if (userDomains.isNotEmpty()) {
   130→                // Convert to lowercase for case-insensitive matching (domains already normalized in DB)
   131→                val userDomainsLowercase = userDomains.map { it.lowercase() }
   132→
   133→                // Use optimized query that filters at database level
   134→                assetRepository.findByAdDomainInIgnoreCase(userDomainsLowercase)
   135→            } else {
   136→                emptyList()
   137→            }
   138→        } else {
   139→            emptyList()
   140→        }
   141→
   142→        // Get assets accessible via AWS account sharing (shared accounts from other users)
   143→        val sharedAwsAccountAssets = if (userEmail != null) {
   144→            val sharedIds = awsAccountSharingService.getSharedAwsAccountIdsByEmail(userEmail)
   145→            if (sharedIds.isNotEmpty()) {
   146→                assetRepository.findByCloudAccountIdIn(sharedIds)
   147→            } else {
   148→                emptyList()
   149→            }
   150→        } else {
   151→            emptyList()
   152→        }
   153→
   154→        // Get assets where user is the owner
   155→        val ownerAssets = assetRepository.findByOwner(username)
   156→
   157→        // Combine and deduplicate by asset ID, then sort by name
   158→        return (workgroupAssets + awsAccountAssets + domainAssets + sharedAwsAccountAssets + ownerAssets)
   159→            .distinctBy { it.id }
   160→            .sortedBy { it.name }
   161→    }
   162→
   163→    /**
   164→     * Get vulnerabilities accessible to the authenticated user
   165→     * FR-014, FR-016, FR-018: Filter by asset accessibility, ADMIN has full access
   166→     *
   167→     * @param authentication Current user authentication
   168→     * @return List of accessible vulnerabilities
   169→     */
   170→    fun getAccessibleVulnerabilities(authentication: Authentication): List<Vulnerability> {
   171→        // ADMIN has universal access
   172→        if (hasRole(authentication, "ADMIN")) {
   173→            return vulnerabilityRepository.findAll()
   174→        }
   175→
   176→        // Regular users and VULN: filter by asset accessibility
   177→        val userId = getUserId(authentication)
   178→        return vulnerabilityRepository.findByAssetWorkgroupsUsersIdOrAssetManualCreatorIdOrAssetScanUploaderIdOrderByScanTimestampDesc(
   179→            userId = userId,
   180→            manualCreatorId = userId,
   181→            scanUploaderId = userId
   182→        )
   183→    }
   184→
   185→    /**
   186→     * Get scans accessible to the authenticated user
   187→     * FR-015, FR-016, FR-019: Filter by uploader workgroup, ADMIN has full access
   188→     *
   189→     * Note: Scan.uploadedBy is a username String, not a User FK.
   190→     * We filter by finding users in the same workgroups and matching their usernames.
   191→     *
   192→     * @param authentication Current user authentication
   193→     * @return List of accessible scans
   194→     */
   195→    fun getAccessibleScans(authentication: Authentication): List<Scan> {
   196→        // ADMIN has universal access
   197→        if (hasRole(authentication, "ADMIN")) {
   198→            return scanRepository.findAll()
   199→        }
   200→
   201→        // Regular users and VULN: Get all scans uploaded by users in same workgroups
   202→        val userId = getUserId(authentication)
   203→
   204→        // Feature 073: Use findByIdWithWorkgroups() to load workgroups with LAZY loading
   205→        val currentUser = userRepository.findByIdWithWorkgroups(userId).orElseThrow {
   206→            IllegalStateException("Current user not found: $userId")
   207→        }
   208→
   209→        // Get all workgroups the user belongs to
   210→        val userWorkgroupIds = currentUser.workgroups.mapNotNull { it.id }
   211→
   212→        if (userWorkgroupIds.isEmpty()) {
   213→            // User not in any workgroups - can only see their own scans
   214→            return scanRepository.findByUploadedByOrderByScanDateDesc(
   215→                authentication.name,
   216→                io.micronaut.data.model.Pageable.UNPAGED
   217→            ).content
   218→        }
   219→
   220→        // Find all users in the same workgroups
   221→        val usersInWorkgroups = userWorkgroupIds.flatMap { workgroupId ->
   222→            userRepository.findByWorkgroupsIdOrderByUsernameAsc(workgroupId)
   223→        }.distinctBy { it.id }
   224→
   225→        // Get usernames of all users in same workgroups
   226→        val accessibleUsernames = usersInWorkgroups.map { it.username }.toSet()
   227→
   228→        // Filter scans by these usernames
   229→        return scanRepository.findAll().filter { scan ->
   230→            accessibleUsernames.contains(scan.uploadedBy)
   231→        }.sortedByDescending { it.scanDate }
   232→    }
   233→
   234→    /**
   235→     * Check if user has access to a specific asset
   236→     * FR-020: Verify asset access before detail view
   237→     *
   238→     * @param assetId Asset ID to check
   239→     * @param authentication Current user authentication
   240→     * @return true if user can access this asset
   241→     */
   242→    fun canAccessAsset(assetId: Long, authentication: Authentication): Boolean {
   243→        // ADMIN has universal access
   244→        if (hasRole(authentication, "ADMIN")) {
   245→            return true
   246→        }
   247→
   248→        // Check if asset is in user's accessible assets
   249→        val accessibleAssets = getAccessibleAssets(authentication)
   250→        return accessibleAssets.any { it.id == assetId }
   251→    }
   252→
   253→    /**
   254→     * Get vulnerabilities for a specific asset (with access control)
   255→     * FR-021: Filter asset vulnerabilities by accessibility
   256→     *
   257→     * @param assetId Asset ID
   258→     * @param authentication Current user authentication
   259→     * @return List of vulnerabilities for the asset (empty if no access)
   260→     */
   261→    fun getAssetVulnerabilities(assetId: Long, authentication: Authentication): List<Vulnerability> {
   262→        // First check if user can access the asset
   263→        if (!canAccessAsset(assetId, authentication)) {
   264→            return emptyList()
   265→        }
   266→
   267→        // Return all vulnerabilities for this asset
   268→        return vulnerabilityRepository.findByAssetId(assetId, io.micronaut.data.model.Pageable.UNPAGED).content
   269→    }
   270→
   271→    /**
   272→     * Extract user ID from authentication
   273→     * Uses "sub" claim from JWT token
   274→     *
   275→     * @param authentication Current user authentication
   276→     * @return User ID as Long
   277→     * @throws IllegalStateException if user ID cannot be extracted
   278→     */
   279→    private fun getUserId(authentication: Authentication): Long {
   280→        return authentication.attributes["userId"]?.toString()?.toLongOrNull()
   281→            ?: throw IllegalStateException("User ID not found in authentication")
   282→    }
   283→
   284→    /**
   285→     * Extract user email from authentication
   286→     * Used for AWS account mapping lookups
   287→     *
   288→     * @param authentication Current user authentication
   289→     * @return User email as String, or null if not found
   290→     */
   291→    private fun getUserEmail(authentication: Authentication): String? {
   292→        return authentication.attributes["email"]?.toString()
   293→    }
   294→
   295→    /**
   296→     * Check if authentication has a specific role
   297→     *
   298→     * @param authentication Current user authentication
   299→     * @param role Role name to check (e.g., "ADMIN", "VULN", "USER")
   300→     * @return true if user has the role
   301→     */
   302→    private fun hasRole(authentication: Authentication, role: String): Boolean {
   303→        return authentication.roles.contains(role)
   304→    }
   305→}
   306→

    73→
    74→    @Post("/upload-xlsx")
    75→    @Consumes(MediaType.MULTIPART_FORM_DATA)
    76→    @Transactional
    77→    @Secured("ADMIN", "REQADMIN")
    78→    open fun uploadXlsx(@Part xlsxFile: CompletedFileUpload): HttpResponse<*> {
    79→        return try {
    80→            log.debug("Processing Excel file upload: {}", xlsxFile.filename)
    81→            
    82→            // Validate file
    83→            val validation = validateFile(xlsxFile)
    84→            if (validation != null) {
    85→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse(validation))
    86→            }
    87→            
    88→            // Process file
    89→            val requirements = parseExcelFile(xlsxFile)
    90→            
    91→            if (requirements.isEmpty()) {
    92→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("No valid requirements found in file"))
    93→            }
    94→            
    95→            // Save requirements
    96→            val processedCount = saveRequirements(requirements)
    97→            
    98→            log.info("Successfully processed {} requirements from Excel file", processedCount)
    99→            HttpResponse.ok(ImportResponse(
   100→                message = "File processed successfully.",
   101→                requirementsProcessed = processedCount
   102→            ))
   103→            
   104→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   105→            log.error("Error processing Excel file", e)
   106→            HttpResponse.status<ErrorResponse>(HttpStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)
   107→                .body(ErrorResponse("An internal error occurred"))
   108→        }
   109→    }
   110→    
   111→    private fun validateFile(file: CompletedFileUpload): String? {
   112→        // Check file size
   113→        if (file.size > MAX_FILE_SIZE) {
   114→            return "File size exceeds maximum limit of ${MAX_FILE_SIZE / 1024 / 1024}MB"
   115→        }
   116→        
   117→        // Check file extension
   118→        val filename = file.filename.orEmpty()
   119→        if (!filename.lowercase().endsWith(".xlsx")) {
   120→            return "Only .xlsx files are supported"
   121→        }
   122→        
   123→        // Check content type
   124→        val contentType = file.contentType.map { it.toString() }.orElse("")
   125→        if (!contentType.contains("spreadsheetml.sheet") && !contentType.contains("excel")) {
   126→            return "Invalid file format. Please upload a valid Excel file."
   127→        }
   128→        
   129→        // Check file is not empty
   130→        if (file.size == 0L) {
   131→            return "File is empty"
   132→        }
   133→        
   134→        return null
   135→    }
   136→    
   137→    private fun parseExcelFile(file: CompletedFileUpload): List<Requirement> {
   138→        val requirements = mutableListOf<Requirement>()
   139→        
   140→        file.inputStream.use { inputStream ->
   141→            val workbook = XSSFWorkbook(inputStream)
   142→            
   143→            // Get required sheet
   144→            val sheet = workbook.getSheet(REQUIRED_SHEET_NAME)
   145→                ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Required sheet '$REQUIRED_SHEET_NAME' not found")
   146→            
   147→            // Validate headers
   148→            val headerValidation = validateHeaders(sheet)
   149→            if (headerValidation != null) {
   150→                throw IllegalArgumentException(headerValidation)
   151→            }
   152→            
   153→            // Get header mapping
   154→            val headerMap = getHeaderMapping(sheet)
   155→            
   156→            // Process data rows (skip header row 0)
   157→            for (rowIndex in 1..sheet.lastRowNum) {
   158→                val row = sheet.getRow(rowIndex) ?: continue
   159→                
   160→                try {
   161→                    val requirement = parseRowToRequirement(row, headerMap)
   162→                    if (requirement != null) {
   163→                        requirements.add(requirement)
   164→                    }
   165→                } catch (e: Exception) {
   166→                    log.warn("Failed to parse row {}: {}", rowIndex + 1, e.message)
   167→                    // Continue processing other rows
   168→                }
   169→            }
   170→            
   171→            workbook.close()
   172→        }
   173→        
   174→        return requirements
   175→    }
   176→    
   177→    private fun validateHeaders(sheet: Sheet): String? {
   178→        val headerRow = sheet.getRow(0) 
   179→            ?: return "Header row not found"
   180→        
   181→        val actualHeaders = mutableListOf<String>()
   182→        for (cellIndex in 0 until headerRow.lastCellNum) {
   183→            val cell = headerRow.getCell(cellIndex)
   184→            if (cell != null) {
   185→                actualHeaders.add(getCellValueAsString(cell).trim())
   186→            }
   187→        }
   188→        
   189→        // Check if all required headers are present (case-insensitive)
   190→        val missingHeaders = REQUIRED_HEADERS.filter { required ->
   191→            actualHeaders.none { actual -> actual.equals(required, ignoreCase = true) }
   192→        }
   193→        
   194→        if (missingHeaders.isNotEmpty()) {
   195→            return "Missing required headers: ${missingHeaders.joinToString(", ")}"
   196→        }
   197→        
   198→        return null
   199→    }
   200→

     2→

     7→import com.secman.repository.AssetRepository

     9→import com.secman.repository.WorkgroupRepository
    10→import io.micronaut.security.authentication.Authentication
    11→import io.micronaut.transaction.annotation.Transactional
    12→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
    13→import org.apache.poi.ss.usermodel.CellType
    14→import org.apache.poi.ss.usermodel.Row
    15→import org.apache.poi.xssf.usermodel.XSSFWorkbook
    16→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    17→import java.io.InputStream
    18→import java.time.LocalDateTime
    19→import java.time.format.DateTimeFormatter
    20→
    21→/**
    22→ * Service for importing assets from Excel files
    23→ * Feature: 029-asset-bulk-operations (User Story 3 - Import Assets from File)
    24→ *
    25→ * Related Requirements:
    26→ * - FR-017: Accept Excel files with validation for file size, format, required fields
    27→ * - FR-018: Validate required fields (name, type, owner)
    28→ * - FR-019: Validate data formats (IP address, type values)
    29→ * - FR-020: Handle duplicate asset names by skipping (no updates)
    30→ * - FR-021: Associate imported assets with workgroups
    31→ * - FR-022: Track importing user as creator
    32→ * - FR-023: Provide import summary with counts and errors
    33→ *
    34→ * Performance Target:
    35→ * - Import 5K assets in <60 seconds with 95%+ success rate (SC-003)
    36→ */
    37→@Singleton
    38→open class AssetImportService(
    39→    private val assetRepository: AssetRepository,
    40→    private val workgroupRepository: WorkgroupRepository,
    41→    private val userRepository: UserRepository
    42→) {
    43→
    44→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(AssetImportService::class.java)
    45→
    46→    // Date formatters for parsing Excel date fields
    47→    private val dateFormatters = listOf(
    48→        DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"),
    49→        DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("yyyy-MM-dd"),
    50→        DateTimeFormatter.ISO_LOCAL_DATE_TIME,
    51→        DateTimeFormatter.ISO_LOCAL_DATE
    52→    )
    53→
    54→    /**
    55→     * Import assets from Excel file
    56→     * FR-017, FR-018, FR-019, FR-020, FR-021, FR-022, FR-023
    57→     *
    58→     * @param inputStream Excel file input stream
    59→     * @param authentication Current user authentication
    60→     * @return ImportResult with imported/skipped counts and errors
    61→     */
    62→    @Transactional
    63→    open fun importFromExcel(inputStream: InputStream, authentication: Authentication): ImportResult {
    64→        log.info("Starting asset import for user: {}", authentication.name)
    65→
    66→        val workbook = XSSFWorkbook(inputStream)
    67→        val sheet = workbook.getSheetAt(0)
    68→
    69→        // Validate headers
    70→        val headerRow = sheet.getRow(0) ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Missing header row")
    71→        val headers = validateAndMapHeaders(headerRow)
    72→
    73→        // Get importing user for creator tracking
    74→        val importingUser = userRepository.findByUsername(authentication.name).orElse(null)
    75→
    76→        // Parse all rows
    77→        val validAssets = mutableListOf<Asset>()
    78→        val errors = mutableListOf<String>()
    79→        var skippedCount = 0
    80→
    81→        for (rowNum in 1..sheet.lastRowNum) {
    82→            val row = sheet.getRow(rowNum) ?: continue
    83→
    84→            try {
    85→                val dto = parseRow(row, headers, rowNum)
    86→
    87→                // Validate required fields
    88→                if (dto.name.isBlank()) {
    89→                    errors.add("Row ${rowNum + 1}: Asset name is required")
    90→                    skippedCount++
    91→                    continue
    92→                }
    93→
    94→                if (dto.type.isBlank()) {
    95→                    errors.add("Row ${rowNum + 1}: Asset type is required")
    96→                    skippedCount++
    97→                    continue
    98→                }
    99→
   100→                if (dto.owner.isBlank()) {
   101→                    errors.add("Row ${rowNum + 1}: Asset owner is required")
   102→                    skippedCount++
   103→                    continue
   104→                }
   105→
   106→                // Check for duplicate by name (skip, don't update)
   107→                val existing = assetRepository.findByName(dto.name.trim()).orElse(null)
   108→                if (existing != null) {
   109→                    errors.add("Row ${rowNum + 1}: Duplicate asset name '${dto.name}'")
   110→                    skippedCount++
   111→                    continue
   112→                }
   113→
   114→                // Resolve workgroup names to entities
   115→                val workgroups = resolveWorkgroups(dto.workgroupNames)
   116→
   117→                // Convert DTO to entity
   118→                val asset = dto.toAsset(workgroups)
   119→
   120→                // Set creator
   121→                if (importingUser != null) {
   122→                    asset.manualCreator = importingUser
   123→                }
   124→
   125→                validAssets.add(asset)
   126→
   127→            } catch (e: Exception) {
   128→                log.warn("Error parsing row {}: {}", rowNum + 1, e.message)
   129→                errors.add("Row ${rowNum + 1}: ${e.message}")
   130→                skippedCount++
   131→            }
   132→        }
   133→
   134→        // Batch save valid assets
   135→        val savedAssets = if (validAssets.isNotEmpty()) {
   136→            assetRepository.saveAll(validAssets).toList()
   137→        } else {
   138→            emptyList()
   139→        }
   140→
   141→        val importedCount = savedAssets.size
   142→
   143→        log.info("Asset import complete: {} imported, {} skipped, {} errors", importedCount, skippedCount, errors.size)
   144→
   145→        // Limit errors to first 20 for performance
   146→        val limitedErrors = errors.take(20)
   147→
   148→        return ImportResult(
   149→            message = "Created $importedCount assets, updated 0, skipped $skippedCount",
   150→            imported = importedCount,

    91→    /**
    92→     * Response DTO for Asset entity to prevent exposing internal JPA fields.
    93→     * Security finding HI-8: Excludes ipNumeric, vulnerabilities, scanResults,
    94→     * workgroup details, manualCreator details, scanUploader details, cloudInstanceId, osVersion.
    95→     */
    96→    @Serdeable
    97→    data class AssetResponse(
    98→        val id: Long,
    99→        val name: String,
   100→        val type: String,
   101→        val ip: String?,
   102→        val owner: String,
   103→        val description: String?,
   104→        val lastSeen: String?,
   105→        val groups: String?,
   106→        val criticality: Criticality?,
   107→        val cloudAccountId: String?,
   108→        val cloudInstanceId: String?,
   109→        val adDomain: String?,
   110→        val createdAt: String?,
   111→        val workgroups: List<WorkgroupSummary>? = null
   112→    ) {
   113→        @Serdeable
   114→        data class WorkgroupSummary(
   115→            val id: Long,
   116→            val name: String
   117→        )
   118→
   119→        companion object {
   120→            fun from(asset: Asset): AssetResponse {
   121→                // Access workgroups safely - may be lazy-loaded
   122→                val workgroupSummaries = try {
   123→                    asset.workgroups.map { WorkgroupSummary(it.id!!, it.name) }
   124→                } catch (e: Exception) {
   125→                    null
   126→                }
   127→
   128→                return AssetResponse(
   129→                    id = asset.id!!,
   130→                    name = asset.name,
   131→                    type = asset.type,
   132→                    ip = asset.ip,
   133→                    owner = asset.owner,
   134→                    description = asset.description,
   135→                    lastSeen = asset.lastSeen?.toString(),
   136→                    groups = asset.groups,
   137→                    criticality = asset.criticality,
   138→                    cloudAccountId = asset.cloudAccountId,
   139→                    cloudInstanceId = asset.cloudInstanceId,
   140→                    adDomain = asset.adDomain,
   141→                    createdAt = asset.createdAt?.toString(),
   142→                    workgroups = workgroupSummaries
   143→                )
   144→            }
   145→        }
   146→    }

   148→    /**
   149→     * Get list of valid owner candidates for the asset owner select box.
   150→     * Returns "CrowdStrike Import" plus all system users sorted by username.
   151→     * Available to ADMIN and SECCHAMPION roles.
   152→     */
   153→    @Get("/owner-candidates")
   154→    @Secured("ADMIN", "SECCHAMPION")
   155→    fun getOwnerCandidates(): HttpResponse<List<OwnerCandidate>> {
   156→        val candidates = mutableListOf<OwnerCandidate>()
   157→        candidates.add(OwnerCandidate("CrowdStrike Import", "CrowdStrike Import"))
   158→        val users = userRepository.findAll().sortedBy { it.username.lowercase() }
   159→        for (user in users) {

   161→        }
   162→        return HttpResponse.ok(candidates)
