   165→    /**
   166→     * List assets accessible to the authenticated user
   167→     * Feature: 008-create-an-additional (Workgroup-Based Access Control)
   168→     *
   169→     * FR-013, FR-016, FR-017: Filter by workgroup membership + ownership
   170→     * ADMIN sees all, regular users and VULN see their workgroup assets + owned assets
   171→     */
   172→    @Get
   173→    @Transactional(readOnly = true)
   174→    open fun list(authentication: Authentication): HttpResponse<List<AssetResponse>> {
   175→        return try {
   176→            log.debug("Fetching accessible assets for user: {}", authentication.name)
   177→
   178→            // Use AssetFilterService for workgroup-based filtering
   179→            val assets = assetFilterService.getAccessibleAssets(authentication)
   180→                .sortedByDescending { it.createdAt }
   181→                .map { AssetResponse.from(it) }
   182→
   183→            log.debug("Found {} accessible assets for user {}", assets.size, authentication.name)
   184→            HttpResponse.ok(assets)
   185→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   186→            log.error("Error fetching assets for user: {}", authentication.name, e)
   187→            HttpResponse.serverError<List<AssetResponse>>()
   188→        }
   189→    }
   190→
   191→    /**
   192→     * Get asset by ID with access control
   193→     * Feature: 008-create-an-additional (Workgroup-Based Access Control)
   194→     *
   195→     * FR-020: Verify asset access before detail view
   196→     */
   197→    @Get("/{id}")
   198→    @Transactional(readOnly = true)
   199→    open fun get(id: Long, authentication: Authentication): HttpResponse<*> {
   200→        return try {
   201→            log.debug("Fetching asset with id: {} for user: {}", id, authentication.name)
   202→
   203→            val asset = assetRepository.findById(id).orElse(null)
   204→
   205→            if (asset == null) {
   206→                log.debug("Asset not found with id: {}", id)
   207→                return HttpResponse.notFound(ErrorResponse("Asset not found"))
   208→            }
   209→
   210→            // Check if user can access this asset (workgroup-based access control)
   211→            if (!assetFilterService.canAccessAsset(id, authentication)) {
   212→                log.warn("User {} denied access to asset {}", authentication.name, id)
   213→                return HttpResponse.notFound(ErrorResponse("Asset not found"))
   214→            }
   215→
   216→            log.debug("Found asset: {}", asset.name)
   217→            HttpResponse.ok(AssetResponse.from(asset))
   218→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   219→            log.error("Error fetching asset with id: {}", id, e)
   220→            HttpResponse.serverError<Any>()
   221→        }
   222→    }

   252→    /**
   253→     * Create asset with manual creator tracking
   254→     * Feature: 008-create-an-additional (Workgroup-Based Access Control)
   255→     *
   256→     * FR-023: Track manual creator for ownership-based access
   257→     */
   258→    @Post
   259→    @Transactional
   260→    open fun create(@Valid @Body request: CreateAssetRequest, authentication: Authentication): HttpResponse<*> {
   261→        return try {
   262→            log.debug("Creating asset with name: {} for user: {}", request.name, authentication.name)
   263→
   264→            // Validate required fields are not blank after trimming
   265→            val trimmedName = request.name.trim()
   266→            val trimmedType = request.type.trim()
   267→            val trimmedOwner = request.owner.trim()
   268→
   269→            if (trimmedName.isBlank()) {
   270→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("Name cannot be empty"))
   271→            }
   272→
   273→            if (trimmedType.isBlank()) {
   274→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("Type cannot be empty"))
   275→            }
   276→
   277→            if (trimmedOwner.isBlank()) {
   278→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("Owner cannot be empty"))
   279→            }
   280→
   281→            // Get user ID from authentication for manual creator tracking
   282→            val userId = authentication.attributes["userId"]?.toString()?.toLongOrNull()
   283→            val manualCreator = if (userId != null) {
   284→                userRepository.findById(userId).orElse(null)
   285→            } else {
   286→                null
   287→            }
   288→
   289→            // Create new asset with manual creator tracking
   290→            val asset = Asset(
   291→                name = trimmedName,
   292→                type = trimmedType,
   293→                ip = request.ip?.trim()?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() },
   294→                owner = trimmedOwner,
   295→                description = request.description?.trim()?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() },
   296→                criticality = request.criticality,
   297→                manualCreator = manualCreator
   298→            )
   299→            asset.adDomain = request.adDomain?.trim()?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
   300→
   301→            val savedAsset = assetRepository.save(asset)
   302→
   303→            log.info("Created asset: {} with id: {} by user: {}", savedAsset.name, savedAsset.id, authentication.name)
   304→            HttpResponse.status<AssetResponse>(HttpStatus.CREATED).body(AssetResponse.from(savedAsset))
   305→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   306→            log.error("Error creating asset", e)
   307→            HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("An internal error occurred"))
   308→        }
   309→    }
   310→
   311→    @Put("/{id}")
   312→    @Transactional
   313→    open fun update(id: Long, @Valid @Body request: UpdateAssetRequest, authentication: Authentication): HttpResponse<*> {
   314→        return try {
   315→            log.debug("Updating asset with id: {} for user: {}", id, authentication.name)
   316→
   317→            // Access control: verify user can access this asset
   318→            if (!assetFilterService.canAccessAsset(id, authentication)) {
   319→                log.warn("User {} denied update access to asset {}", authentication.name, id)
   320→                return HttpResponse.notFound(ErrorResponse("Asset not found"))
   321→            }
   322→
   323→            val asset = assetRepository.findById(id).orElse(null)
   324→                ?: return HttpResponse.notFound(ErrorResponse("Asset not found"))
   325→            
   326→            // Update fields if provided (partial update support like Java implementation)
   327→            request.name?.let { newName ->
   328→                val trimmedName = newName.trim()
   329→                if (trimmedName.isBlank()) {
   330→                    return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("Name cannot be empty"))
   331→                }
   332→                asset.name = trimmedName
   333→            }
   334→            
   335→            request.type?.let { newType ->
   336→                val trimmedType = newType.trim()
   337→                if (trimmedType.isBlank()) {
   338→                    return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("Type cannot be empty"))
   339→                }
   340→                asset.type = trimmedType
   341→            }
   342→            
   343→            request.ip?.let { newIp ->
   344→                asset.ip = newIp.trim().takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
   345→            }
   346→            
   347→            request.owner?.let { newOwner ->
   348→                val trimmedOwner = newOwner.trim()
   349→                if (trimmedOwner.isBlank()) {
   350→                    return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("Owner cannot be empty"))
   351→                }
   352→                asset.owner = trimmedOwner
   353→            }
   354→            
   355→            request.description?.let { newDescription ->
   356→                asset.description = newDescription.trim().takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
   357→            }
   358→
   359→            // Feature 039: Handle criticality update (null explicitly allowed to revert to inheritance)
   360→            if (request.criticality !== null) {
   361→                asset.criticality = request.criticality
   362→            }
   363→
   364→            // Feature 053: Handle adDomain update
   365→            request.adDomain?.let { newAdDomain ->
   366→                asset.adDomain = newAdDomain.trim().takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
   367→            }
   368→
   369→            request.workgroupIds?.let { workgroupIds ->
   370→                // Workgroup reassignment requires ADMIN role
   371→                val roles = authentication.roles
   372→                if (!roles.contains("ADMIN")) {
   373→                    return HttpResponse.status<ErrorResponse>(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN)
   374→                        .body(ErrorResponse("Workgroup reassignment requires ADMIN role"))
   375→                }
   376→
   377→                val workgroups = workgroupIds.mapNotNull { id ->
   378→                    workgroupRepository.findById(id).orElse(null)
   379→                }
   380→
   381→                if (workgroups.size != workgroupIds.size) {
   382→                    return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("One or more workgroup IDs not found"))
   383→                }
   384→
   385→                // Update workgroup assignments
   386→                asset.workgroups.clear()
   387→                asset.workgroups.addAll(workgroups)
   388→            }
   389→
   390→            val updatedAsset = assetRepository.update(asset)
   391→
   392→            log.info("Updated asset: {} with id: {}", updatedAsset.name, updatedAsset.id)
   393→            HttpResponse.ok(AssetResponse.from(updatedAsset))
   394→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   395→            log.error("Error updating asset with id: {}", id, e)
   396→            HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("An internal error occurred"))
   397→        }
   398→    }
   399→
   400→    /**
   401→     * Get cascade deletion summary for pre-flight validation
   402→     * Feature: 033-cascade-asset-deletion (User Story 4 - UI Warning Before Cascade Deletion)
   403→     *
   404→     * GET /api/assets/{id}/cascade-summary
   405→     * Auth: Any authenticated user (ADMIN recommended)
   406→     * Response: CascadeDeleteSummaryDto
   407→     *
   408→     * Related Requirements:
   409→     * - FR-012: System MUST perform a pre-flight count of related records
   410→     * - Contract: contracts/cascade-delete-api.yaml
   411→     *
   412→     * Error Responses:
   413→     * - 404: Asset not found
   414→     * - 500: Internal server error
   415→     */
   416→    @Get("/{id}/cascade-summary")
   417→    @Secured("ADMIN")
   418→    @Transactional(readOnly = true)
   419→    open fun getCascadeSummary(id: Long): HttpResponse<*> {
   420→        return try {
   421→            log.debug("Getting cascade summary for asset id: {}", id)
   422→
   423→            val summary = assetCascadeDeleteService.getCascadeSummary(id)
   424→
   425→            log.info("Cascade summary for asset {}: {} vulns, {} exceptions, {} requests, estimated {}s",
   426→                id, summary.vulnerabilitiesCount, summary.assetExceptionsCount,
   427→                summary.exceptionRequestsCount, summary.estimatedDurationSeconds)
   428→
   429→            HttpResponse.ok(summary)
   430→
   431→        } catch (e: AssetCascadeDeleteService.AssetNotFoundException) {
   432→            log.warn("Asset not found for cascade summary: {}", id)
   433→            HttpResponse.notFound(ErrorResponse(e.message ?: "Asset not found"))
   434→
   435→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   436→            log.error("Error getting cascade summary for asset: {}", id, e)
   437→            HttpResponse.serverError<ErrorResponse>()
   438→                .body(ErrorResponse("An internal error occurred"))
   439→        }
   440→    }
   441→
   442→    /**
   443→     * Delete asset with cascade deletion of all related data
   444→     * Feature: 033-cascade-asset-deletion (User Story 1 - Delete Asset with All Related Data)
   445→     *
   446→     * DELETE /api/assets/{id}
   447→     * Auth: ADMIN role required
   448→     * Response: CascadeDeletionResultDto
   449→     *
   450→     * Related Requirements:
   451→     * - FR-001: System MUST cascade delete vulnerabilities when asset is deleted
   452→     * - FR-002: System MUST cascade delete ASSET-type exceptions
   453→     * - FR-003: System MUST cascade delete vulnerability exception requests
   454→     * - FR-011: Use pessimistic row-level locking to prevent concurrent deletion
   455→     * - FR-013: Provide detailed structured error messages
   456→     * - Contract: contracts/cascade-delete-api.yaml
   457→     *
   458→     * Error Responses:
   459→     * - 403: User does not have ADMIN role
   460→     * - 404: Asset not found
   461→     * - 409: Asset is locked by another transaction (concurrent deletion)
   462→     * - 422: Deletion would exceed timeout (forceTimeout parameter can override)
   463→     * - 500: Internal server error or transaction timeout
   464→     */
   465→    @Delete("/{id}")
   466→    @Secured("ADMIN")
   467→    open fun delete(
   468→        id: Long,
   469→        @QueryValue(defaultValue = "false") forceTimeout: Boolean,
   470→        authentication: Authentication
   471→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
   472→        return try {
   473→            log.info("Cascade delete request for asset {} by user {} (forceTimeout={})",
   474→                id, authentication.name, forceTimeout)
   475→
   476→            // Check for non-cascade references before deletion (demands, risk assessments, risks)
   477→            val referencingDemands = demandRepository.findByExistingAssetId(id)
   478→            val referencingRiskAssessments = riskAssessmentRepository.findAllByInvolvedAssetId(id)
   479→            val referencingRisks = riskRepository.findByAssetId(id)
   480→
   481→            if (referencingDemands.isNotEmpty() || referencingRiskAssessments.isNotEmpty() || referencingRisks.isNotEmpty()) {
   482→                val errorMessages = mutableListOf<String>()
   483→
   484→                if (referencingDemands.isNotEmpty()) {
   485→                    errorMessages.add("${referencingDemands.size} demand(s) reference this asset")
   486→                }
   487→
   488→                if (referencingRiskAssessments.isNotEmpty()) {
   489→                    errorMessages.add("${referencingRiskAssessments.size} risk assessment(s) reference this asset")
   490→                }
   491→
   492→                if (referencingRisks.isNotEmpty()) {
   493→                    errorMessages.add("${referencingRisks.size} risk(s) reference this asset")
   494→                }
   495→
   496→                // Get asset name for error message
   497→                val asset = assetRepository.findById(id).orElse(null)
   498→                val assetName = asset?.name ?: "Asset #$id"
   499→
   500→                val detailedMessage = "Cannot delete asset '$assetName' - it is referenced by: ${errorMessages.joinToString(" and ")}. Please handle these references first."
   501→
   502→                log.warn("Asset deletion blocked for id: {} - {}", id, detailedMessage)
   503→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse(detailedMessage))
   504→            }
   505→
   506→            // Perform cascade deletion
   507→            val result = assetCascadeDeleteService.deleteAsset(
   508→                assetId = id,
   509→                username = authentication.name,
   510→                forceTimeout = forceTimeout
   511→            )
   512→
   513→            log.info("Cascade delete successful for asset {}: {} vulns, {} exceptions, {} requests deleted",
   514→                id, result.deletedVulnerabilities, result.deletedExceptions, result.deletedRequests)
   515→
   516→            HttpResponse.ok(result)
   517→
   518→        } catch (e: AssetCascadeDeleteService.AssetNotFoundException) {
   519→            log.warn("Asset not found for deletion: {}", id)
   520→            HttpResponse.notFound(ErrorResponse(e.message ?: "Asset not found"))
   521→
   522→        } catch (e: PessimisticLockException) {
   523→            log.warn("Asset {} is locked by another transaction", id)
   524→            val asset = assetRepository.findById(id).orElse(null)
   525→            val errorDto = assetCascadeDeleteService.buildLockedErrorDto(
   526→                assetId = id,
   527→                assetName = asset?.name ?: "Asset #$id",
   528→                cause = "Asset is currently being deleted by another user"
   529→            )
   530→            HttpResponse.status<DeletionErrorDto>(HttpStatus.CONFLICT).body(errorDto)
   531→
   532→        } catch (e: AssetCascadeDeleteService.TimeoutWarningException) {
   533→            log.warn("Asset {} deletion would exceed timeout: {}s", id, e.estimatedSeconds)
   534→            val errorDto = assetCascadeDeleteService.buildTimeoutWarningDto(
   535→                assetId = e.assetId,
   536→                assetName = e.assetName,
   537→                estimatedSeconds = e.estimatedSeconds
   538→            )
   539→            HttpResponse.status<DeletionErrorDto>(HttpStatus.UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY).body(errorDto)
   540→
   541→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   542→            log.error("Failed to delete asset {}", id, e)
   543→            val asset = assetRepository.findById(id).orElse(null)
   544→            val errorDto = assetCascadeDeleteService.buildInternalErrorDto(
   545→                assetId = id,
   546→                assetName = asset?.name ?: "Asset #$id",
   547→                cause = "Deletion failed due to internal error",
   548→                technicalDetails = "An internal error occurred"
   549→            )
   550→            HttpResponse.serverError<DeletionErrorDto>().body(errorDto)
   551→        }
   552→    }
   553→
   554→    /**
   555→     * Get port history for an asset
   556→     *
   557→     * GET /api/assets/{id}/ports
   558→     * Auth: Any authenticated user
   559→     * Response: PortHistoryDTO with scan history and port data
   560→     *
   561→     * Related to:
   562→     * - Feature: 002-implement-a-parsing (Nmap Scan Import)
   563→     * - Contract: specs/002-implement-a-parsing/contracts/asset-ports.yaml
   564→     * - FR-011: Display port scan history
   565→     */
   566→    @Get("/{id}/ports")
   567→    @Transactional(readOnly = true)
   568→    open fun getPortHistory(id: Long, authentication: Authentication): HttpResponse<*> {
   569→        return try {
   570→            log.debug("Fetching port history for asset id: {} for user: {}", id, authentication.name)
   571→
   572→            // Access control: verify user can access this asset
   573→            if (!assetFilterService.canAccessAsset(id, authentication)) {
   574→                log.warn("User {} denied access to port history for asset {}", authentication.name, id)
   575→                return HttpResponse.notFound(ErrorResponse("Asset not found"))
   576→            }
   577→
   578→            val asset = assetRepository.findById(id).orElse(null)
   579→                ?: return HttpResponse.notFound(ErrorResponse("Asset not found"))
   580→
   581→            // Get scan results ordered by discoveredAt DESC (newest first)
   582→            val scanResults = scanResultRepository.findByAssetIdOrderByDiscoveredAtDesc(id)
   583→
   584→            // Convert to DTOs
   585→            val scans = scanResults.map { result ->
   586→                val ports = result.ports.map { port ->
   587→                    PortDTO(
   588→                        portNumber = port.portNumber,
   589→                        protocol = port.protocol,
   590→                        state = port.state,
   591→                        service = port.service,
   592→                        version = port.version
   593→                    )
   594→                }
   595→
   596→                ScanPortsDTO(
   597→                    scanId = result.scan.id!!,
   598→                    scanDate = result.discoveredAt,
   599→                    scanType = result.scan.scanType,
   600→                    ports = ports
   601→                )
   602→            }
   603→
   604→            val response = PortHistoryDTO(
   605→                assetId = asset.id!!,
   606→                assetName = asset.name,
   607→                scans = scans
   608→            )
   609→
   610→            log.debug("Found {} scans for asset {}", scans.size, asset.name)
   611→            HttpResponse.ok(response)
   612→
   613→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   614→            log.error("Error fetching port history for asset id: {}", id, e)
   615→            HttpResponse.serverError<ErrorResponse>().body(ErrorResponse("An internal error occurred"))
   616→        }
   617→    }
   618→
   619→    /**
   620→     * Get vulnerabilities for an asset with access control
   621→     * Feature: 008-create-an-additional (Workgroup-Based Access Control)
   622→     *
   623→     * GET /api/assets/{assetId}/vulnerabilities
   624→     * Auth: Any authenticated user
   625→     * Response: List<Vulnerability>
   626→     *
   627→     * Related to:
   628→     * - Feature: 003-i-want-to (Vulnerability Management System)
   629→     * - Feature: 008-create-an-additional (Workgroup-Based Access Control)
   630→     * - FR-021: Filter asset vulnerabilities by accessibility
   631→     */
   632→    @Get("/{assetId}/vulnerabilities")
   633→    @Transactional(readOnly = true)
   634→    open fun getVulnerabilities(
   635→        assetId: Long,
   636→        authentication: Authentication
   637→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
   638→        return try {
   639→            log.debug("Fetching vulnerabilities for asset id: {} for user: {}", assetId, authentication.name)
   640→
   641→            // Use AssetFilterService for access-controlled vulnerability retrieval
   642→            val vulnerabilities = assetFilterService.getAssetVulnerabilities(assetId, authentication)
   643→
   644→            if (vulnerabilities.isEmpty()) {
   645→                // Could mean asset not found OR user doesn't have access
   646→                val asset = assetRepository.findById(assetId).orElse(null)
   647→                if (asset == null) {
   648→                    return HttpResponse.notFound(ErrorResponse("Asset not found"))
   649→                }
   650→                // Asset exists but user can't access it - return empty list
   651→                log.debug("User {} has no access to asset {}", authentication.name, assetId)
   652→            }
   653→
   654→            log.debug("Found {} vulnerabilities for asset {}", vulnerabilities.size, assetId)
   655→            HttpResponse.ok(vulnerabilities)
   656→
   657→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   658→            log.error("Error fetching vulnerabilities for asset id: {}", assetId, e)
   659→            HttpResponse.serverError<ErrorResponse>().body(ErrorResponse("An internal error occurred"))
   660→        }
   661→    }
   662→
   663→    /**
   664→     * Export assets to Excel file
   665→     * Feature: 029-asset-bulk-operations (User Story 2 - Export Assets to File)
   666→     *
   667→     * GET /api/assets/export
   668→     * Auth: Any authenticated user
   669→     * Response: Binary Excel file (.xlsx)
   670→     *
   671→     * Related Requirements:
   672→     * - FR-010: Export assets to Excel with all fields
   673→     * - FR-011: Apply workgroup-based access control (ADMIN sees all, non-ADMIN sees workgroup+owned)
   674→     * - FR-012: Format export file with clear column headers
   675→     * - FR-014: Provide user feedback during export (loading indicators handled by frontend)
   676→     * - FR-015: Display error when no assets available
   677→     *
   678→     * Error Responses:
   679→     * - 400: No assets available to export
   680→     * - 401: User not authenticated
   681→     * - 500: Export failed
   682→     */
   683→    @Get("/export")
   684→    open fun exportAssets(authentication: Authentication): HttpResponse<*> {
   685→        return try {
   686→            log.info("Asset export request from user: {}", authentication.name)
   687→
   688→            // Get assets with workgroup filtering
   689→            val dtos = assetExportService.exportAssets(authentication)
   690→
   691→            // Check if any assets are available
   692→            if (dtos.isEmpty()) {
   693→                log.warn("No assets available to export for user: {}", authentication.name)
   694→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("No assets available to export"))
   695→            }
   696→
   697→            // Write to Excel
   698→            val outputStream = assetExportService.writeToExcel(dtos)
   699→
   700→            // Generate filename with current date
   701→            val dateStr = java.time.LocalDate.now().toString()
   702→            val filename = "assets_export_$dateStr.xlsx"
   703→
   704→            log.info("Asset export successful: {} assets exported for user {}", dtos.size, authentication.name)
   705→
   706→            // Return binary Excel file with proper headers
   707→            HttpResponse.ok(outputStream.toByteArray())
   708→                .header("Content-Type", "application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet")
   709→                .header("Content-Disposition", "attachment; filename=\"$filename\"")
   710→
   711→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   712→            log.error("Asset export failed for user: {}", authentication.name, e)
   713→            HttpResponse.serverError<ErrorResponse>()
   714→                .body(ErrorResponse("An internal error occurred"))
   715→        }
   716→    }
   717→
   718→    /**
   719→     * Bulk delete all assets (ADMIN only)
   720→     * Feature: 029-asset-bulk-operations (User Story 1 - Bulk Delete Assets)
   721→     *
   722→     * DELETE /api/assets/bulk
   723→     * Auth: ADMIN role required
   724→     * Response: BulkDeleteResult
   725→     *
   726→     * Related Requirements:
   727→     * - FR-001: Delete All Assets button visible only to ADMIN users
   728→     * - FR-003: Delete all assets when ADMIN confirms
   729→     * - FR-006: Prevent non-ADMIN users from accessing bulk delete
   730→     * - FR-007: Handle cascade deletion (vulnerabilities, scan results)
   731→     * - FR-008: Execute within transaction with rollback on failure
   732→     *
   733→     * Error Responses:
   734→     * - 403: User does not have ADMIN role
   735→     * - 409: Another bulk delete operation is in progress
   736→     * - 500: Transaction failed and was rolled back
   737→     */
   738→    @Delete("/bulk")
   739→    @Secured("ADMIN")
   740→    open fun bulkDeleteAssets(authentication: Authentication): HttpResponse<*> {
   741→        return try {
   742→            log.info("Bulk delete request received from user: {}", authentication.name)
   743→
   744→            val result = assetBulkDeleteService.deleteAllAssets()
   745→
   746→            log.info("Bulk delete successful: {}", result.message)
   747→            HttpResponse.ok(result)
   748→
   749→        } catch (e: com.secman.service.AssetBulkDeleteService.ConcurrentOperationException) {
   750→            log.warn("Concurrent bulk delete attempt rejected: {}", e.message)
   751→            HttpResponse.status<ErrorResponse>(HttpStatus.CONFLICT)
   752→                .body(ErrorResponse(e.message ?: "Bulk asset deletion already in progress"))
   753→
   754→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   755→            log.error("Bulk delete failed", e)
   756→            HttpResponse.serverError<ErrorResponse>()
   757→                .body(ErrorResponse("Bulk delete failed. No assets were deleted."))
   758→        }
   759→    }
   760→}

1→package com.secman.filter
     2→
     3→import io.micronaut.http.HttpRequest
     4→import io.micronaut.http.MutableHttpResponse
     5→import io.micronaut.http.annotation.Filter
     6→import io.micronaut.http.filter.HttpServerFilter
     7→import io.micronaut.http.filter.ServerFilterChain
     8→import io.micronaut.http.filter.ServerFilterPhase
     9→import org.reactivestreams.Publisher
    10→import reactor.core.publisher.Flux
    11→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    12→
    13→/**
    14→ * Security headers filter to add comprehensive security headers to all responses.
    15→ *
    16→ * SECURITY DOCUMENTATION:
    17→ *
    18→ * Content Security Policy (CSP) Configuration:
    19→ * - 'unsafe-inline' is REQUIRED for Astro's hydration scripts and React islands
    20→ * - Full nonce-based CSP migration requires frontend changes:
    21→ *   1. Backend: Generate per-request nonce and pass to templates
    22→ *   2. Frontend: Add nonce="..." to all <script> and <style> tags
    23→ *   3. Update Astro config to support nonce injection
    24→ * - Current mitigation: strict-dynamic is NOT used because it requires nonces
    25→ *
    26→ * To migrate to nonce-based CSP (recommended for higher security):
    27→ * 1. Create NonceProvider singleton that generates cryptographic nonces
    28→ * 2. Add nonce to response attributes in this filter
    29→ * 3. Update Astro/React to read nonce from response context
    30→ * 4. Replace 'unsafe-inline' with 'nonce-{value}'
    31→ */
    32→@Filter("/**")
    33→class SecurityHeadersFilter : HttpServerFilter {
    34→
    35→    private val logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(SecurityHeadersFilter::class.java)
    36→
    37→    companion object {
    38→        // Content Security Policy - Hardened policy to prevent XSS
    39→        // SECURITY: 'unsafe-inline' is required for Astro framework compatibility
    40→        // See class documentation for nonce-based CSP migration plan
    41→        private const val CSP_POLICY = "default-src 'self'; " +
    42→            "script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' https://cdn.jsdelivr.net; " +  // Removed unsafe-eval
    43→            "style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' https://cdn.jsdelivr.net; " +
    44→            "img-src 'self' data: https:; " +
    45→            "font-src 'self' data: https://cdn.jsdelivr.net; " +
    46→            "connect-src 'self'; " +
    47→            "frame-ancestors 'none'; " +
    48→            "form-action 'self'; " +
    49→            "base-uri 'self'; " +
    50→            "object-src 'none'; " +
    51→            "upgrade-insecure-requests"
    52→
    53→        // Permissions Policy (formerly Feature Policy) - disable all risky APIs
    54→        private const val PERMISSIONS_POLICY = "geolocation=(), " +
    55→            "microphone=(), " +
    56→            "camera=(), " +
    57→            "payment=(), " +
    58→            "usb=(), " +
    59→            "magnetometer=(), " +
    60→            "gyroscope=(), " +
    61→            "accelerometer=()"
    62→
    63→        // Cross-Origin policies for additional isolation
    64→        private const val CROSS_ORIGIN_EMBEDDER_POLICY = "require-corp"
    65→        private const val CROSS_ORIGIN_OPENER_POLICY = "same-origin"
    66→        private const val CROSS_ORIGIN_RESOURCE_POLICY = "same-origin"
    67→    }
    68→    
    69→    override fun doFilter(request: HttpRequest<*>, chain: ServerFilterChain): Publisher<MutableHttpResponse<*>> {
    70→        return Flux.from(chain.proceed(request))
    71→            .map { response ->
    72→                addSecurityHeaders(response, request)
    73→                response
    74→            }
    75→    }
    76→    
    77→    private fun addSecurityHeaders(response: MutableHttpResponse<*>, request: HttpRequest<*>) {
    78→        // Prevent clickjacking attacks
    79→        response.header("X-Frame-Options", "DENY")
    80→        
    81→        // Prevent MIME type sniffing
    82→        response.header("X-Content-Type-Options", "nosniff")
    83→        
    84→        // Enable XSS protection in older browsers
    85→        response.header("X-XSS-Protection", "1; mode=block")
    86→        
    87→        // Control referrer information
    88→        response.header("Referrer-Policy", "strict-origin-when-cross-origin")
    89→        
    90→        // Content Security Policy
    91→        response.header("Content-Security-Policy", CSP_POLICY)
    92→
    93→        // Permissions Policy
    94→        response.header("Permissions-Policy", PERMISSIONS_POLICY)
    95→
    96→        // Cross-Origin Isolation headers for defense-in-depth
    97→        // These headers prevent various cross-origin attacks
    98→        // Excluded for OAuth paths: COOP/CORP interfere with cross-origin redirect chains
    99→        // (secman → Microsoft/Google → secman) in some browsers
   100→        if (!request.uri.path.startsWith("/oauth/")) {
   101→            response.header("Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy", CROSS_ORIGIN_OPENER_POLICY)
   102→            response.header("Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy", CROSS_ORIGIN_RESOURCE_POLICY)
   103→        }
   104→        
   105→        // Strict Transport Security (HSTS) - only for HTTPS
   106→        if (request.uri.scheme == "https") {
   107→            response.header("Strict-Transport-Security", "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload")
   108→        }
   109→        
   110→        // Remove server header to avoid information disclosure
   111→        response.headers.remove("Server")
   112→        response.headers.remove("X-Powered-By")
   113→        
   114→        // Add custom security header
   115→        response.header("X-Security-Policy", "enabled")
   116→        
   117→        // Cache control for sensitive data (API endpoints)
   118→        if (request.uri.path.startsWith("/api/")) {
   119→            response.header("Cache-Control", "no-store, no-cache, must-revalidate, private")
   120→            response.header("Pragma", "no-cache")
   121→            response.header("Expires", "0")
   122→        }
   123→
   124→        // SSE endpoints: disable proxy buffering and keep connection alive
   125→        // X-Accel-Buffering: no — prevents nginx from buffering SSE responses
   126→        // Connection: keep-alive — signals proxies (F5, ALB) to maintain the TCP connection
   127→        if (request.uri.path == "/api/exception-badge-updates" ||
   128→            request.uri.path == "/api/materialized-view-refresh/progress") {
   129→            response.header("X-Accel-Buffering", "no")
   130→            response.header("Connection", "keep-alive")
   131→        }
   132→
   133→        // AGGRESSIVE cache control for OAuth endpoints to prevent "state" errors
   134→        // in corporate AAD environments where cached responses cause state mismatches
   135→        if (request.uri.path.startsWith("/oauth/")) {
   136→            response.header("Cache-Control", "no-store, no-cache, must-revalidate, max-age=0")
   137→            response.header("Pragma", "no-cache")
   138→            response.header("Expires", "0")
   139→        }
   140→    }
   141→    
   142→    override fun getOrder(): Int {
   143→        // Run early in the filter chain
   144→        return ServerFilterPhase.SECURITY.order()
   145→    }
   146→}

1→package com.secman.controller
     2→
     3→import com.secman.config.AppConfig
     4→import com.secman.service.AuthCookieService
     5→import com.secman.service.OAuthService
     6→import io.micronaut.context.annotation.Value
     7→import io.micronaut.http.HttpRequest
     8→import io.micronaut.http.HttpResponse
     9→import io.micronaut.http.annotation.*
    10→import io.micronaut.scheduling.TaskExecutors
    11→import io.micronaut.scheduling.annotation.ExecuteOn
    12→import io.micronaut.security.annotation.Secured
    13→import io.micronaut.security.rules.SecurityRule
    14→import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.ObjectMapper
    15→import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable
    16→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    17→import java.net.URI
    18→import java.time.LocalDateTime
    19→import jakarta.validation.Valid
    20→
    21→@Controller("/oauth")
    22→@Secured(SecurityRule.IS_ANONYMOUS)
    23→open class OAuthController(
    24→    private val oauthService: OAuthService,
    25→    private val appConfig: AppConfig,
    26→    private val authCookieService: AuthCookieService,
    27→    private val objectMapper: ObjectMapper
    28→) {
    29→    
    30→    private val logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(OAuthController::class.java)
    31→    private val backendBaseUrl: String = appConfig.backend.baseUrl
    32→    private val frontendBaseUrl: String = appConfig.frontend.baseUrl
    33→
    34→
    35→    init {
    36→
    37→		logger.info("OAuthController initialized with backendBaseUrl: {}, frontendBaseUrl: {}", backendBaseUrl, frontendBaseUrl)
    38→        // Validate that the URLs are properly formatted
    39→        if (!frontendBaseUrl.startsWith("http://") && !frontendBaseUrl.startsWith("https://")) {
    40→            logger.error("Invalid frontendBaseUrl configuration: {}. Expected format: http://localhost:4321", frontendBaseUrl)
    41→        }
    42→        if (!backendBaseUrl.startsWith("http://") && !backendBaseUrl.startsWith("https://")) {
    43→            logger.error("Invalid backendBaseUrl configuration: {}. Expected format: http://localhost:8080", backendBaseUrl)
    44→        }
    45→    }
    46→
    47→    @Serdeable
    48→    data class ErrorResponse(
    49→        val error: String,
    50→        val timestamp: LocalDateTime = LocalDateTime.now()
    51→    )
    52→
    53→    @Serdeable
    54→    data class LoginResponse(
    55→        val id: Long,
    56→        val username: String,
    57→        val email: String,
    58→        val roles: List<String>
    59→    )
    60→
    61→    /**
    62→     * Initiate OAuth authorization flow
    63→     *
    64→     * IMPORTANT: Cache-control headers are added by SecurityHeadersFilter for all OAuth endpoints
    65→     * to prevent browser caching of OAuth redirects, which can cause "state" errors in
    66→     * corporate AAD environments where cached responses contain stale state tokens.
    67→     */
    68→    @Get("/authorize/{providerId}")
    69→    fun authorize(@PathVariable providerId: Long, request: HttpRequest<*>): HttpResponse<*> {
    70→        return try {
    71→            logger.info("=== OAuth Authorization Request START ===")
    72→            logger.info("Provider ID: {}", providerId)
    73→            logger.info("Backend Base URL (from config): {}", backendBaseUrl)
    74→            logger.info("Request URI: {}", request.uri)
    75→            logger.info("Request remote address: {}", request.remoteAddress.toString())
    76→
    77→            // Log whether a stale auth cookie is present (helps diagnose new-user failures)
    78→            val hasExistingCookie = request.cookies.get(AuthCookieService.AUTH_COOKIE_NAME) != null
    79→            if (hasExistingCookie) {
    80→                logger.info("Stale secman_auth cookie detected - will be cleared on redirect to prevent interference")
    81→            }
    82→
    83→            val authUrl = oauthService.buildAuthorizationUrl(providerId, backendBaseUrl)
    84→
    85→            if (authUrl != null) {
    86→                logger.info("Successfully built authorization URL")
    87→                logger.info("Full authorization URL: {}", authUrl)
    88→                logger.info("Redirecting to OAuth provider {} with URL: {}", providerId, authUrl)
    89→                logger.info("=== OAuth Authorization Request END ===")
    90→                // Clear any existing secman_auth cookie before redirecting to OAuth provider.
    91→                // This prevents stale cookies from a previous session (shared browser, expired session,
    92→                // or failed prior OAuth attempt) from being sent with the callback request.
    93→                // The HttpOnly cookie cannot be cleared by frontend JavaScript, so we must clear it here.
    94→                HttpResponse.redirect<Any>(URI.create(authUrl))
    95→                    .cookie(authCookieService.createLogoutCookie())
    96→            } else {
    97→                logger.error("Failed to build authorization URL for provider: {}", providerId)
    98→                HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("Failed to build authorization URL"))
    99→            }
   100→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   101→            logger.error("OAuth authorization error for provider {}: {}", providerId, e.message, e)
   102→            HttpResponse.serverError(ErrorResponse("An internal error occurred"))
   103→        }
   104→    }
   105→
   106→    /**
   107→     * Handle OAuth callback without provider ID - fallback for external OAuth providers
   108→     *
   109→     * Cache-control headers are added by SecurityHeadersFilter for all OAuth endpoints.
   110→     */
   111→    @Get("/callback")
   112→    @ExecuteOn(TaskExecutors.BLOCKING)
   113→    fun callbackWithoutProvider(
   114→        @QueryValue code: String?,
   115→        @QueryValue state: String?,
   116→        @QueryValue error: String?,
   117→        request: HttpRequest<*>
   118→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
   119→        return try {
   120→            logger.info("=== OAuth Callback Received === hasCode={}, hasState={}, hasError={}, remoteAddr={}",
   121→                !code.isNullOrBlank(), !state.isNullOrBlank(), error != null, request.remoteAddress.toString())
   122→
   123→            // Log whether the request carries a stale auth cookie (aids debugging new-user issues)
   124→            val hasExistingCookie = request.cookies.get(AuthCookieService.AUTH_COOKIE_NAME) != null
   125→            if (hasExistingCookie) {
   126→                logger.info("OAuth callback received with existing secman_auth cookie (will be overwritten on success)")
   127→            }
   128→
   129→            // Check for OAuth error
   130→            if (error != null) {
   131→                logger.error("OAuth provider returned error: {}", error)
   132→                val errorMessage = when (error) {
   133→                    "access_denied" -> "Access was denied. Please try again if you wish to log in."
   134→                    "unauthorized_client" -> "OAuth application is not properly configured. Please contact support."
   135→                    "invalid_request" -> "Invalid OAuth request. Please try logging in again."
   136→                    "unsupported_response_type" -> "OAuth configuration error. Please contact support."
   137→                    "invalid_scope" -> "Requested permissions are not available. Please contact support."
   138→                    "server_error" -> "OAuth provider encountered an error. Please try again later."
   139→                    "temporarily_unavailable" -> "OAuth service is temporarily unavailable. Please try again later."
   140→                    else -> "OAuth authentication failed. Please try again."
   141→                }
   142→                return HttpResponse.redirect<Any>(URI.create("$frontendBaseUrl/login?error=${java.net.URLEncoder.encode(errorMessage, "UTF-8")}"))
   143→            }
   144→
   145→            // Validate required parameters
   146→            if (code.isNullOrBlank() || state.isNullOrBlank()) {
   147→                logger.error("Missing required OAuth parameters: code={}, state={}",
   148→                    if (code.isNullOrBlank()) "MISSING" else "present",
   149→                    if (state.isNullOrBlank()) "MISSING" else "present(${state.take(10)}...)")
   150→                return HttpResponse.redirect<Any>(URI.create("$frontendBaseUrl/login?error=${java.net.URLEncoder.encode("Invalid OAuth callback parameters", "UTF-8")}"))

1→package com.secman.controller
     2→
     3→import com.secman.domain.User
     4→import com.secman.repository.AwsAccountSharingRepository
     5→import com.secman.repository.UserMappingRepository
     6→import com.secman.repository.UserRepository
     7→import com.secman.service.AuthCookieService
     8→import com.secman.service.InputValidationService
     9→import io.micronaut.http.HttpRequest
    10→import io.micronaut.http.HttpResponse
    11→import io.micronaut.http.annotation.*
    12→import io.micronaut.security.annotation.Secured
    13→import io.micronaut.security.authentication.Authentication
    14→import io.micronaut.security.authentication.UsernamePasswordCredentials
    15→import io.micronaut.security.rules.SecurityRule
    16→import io.micronaut.security.token.generator.TokenGenerator
    17→import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable
    18→import jakarta.inject.Inject
    19→import jakarta.transaction.Transactional
    20→import jakarta.validation.Valid
    21→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    22→import org.springframework.security.crypto.bcrypt.BCryptPasswordEncoder
    23→import java.time.Instant
    24→import java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentHashMap
    25→import java.util.*
    26→
    27→@Controller("/api/auth")
    28→open class AuthController(
    29→    private val userRepository: UserRepository,
    30→    private val userMappingRepository: UserMappingRepository,
    31→    private val awsAccountSharingRepository: AwsAccountSharingRepository,
    32→    private val tokenGenerator: TokenGenerator,
    33→    private val inputValidationService: InputValidationService,
    34→    private val authCookieService: AuthCookieService
    35→) {
    36→
    37→    private val passwordEncoder = BCryptPasswordEncoder()
    38→    private val logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(AuthController::class.java)
    39→
    40→    // Login rate limiting: track failed attempts per username
    41→    private data class LoginAttemptRecord(val count: Int, val firstAttemptAt: Instant)
    42→    private val loginAttempts = ConcurrentHashMap<String, LoginAttemptRecord>()
    43→    private val maxLoginAttempts = 5
    44→    private val lockoutDurationSeconds = 900L // 15 minutes
    45→
    46→    private fun isLoginLocked(key: String): Boolean {
    47→        val record = loginAttempts[key] ?: return false
    48→        if (record.count >= maxLoginAttempts) {
    49→            val elapsed = java.time.Duration.between(record.firstAttemptAt, Instant.now()).seconds
    50→            if (elapsed < lockoutDurationSeconds) {
    51→                return true
    52→            }
    53→            // Lockout expired, reset
    54→            loginAttempts.remove(key)
    55→        }
    56→        return false
    57→    }
    58→
    59→    private fun recordFailedLogin(key: String) {
    60→        loginAttempts.compute(key) { _, existing ->
    61→            if (existing == null) {
    62→                LoginAttemptRecord(1, Instant.now())
    63→            } else {
    64→                val elapsed = java.time.Duration.between(existing.firstAttemptAt, Instant.now()).seconds
    65→                if (elapsed >= lockoutDurationSeconds) {
    66→                    LoginAttemptRecord(1, Instant.now()) // Reset window
    67→                } else {
    68→                    LoginAttemptRecord(existing.count + 1, existing.firstAttemptAt)
    69→                }
    70→            }
    71→        }
    72→    }
    73→
    74→    private fun clearFailedLogins(key: String) {
    75→        loginAttempts.remove(key)
    76→    }
    77→
    78→    @Serdeable
    79→    data class LoginRequest(
    80→        val username: String,
    81→        val password: String
    82→    )
    83→
    84→    @Serdeable
    85→    data class LoginResponse(
    86→        val id: Long,
    87→        val username: String,
    88→        val email: String,
    89→        val roles: List<String>,
    90→        val workgroupCount: Long = 0,
    91→        val awsAccountCount: Long = 0,
    92→        val domainCount: Long = 0
    93→    )
    94→
    95→    @Serdeable
    96→    data class StatusResponse(
    97→        val id: Long,
    98→        val username: String,
    99→        val email: String,
   100→        val roles: List<String>,
   101→        val workgroupCount: Long = 0,
   102→        val awsAccountCount: Long = 0,
   103→        val domainCount: Long = 0
   104→    )
   105→
   106→    @Post("/login")
   107→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_ANONYMOUS)
   108→    @Transactional
   109→    open fun login(@Valid @Body loginRequest: LoginRequest): HttpResponse<*> {
   110→        if (loginRequest.username.isBlank() || loginRequest.password.isBlank()) {
   111→            return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf("error" to "Username and password are required"))
   112→        }
   113→        
   114→        // Validate username format
   115→        val usernameValidation = inputValidationService.validateName(loginRequest.username, "Username")
   116→        if (!usernameValidation.isValid) {
   117→            return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf("error" to usernameValidation.errorMessage))
   118→        }
   119→        
   120→        // Validate password length (basic check, don't reveal too much)
   121→        if (loginRequest.password.length > 200) {
   122→            return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf("error" to "Invalid credentials"))
   123→        }
   124→        
   125→        // Check for potential injection attempts
   126→        if (inputValidationService.containsCommandInjection(loginRequest.username) ||
   127→            inputValidationService.containsPathTraversal(loginRequest.username)) {
   128→            // Log the attempt but return generic error
   129→            return HttpResponse.unauthorized<Any>().body(mapOf("error" to "Invalid credentials"))
   130→        }
   131→
   132→        // Rate limiting: check if login is locked for this username
   133→        val rateLimitKey = loginRequest.username.lowercase()
   134→        if (isLoginLocked(rateLimitKey)) {
   135→            logger.warn("Login rate limit exceeded for user: {}", loginRequest.username)
   136→            return HttpResponse.status<Any>(io.micronaut.http.HttpStatus.TOO_MANY_REQUESTS)
   137→                .body(mapOf("error" to "Too many failed login attempts. Please try again later."))
   138→        }
   139→
   140→        val userOptional = userRepository.findByUsername(loginRequest.username)
   141→        
   142→        if (userOptional.isEmpty) {
   143→            recordFailedLogin(rateLimitKey)
   144→            return HttpResponse.unauthorized<Any>().body(mapOf("error" to "Invalid credentials"))
   145→        }
   146→
   147→        val user = userOptional.get()
   148→        
   149→        if (!passwordEncoder.matches(loginRequest.password, user.passwordHash)) {
   150→            recordFailedLogin(rateLimitKey)
   151→            return HttpResponse.unauthorized<Any>().body(mapOf("error" to "Invalid credentials"))
   152→        }
   153→
   154→        // Successful login - clear failed attempts
   155→        clearFailedLogins(rateLimitKey)
   156→
   157→        // Generate JWT token
   158→        val userDetails = mapOf(
   159→            "sub" to user.username,
   160→            "username" to user.username,
   161→            "email" to user.email,
   162→            "roles" to user.roles.map { it.name },
   163→            "iss" to "secman-backend-ng",
   164→            "userId" to user.id.toString()
   165→        )
   166→        
   167→        val tokenOptional = tokenGenerator.generateToken(userDetails)
   168→        
   169→        if (tokenOptional.isEmpty) {
   170→            return HttpResponse.serverError<Any>().body(mapOf("error" to "Failed to generate token"))
   171→        }
   172→
   173→        // Update last login timestamp
   174→        user.lastLogin = Instant.now()
   175→        userRepository.update(user)
   176→
   177→        val token = tokenOptional.get()
   178→
   179→        val directAwsCount = userMappingRepository.countDistinctAwsAccountsByEmail(user.email)
   180→        val sharedAwsCount = awsAccountSharingRepository.countByTargetUserId(user.id!!)
   181→
   182→        val response = LoginResponse(
   183→            id = user.id!!,
   184→            username = user.username,
   185→            email = user.email,
   186→            roles = user.roles.map { it.name },
   187→            workgroupCount = userRepository.countWorkgroupsByUsername(user.username),
   188→            awsAccountCount = directAwsCount + sharedAwsCount,
   189→            domainCount = userMappingRepository.countDistinctDomainsByEmail(user.email)
   190→        )
   191→
   192→        // Set JWT in HttpOnly secure cookie (primary auth mechanism)
   193→        return HttpResponse.ok(response).cookie(authCookieService.createAuthCookie(token))
   194→    }
   195→
   196→    @Post("/logout")
   197→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_AUTHENTICATED)
   198→    fun logout(): HttpResponse<*> {
   199→        // Clear the auth cookie by setting an expired cookie
   200→        return HttpResponse.ok(mapOf("message" to "Logged out successfully"))
   201→            .cookie(authCookieService.createLogoutCookie())
   202→    }

   205→     * Clear the auth session cookie without requiring authentication.

   207→     */
   208→    @Post("/clear-session")
   209→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_ANONYMOUS)
   210→    fun clearSession(): HttpResponse<*> {
   211→        return HttpResponse.ok(mapOf("message" to "Session cleared"))
   212→            .cookie(authCookieService.createLogoutCookie())

   215→    @Get("/status")
   216→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_AUTHENTICATED)
   217→    fun status(authentication: Authentication): HttpResponse<*> {
   218→        val username = authentication.name
   219→        val userOptional = userRepository.findByUsername(username)
   220→
   221→        if (userOptional.isEmpty) {
   222→            return HttpResponse.unauthorized<Any>().body(mapOf("error" to "User not found"))
   223→        }
   224→
   225→        val user = userOptional.get()
   226→        val directAwsCount = userMappingRepository.countDistinctAwsAccountsByEmail(user.email)
   227→        val sharedAwsCount = awsAccountSharingRepository.countByTargetUserId(user.id!!)
   228→
   229→        val response = StatusResponse(
   230→            id = user.id!!,
   231→            username = user.username,
   232→            email = user.email,
   233→            roles = user.roles.map { it.name },
   234→            workgroupCount = userRepository.countWorkgroupsByUsername(user.username),
   235→            awsAccountCount = directAwsCount + sharedAwsCount,
   236→            domainCount = userMappingRepository.countDistinctDomainsByEmail(user.email)
   237→        )
   238→
   239→        return HttpResponse.ok(response)
   240→    }
   241→
   242→    @Serdeable
   243→    data class RefreshResponse(
   244→        val token: String,
   245→        val expiresIn: Int = 28800  // 8 hours in seconds
   246→    )
   247→
   248→    @Serdeable
   249→    data class HeartbeatResponse(
   250→        val status: String = "ok",
   251→        val timestamp: Long = System.currentTimeMillis()
   252→    )
   253→
   254→    /**
   255→     * Refresh the current JWT token.
   256→     * Issues a new token with extended expiration if the current token is still valid.
   257→     */
   258→    @Post("/refresh")
   259→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_AUTHENTICATED)
   260→    fun refresh(authentication: Authentication): HttpResponse<*> {
   261→        val username = authentication.name
   262→        val userOptional = userRepository.findByUsername(username)
   263→
   264→        if (userOptional.isEmpty) {
   265→            return HttpResponse.unauthorized<Any>().body(mapOf("error" to "User not found"))
   266→        }
   267→
   268→        val user = userOptional.get()
   269→
   270→        // Generate a fresh JWT token
   271→        val userDetails = mapOf(
   272→            "sub" to user.username,
   273→            "username" to user.username,
   274→            "email" to user.email,
   275→            "roles" to user.roles.map { it.name },
   276→            "iss" to "secman-backend-ng",
   277→            "userId" to user.id.toString()
   278→        )
   279→
   280→        val tokenOptional = tokenGenerator.generateToken(userDetails)
   281→
   282→        if (tokenOptional.isEmpty) {
   283→            return HttpResponse.serverError<Any>().body(mapOf("error" to "Failed to generate token"))
   284→        }
   285→
   286→        val token = tokenOptional.get()
   287→        // Update both the cookie and return the token in response
   288→        return HttpResponse.ok(RefreshResponse(token = token))
   289→            .cookie(authCookieService.createAuthCookie(token))
   290→    }
   291→
   292→    /**
   293→     * Lightweight heartbeat endpoint for session keep-alive.
   294→     * Validates the token is still valid without generating a new one.
   295→     */
   296→    @Get("/heartbeat")
   297→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_AUTHENTICATED)
   298→    fun heartbeat(authentication: Authentication): HttpResponse<HeartbeatResponse> {
   299→        // If we reach here, the token is valid
   300→        return HttpResponse.ok(HeartbeatResponse())
   301→    }
   302→}

    32→    private val passwordEncoder = BCryptPasswordEncoder()

    47→        @field:Email @field:Size(max = 255) val email: String? = null,
    48→        @field:Size(min = 8, max = 200) val password: String? = null,

    54→    @Serdeable

    60→        val mfaEnabled: Boolean,
    61→        val createdAt: String?,

    63→        val lastLogin: String?,
    64→        val workgroups: List<WorkgroupSummary>? = null,
    65→        val workgroupCount: Int? = null
    66→    ) {
    67→        companion object {
    68→            fun from(user: User, includeWorkgroups: Boolean = false): UserResponse {
