    87→    @Serdeable
    88→    data class WorkgroupSummary(
    89→        val id: Long,
    90→        val name: String
    91→    )
    92→
    93→    /**
    94→     * List all users with optional workgroup information
    95→     * Feature: 008-create-an-additional (Workgroup-Based Access Control)
    96→     *
    97→     * GET /api/users?includeWorkgroups=true
    98→     * FR-010: Display user workgroup membership in admin views
    99→     */
   100→    @Get
   101→    fun list(@QueryValue(defaultValue = "false") includeWorkgroups: Boolean): HttpResponse<List<UserResponse>> {
   102→        val users = if (includeWorkgroups) {
   103→            userRepository.findAllWithWorkgroups().map { UserResponse.from(it, true) }
   104→        } else {
   105→            userRepository.findAll().map { UserResponse.from(it, false) }
   106→        }
   107→        return HttpResponse.ok(users)
   108→    }
   109→
   110→    @Post
   111→    @Transactional
   112→    open fun create(@Valid @Body request: CreateUserRequest, authentication: io.micronaut.security.authentication.Authentication): HttpResponse<*> {
   113→        // Validation
   114→        if (request.username.isBlank() || request.email.isBlank() || request.password.isBlank()) {
   115→            return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf("error" to "Username, email, and password are required"))
   116→        }
   117→
   118→        // Check for duplicates
   119→        if (userRepository.existsByUsername(request.username)) {
   120→            return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf("error" to "Username already exists"))
   121→        }
   122→
   123→        if (userRepository.existsByEmail(request.email)) {
   124→            return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf("error" to "Email already exists"))
   125→        }
   126→
   127→        try {
   128→            // Parse roles
   129→            val roles = mutableSetOf<User.Role>()
   130→            request.roles?.forEach { roleString ->
   131→                try {
   132→                    roles.add(User.Role.valueOf(roleString.uppercase()))
   133→                } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
   134→                    return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf("error" to "Invalid role: $roleString"))
   135→                }
   136→            }
   137→            
   138→            // Default to USER, VULN, REQ roles if none provided
   139→            if (roles.isEmpty()) {
   140→                roles.addAll(setOf(User.Role.USER, User.Role.VULN, User.Role.REQ))
   141→            }
   142→
   143→            val user = User(
   144→                username = request.username,
   145→                email = request.email,
   146→                passwordHash = passwordEncoder.encode(request.password)!!,
   147→                roles = roles,
   148→                mfaEnabled = request.mfaEnabled ?: false
   149→            )
   150→
   151→            val savedUser = userRepository.save(user)
   152→
   153→            // Assign workgroups if provided
   154→            request.workgroupIds?.let { workgroupIds ->
   155→                val workgroups = workgroupIds.mapNotNull { id ->
   156→                    workgroupRepository.findById(id).orElse(null)
   157→                }
   158→
   159→                if (workgroups.size != workgroupIds.size) {
   160→                    return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf("error" to "One or more workgroup IDs not found"))
   161→                }
   162→
   163→                savedUser.workgroups.clear()
   164→                savedUser.workgroups.addAll(workgroups)
   165→                userRepository.update(savedUser)
   166→            }
   167→
   168→            // Send email notification to ADMIN users (async, non-blocking)
   169→            // Feature: 027-admin-user-notifications
   170→            try {
   171→                val createdByUsername = authentication.name
   172→                adminNotificationService.sendNewUserNotificationForManualCreation(savedUser, createdByUsername)
   173→            } catch (e: Exception) {
   174→                // Log but don't fail user creation if notification fails
   175→                // Notification failures are already logged in AdminNotificationService
   176→            }
   177→
   178→            return HttpResponse.ok(UserResponse.from(savedUser, includeWorkgroups = true))
   179→            
   180→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   181→            return HttpResponse.serverError<Any>().body(mapOf("error" to "An internal error occurred"))
   182→        }
   183→    }
   184→
   185→    /**
   186→     * Get user by ID with workgroup information
   187→     * Feature: 008-create-an-additional (Workgroup-Based Access Control)
   188→     *
   189→     * GET /api/users/{id}
   190→     * FR-010: Display user workgroup membership in detail views
   191→     */
   192→    @Get("/{id}")
   193→    fun get(@PathVariable id: Long): HttpResponse<*> {
   194→        val userOptional = userRepository.findByIdWithWorkgroups(id)
   195→
   196→        if (userOptional.isEmpty) {
   197→            return HttpResponse.notFound<Any>().body(mapOf("error" to "User not found"))
   198→        }
   199→
   200→        // Always include workgroups in detail view
   201→        return HttpResponse.ok(UserResponse.from(userOptional.get(), includeWorkgroups = true))
   202→    }
   203→
   204→    @Put("/{id}")
   205→    @Transactional
   206→    open fun update(@PathVariable id: Long, @Valid @Body request: UpdateUserRequest): HttpResponse<*> {
   207→        val userOptional = userRepository.findById(id)
   208→        
   209→        if (userOptional.isEmpty) {
   210→            return HttpResponse.notFound<Any>().body(mapOf("error" to "User not found"))
   211→        }
   212→
   213→        val user = userOptional.get()
   214→
   215→        try {
   216→            // Update fields if provided
   217→            request.username?.let { 
   218→                if (it.isNotBlank() && it != user.username) {
   219→                    if (userRepository.existsByUsername(it)) {
   220→                        return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf("error" to "Username already exists"))
   221→                    }
   222→                    user.username = it
   223→                }
   224→            }
   225→
   226→            request.email?.let { 
   227→                if (it.isNotBlank() && it != user.email) {
   228→                    if (userRepository.existsByEmail(it)) {
   229→                        return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf("error" to "Email already exists"))
   230→                    }
   231→                    user.email = it
   232→                }
   233→            }
   234→
   235→            request.password?.let {
   236→                if (it.isNotBlank()) {
   237→                    user.passwordHash = passwordEncoder.encode(it)!!
   238→                }
   239→            }
   240→
   241→            request.mfaEnabled?.let {
   242→                user.mfaEnabled = it
   243→            }
   244→
   245→            request.roles?.let { roleStrings ->
   246→                val roles = mutableSetOf<User.Role>()
   247→                roleStrings.forEach { roleString ->
   248→                    try {
   249→                        roles.add(User.Role.valueOf(roleString.uppercase()))
   250→                    } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
   251→                        return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf("error" to "Invalid role: $roleString"))
   252→                    }
   253→                }
   254→
   255→                // Feature 037: Validate admin role removal
   256→                val roleValidation = userDeletionValidator.validateAdminRoleRemoval(id, roles)
   257→                if (!roleValidation.canDelete) {
   258→                    val response = mapOf(
   259→                        "error" to "Cannot update user",
   260→                        "message" to roleValidation.message,
   261→                        "blockingReferences" to roleValidation.blockingReferences.map { ref ->
   262→                            mapOf(
   263→                                "entityType" to ref.entityType,
   264→                                "count" to ref.count,
   265→                                "role" to ref.role,
   266→                                "details" to ref.details
   267→                            )
   268→                        }
   269→                    )
   270→                    return HttpResponse.status<Any>(HttpStatus.CONFLICT).body(response)
   271→                }
   272→
   273→                // Properly manage the collection instead of replacing it
   274→                user.roles.clear()
   275→                user.roles.addAll(roles)
   276→            }
   277→
   278→            request.workgroupIds?.let { workgroupIds ->
   279→                val workgroups = workgroupIds.mapNotNull { id ->
   280→                    workgroupRepository.findById(id).orElse(null)
   281→                }
   282→
   283→                if (workgroups.size != workgroupIds.size) {
   284→                    return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf("error" to "One or more workgroup IDs not found"))
   285→                }
   286→
   287→                // Update workgroup assignments
   288→                user.workgroups.clear()
   289→                user.workgroups.addAll(workgroups)
   290→            }
   291→
   292→            val savedUser = userRepository.update(user)
   293→            return HttpResponse.ok(UserResponse.from(savedUser, includeWorkgroups = true))
   294→            
   295→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   296→            return HttpResponse.serverError<Any>().body(mapOf("error" to "An internal error occurred"))
   297→        }
   298→    }
   299→
   300→    @Delete("/{id}")
   301→    open fun delete(@PathVariable id: Long): HttpResponse<*> {
   302→        val userOptional = userRepository.findById(id)
   303→
   304→        if (userOptional.isEmpty) {
   305→            return HttpResponse.notFound<Any>().body(mapOf("error" to "User not found"))
   306→        }
   307→
   308→        // Validate user deletion
   309→        val validationResult = userDeletionValidator.validateUserDeletion(id)
   310→
   311→        if (!validationResult.canDelete) {
   312→            // Return detailed error with blocking references
   313→            val response = mapOf(
   314→                "error" to "Cannot delete user",
   315→                "message" to validationResult.message,
   316→                "blockingReferences" to validationResult.blockingReferences.map { ref ->
   317→                    mapOf(
   318→                        "entityType" to ref.entityType,
   319→                        "count" to ref.count,
   320→                        "role" to ref.role,
   321→                        "details" to ref.details
   322→                    )
   323→                }
   324→            )
   325→            // Feature 037: Return 409 Conflict for last admin protection (SystemConstraint)
   326→            // Return 400 Bad Request for other blocking references (demands, risk assessments, etc.)
   327→            val isLastAdminProtection = validationResult.blockingReferences.any { it.entityType == "SystemConstraint" }
   328→            return if (isLastAdminProtection) {
   329→                HttpResponse.status<Any>(HttpStatus.CONFLICT).body(response)
   330→            } else {
   331→                HttpResponse.badRequest<Any>().body(response)
   332→            }
   333→        }
   334→
   335→        try {
   336→            userService.deleteUser(id)
   337→            return HttpResponse.ok(mapOf("message" to "User deleted successfully"))
   338→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   339→            return HttpResponse.serverError<Any>().body(mapOf("error" to "An internal error occurred"))
   340→        }
   341→    }
   342→
   343→    // User Mapping Management Endpoints (Feature 017)
   344→    
   345→    /**
   346→     * Get all mappings for a user
   347→     * Feature: 017-user-mapping-management
   348→     * User Story 1: View User's Existing Mappings (P1)
   349→     */
   350→    @Get("/{userId}/mappings")
   351→    fun getUserMappings(@PathVariable userId: Long): List<UserMappingResponse> {
   352→        return userMappingService.getUserMappings(userId)
   353→    }
   354→    
   355→    /**
   356→     * Create a new mapping for a user
   357→     * Feature: 017-user-mapping-management
   358→     * User Story 2: Add New Mapping (P2)
   359→     */
   360→    @Post("/{userId}/mappings")
   361→    @Status(HttpStatus.CREATED)
   362→    open fun createUserMapping(
   363→        @PathVariable userId: Long,
   364→        @Valid @Body request: CreateUserMappingRequest
   365→    ): UserMappingResponse {
   366→        return userMappingService.createMapping(userId, request)
   367→    }
   368→    
   369→    /**
   370→     * Update an existing mapping
   371→     * Feature: 017-user-mapping-management
   372→     * User Story 4: Edit Existing Mapping (P3)
   373→     */
   374→    @Put("/{userId}/mappings/{mappingId}")
   375→    open fun updateUserMapping(
   376→        @PathVariable userId: Long,
   377→        @PathVariable mappingId: Long,
   378→        @Valid @Body request: UpdateUserMappingRequest
   379→    ): UserMappingResponse {
   380→        return userMappingService.updateMapping(userId, mappingId, request)
   381→    }
   382→    
   383→    /**
   384→     * Delete a mapping
   385→     * Feature: 017-user-mapping-management
   386→     * User Story 3: Delete Existing Mapping (P2)
   387→     */
   388→    @Delete("/{userId}/mappings/{mappingId}")
   389→    @Status(HttpStatus.NO_CONTENT)
   390→    fun deleteUserMapping(
   391→        @PathVariable userId: Long,
   392→        @PathVariable mappingId: Long
   393→    ) {
   394→        userMappingService.deleteMapping(userId, mappingId)
   395→    }
   396→}

1→@file:Suppress("DEPRECATION")
     2→
     3→package com.secman.security
     4→
     5→import com.secman.repository.UserRepository
     6→import io.micronaut.http.HttpRequest
     7→import io.micronaut.security.authentication.AuthenticationProvider
     8→import io.micronaut.security.authentication.AuthenticationRequest
     9→import io.micronaut.security.authentication.AuthenticationResponse
    10→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
    11→import org.reactivestreams.Publisher
    12→import org.springframework.security.crypto.bcrypt.BCryptPasswordEncoder
    13→import reactor.core.publisher.Flux
    14→import reactor.core.publisher.FluxSink
    15→
    16→@Singleton
    17→@Suppress("DEPRECATION")
    18→class AuthenticationProviderUserPassword(
    19→    private val userRepository: UserRepository
    20→) : AuthenticationProvider<HttpRequest<*>> {
    21→
    22→    private val passwordEncoder = BCryptPasswordEncoder()
    23→
    24→    override fun authenticate(
    25→        httpRequest: HttpRequest<*>?,
    26→        authenticationRequest: AuthenticationRequest<*, *>
    27→    ): Publisher<AuthenticationResponse> {
    28→        return Flux.create { emitter: FluxSink<AuthenticationResponse> ->
    29→            val username = authenticationRequest.identity.toString()
    30→            val password = authenticationRequest.secret.toString()
    31→
    32→            try {
    33→                val userOptional = userRepository.findByUsername(username)
    34→                
    35→                if (userOptional.isPresent) {
    36→                    val user = userOptional.get()
    37→                    
    38→                    if (passwordEncoder.matches(password, user.passwordHash)) {
    39→                        // Extract roles as strings
    40→                        val roles = user.roles.map { it.name }
    41→                        
    42→                        emitter.next(AuthenticationResponse.success(
    43→                            username,
    44→                            roles,
    45→                            mapOf(
    46→                                "userId" to user.id.toString(),
    47→                                "email" to user.email
    48→                            )
    49→                        ))
    50→                    } else {
    51→                        emitter.error(AuthenticationResponse.exception("Invalid credentials"))
    52→                    }
    53→                } else {
    54→                    // Use same error message as invalid password to prevent user enumeration
    55→                    emitter.error(AuthenticationResponse.exception("Invalid credentials"))
    56→                }
    57→            } catch (e: Exception) {
    58→                emitter.error(AuthenticationResponse.exception("Authentication error: ${e.message}"))
    59→            }
    60→            
    61→            emitter.complete()
    62→        }
    63→    }
    64→}

1→package com.secman.config
     2→
     3→import com.secman.domain.User
     4→import com.secman.repository.UserRepository
     5→import io.micronaut.context.event.ApplicationEventListener
     6→import io.micronaut.runtime.event.ApplicationStartupEvent
     7→import io.micronaut.context.annotation.Requires
     8→import io.micronaut.transaction.annotation.Transactional
     9→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
    10→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    11→import org.springframework.security.crypto.bcrypt.BCryptPasswordEncoder
    12→
    13→/**
    14→ * Creates a default admin user on first application startup if no users exist.
    15→ *
    16→ * Default credentials:
    17→ *   Username: admin
    18→ *   Email:    admin@localhost
    19→ *   Password: password
    20→ *   Roles:    ADMIN, USER
    21→ *
    22→ * IMPORTANT: Change the default password immediately after first login in production.
    23→ */
    24→@Requires(notEnv = ["cli"])
    25→@Singleton
    26→open class DefaultAdminBootstrapper(
    27→    private val userRepository: UserRepository
    28→) : ApplicationEventListener<ApplicationStartupEvent> {
    29→
    30→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(DefaultAdminBootstrapper::class.java)
    31→    private val passwordEncoder = BCryptPasswordEncoder()
    32→
    33→    companion object {
    34→        const val DEFAULT_ADMIN_USERNAME = "admin"
    35→        const val DEFAULT_ADMIN_EMAIL = "admin@localhost"
    36→        const val DEFAULT_ADMIN_PASSWORD = "password"
    37→    }
    38→
    39→    override fun onApplicationEvent(event: ApplicationStartupEvent) {
    40→        bootstrapDefaultAdmin()
    41→    }
    42→
    43→    @Transactional
    44→    open fun bootstrapDefaultAdmin() {
    45→        try {
    46→            val userCount = userRepository.count()
    47→            if (userCount > 0) {
    48→                log.debug("Users already exist (count={}), skipping default admin creation", userCount)
    49→                return
    50→            }
    51→
    52→            val admin = User(
    53→                username = DEFAULT_ADMIN_USERNAME,
    54→                email = DEFAULT_ADMIN_EMAIL,
    55→                passwordHash = passwordEncoder.encode(DEFAULT_ADMIN_PASSWORD)!!,
    56→                roles = mutableSetOf(User.Role.ADMIN, User.Role.USER),
    57→                authSource = User.AuthSource.LOCAL
    58→            )
    59→
    60→            userRepository.save(admin)
    61→            log.warn("==========================================================")
    62→            log.warn("  DEFAULT ADMIN USER CREATED")
    63→            log.warn("  Username: {}", DEFAULT_ADMIN_USERNAME)
    64→            log.warn("  Password: {} (CHANGE IMMEDIATELY!)", DEFAULT_ADMIN_PASSWORD)
    65→            log.warn("==========================================================")
    66→        } catch (e: Exception) {
    67→            log.error("Failed to bootstrap default admin user: {}", e.message, e)
    68→        }
    69→    }
    70→}
    71→

1→package com.secman.controller
     2→
     3→import com.secman.service.*
     4→import com.secman.service.mcp.McpAccessControlService
     5→import com.secman.mcp.McpToolRegistry
     6→import com.secman.domain.*
     7→import com.secman.dto.mcp.*
     8→import io.micronaut.http.HttpResponse
     9→import io.micronaut.http.HttpStatus
    10→import io.micronaut.http.annotation.*
    11→import io.micronaut.security.annotation.Secured
    12→import io.micronaut.security.rules.SecurityRule
    13→import jakarta.inject.Inject
    14→import jakarta.validation.Valid
    15→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    16→
    17→/**
    18→ * Delegation context passed through the request when X-MCP-User-Email header is present.
    19→ * Feature: 050-mcp-user-delegation
    20→ */
    21→data class DelegationContext(
    22→    val delegatedUserEmail: String,
    23→    val delegatedUserId: Long,
    24→    val effectivePermissions: Set<McpPermission>
    25→)
    26→
    27→/**
    28→ * Main MCP protocol controller handling core MCP operations.
    29→ *
    30→ * Implements the Model Context Protocol for AI assistant integration.
    31→ */
    32→@Controller("/api/mcp")
    33→open class McpController(
    34→    @Inject private val authService: McpAuthenticationService,
    35→    @Inject private val sessionService: McpSessionService,
    36→    @Inject private val auditService: McpAuditService,
    37→    @Inject private val toolPermissionService: McpToolPermissionService,
    38→    @Inject private val toolRegistry: McpToolRegistry,
    39→    @Inject private val delegationService: McpDelegationService,
    40→    @Inject private val accessControlService: McpAccessControlService
    41→) {
    42→    private val logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(McpController::class.java)
    43→
    44→    companion object {
    45→        /** Header name for user delegation email. Feature: 050-mcp-user-delegation */
    46→        const val DELEGATION_HEADER = "X-MCP-User-Email"
    47→    }
    48→
    49→    /**
    50→     * Get MCP server capabilities.
    51→     * Returns available tools based on effective permissions (user roles ∩ API key permissions).
    52→     *
    53→     * SECURITY: User delegation via X-MCP-User-Email header is mandatory.
    54→     * Requests without this header are rejected with DELEGATION_HEADER_REQUIRED.
    55→     */
    56→    @Get("/capabilities")
    57→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_ANONYMOUS)
    58→    suspend fun getCapabilities(
    59→        @Header("X-MCP-API-Key") apiKey: String?,
    60→        @Header(DELEGATION_HEADER) delegatedUserEmail: String?
    61→    ): HttpResponse<McpCapabilitiesResponse> {
    62→        return try {
    63→            if (apiKey == null) {
    64→                return HttpResponse.status<McpCapabilitiesResponse>(HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED)
    65→                    .body(McpCapabilitiesResponse(error = McpErrorResponse("AUTH_REQUIRED", "API key required")))
    66→            }
    67→
    68→            val authResult = authService.authenticateApiKey(apiKey)
    69→            if (!authResult.success) {
    70→                return HttpResponse.status<McpCapabilitiesResponse>(HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED)
    71→                    .body(McpCapabilitiesResponse(error = McpErrorResponse("AUTH_FAILED", authResult.errorMessage ?: "Authentication failed")))
    72→            }
    73→
    74→            val mcpApiKey = authResult.apiKey!!
    75→
    76→            // SECURITY: User delegation is mandatory
    77→            if (delegatedUserEmail.isNullOrBlank()) {
    78→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(
    79→                    McpCapabilitiesResponse(error = McpErrorResponse(
    80→                        DelegationErrorCodes.DELEGATION_HEADER_REQUIRED,
    81→                        "X-MCP-User-Email header is required for all data-accessing endpoints"
    82→                    ))
    83→                )
    84→            }
    85→
    86→            if (!mcpApiKey.delegationEnabled) {
    87→                return HttpResponse.status<McpCapabilitiesResponse>(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN)
    88→                    .body(McpCapabilitiesResponse(error = McpErrorResponse(
    89→                        DelegationErrorCodes.DELEGATION_NOT_ENABLED,
    90→                        "User delegation is not enabled for this API key. Enable delegation in API key settings."
    91→                    )))
    92→            }
    93→
    94→            val validationResult = delegationService.validateDelegation(mcpApiKey, delegatedUserEmail)
    95→            if (!validationResult.success) {
    96→                return HttpResponse.status<McpCapabilitiesResponse>(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN)
    97→                    .body(McpCapabilitiesResponse(error = McpErrorResponse(
    98→                        validationResult.errorCode ?: DelegationErrorCodes.DELEGATION_FAILED,
    99→                        validationResult.errorMessage ?: "Delegation validation failed"
   100→                    )))
   101→            }
   102→
   103→            val effectivePermissions = validationResult.effectivePermissions
   104→            val toolCapabilities = toolRegistry.getToolCapabilities(effectivePermissions)
   105→
   106→            val serverInfo = mutableMapOf<String, Any>(
   107→                "name" to "Secman MCP Server",
   108→                "version" to "1.0.0",
   109→                "protocol" to "mcp/1.0",
   110→                "delegationActive" to true,
   111→                "delegatedUser" to delegatedUserEmail
   112→            )
   113→
   114→            val response = McpCapabilitiesResponse(
   115→                capabilities = mapOf(
   116→                    "tools" to (toolCapabilities["tools"] as? List<*> ?: emptyList<Any>()),
   117→                    "resources" to emptyMap<String, Any>(),
   118→                    "prompts" to emptyMap<String, Any>()
   119→                ),
   120→                serverInfo = serverInfo
   121→            )
   122→
   123→            HttpResponse.ok(response)
   124→
   125→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   126→            logger.error("Capabilities request failed", e)
   127→            HttpResponse.status<McpCapabilitiesResponse>(HttpStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)
   128→                .body(McpCapabilitiesResponse(error = McpErrorResponse("SYSTEM_ERROR", "Internal server error")))
   129→        }
   130→    }
   131→
   132→    /**
   133→     * Create a new MCP session.
   134→     */
   135→    @Post("/session")
   136→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_ANONYMOUS)
   137→    open suspend fun createSession(
   138→        @Header("X-MCP-API-Key") apiKey: String?,
   139→        @Valid @Body request: McpSessionCreateRequest
   140→    ): HttpResponse<McpSessionResponse> {
   141→        return try {
   142→            if (apiKey == null) {
   143→                return HttpResponse.status<McpSessionResponse>(HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED)
   144→                    .body(McpSessionResponse(error = McpErrorResponse("AUTH_REQUIRED", "API key required")))
   145→            }
   146→
   147→            val authResult = authService.authenticateApiKey(apiKey)
   148→            if (!authResult.success) {
   149→                return HttpResponse.status<McpSessionResponse>(HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED)
   150→                    .body(McpSessionResponse(error = McpErrorResponse("AUTH_FAILED", authResult.errorMessage ?: "Authentication failed")))
   151→            }
   152→
   153→            val mcpApiKey = authResult.apiKey!!
   154→
   155→            // Validate request
   156→            val validationError = validateSessionRequest(request)
   157→            if (validationError != null) {
   158→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(
   159→                    McpSessionResponse(error = McpErrorResponse("INVALID_REQUEST", validationError))
   160→                )
   161→            }
   162→
   163→            val sessionResult = sessionService.createSession(
   164→                apiKeyId = mcpApiKey.id,
   165→                userId = mcpApiKey.userId,
   166→                clientInfo = request.clientInfo.toJsonString(),
   167→                capabilities = request.capabilities.toJsonString(),
   168→                connectionType = McpConnectionType.HTTP
   169→            )
   170→
   171→            if (!sessionResult.success) {
   172→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(
   173→                    McpSessionResponse(error = McpErrorResponse(sessionResult.errorCode!!, sessionResult.errorMessage!!))
   174→                )
   175→            }
   176→
   177→            val response = McpSessionResponse(
   178→                sessionId = sessionResult.sessionId!!,
   179→                capabilities = request.capabilities,
   180→                serverInfo = mapOf(
   181→                    "name" to "Secman MCP Server",
   182→                    "version" to "1.0.0"
   183→                )
   184→            )
   185→
   186→            HttpResponse.status<McpSessionResponse>(HttpStatus.CREATED).body(response)
   187→
   188→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   189→            logger.error("Session creation failed", e)
   190→            HttpResponse.status<McpSessionResponse>(HttpStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)
   191→                .body(McpSessionResponse(error = McpErrorResponse("SYSTEM_ERROR", "Session creation failed")))
   192→        }
   193→    }
   194→
   195→    /**
   196→     * Close an MCP session.
   197→     */
   198→    @Delete("/session/{sessionId}")
   199→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_ANONYMOUS)
   200→    suspend fun closeSession(
   201→        @Header("X-MCP-API-Key") apiKey: String?,
   202→        sessionId: String
   203→    ): HttpResponse<Void> {
   204→        return try {
   205→            if (apiKey == null) {
   206→                return HttpResponse.status(HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED)
   207→            }
   208→
   209→            val authResult = authService.authenticateApiKey(apiKey)
   210→            if (!authResult.success) {
   211→                return HttpResponse.status(HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED)
   212→            }
   213→
   214→            // Validate session belongs to this API key
   215→            val sessionValidation = sessionService.validateSession(sessionId, false)
   216→            if (!sessionValidation.valid) {
   217→                return HttpResponse.status(HttpStatus.NOT_FOUND)
   218→            }
   219→
   220→            val session = sessionValidation.session!!
   221→            if (session.apiKeyId != authResult.apiKey!!.id) {
   222→                return HttpResponse.status(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN)
   223→            }
   224→
   225→            val closeResult = sessionService.closeSession(sessionId, "Client requested")
   226→            if (!closeResult.success) {
   227→                return HttpResponse.status(HttpStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)
   228→            }
   229→
   230→            HttpResponse.noContent()
   231→
   232→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   233→            logger.error("Session close failed for sessionId: $sessionId", e)
   234→            HttpResponse.status(HttpStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)
   235→        }
   236→    }
   237→
   238→    /**
   239→     * Execute an MCP tool.
   240→     *
   241→     * SECURITY: User delegation via X-MCP-User-Email header is mandatory.
   242→     * Computes effective permissions as intersection of user roles and API key permissions.
   243→     */
   244→    @Post("/tools/call")
   245→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_ANONYMOUS)
   246→    open suspend fun callTool(
   247→        @Header("X-MCP-API-Key") apiKey: String?,
   248→        @Header(DELEGATION_HEADER) delegatedUserEmail: String?,
   249→        @Valid @Body request: McpToolCallRequest
   250→    ): HttpResponse<McpToolCallResponse> {
   251→        val startTime = System.currentTimeMillis()
   252→
   253→        return try {
   254→            if (apiKey == null) {
   255→                return HttpResponse.status<McpToolCallResponse>(HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED)
   256→                    .body(McpToolCallResponse(
   257→                        jsonrpc = request.jsonrpc,
   258→                        id = request.id,
   259→                        error = McpErrorResponse("AUTH_REQUIRED", "API key required")
   260→                    ))
   261→            }
   262→
   263→            val authResult = authService.authenticateApiKey(apiKey)
   264→            if (!authResult.success) {
   265→                return HttpResponse.status<McpToolCallResponse>(HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED)
   266→                    .body(McpToolCallResponse(
   267→                        jsonrpc = request.jsonrpc,
   268→                        id = request.id,
   269→                        error = McpErrorResponse("AUTH_FAILED", authResult.errorMessage ?: "Authentication failed")
   270→                    ))
   271→            }
   272→
   273→            val mcpApiKey = authResult.apiKey!!
   274→
   275→            // Security: Check rate limits before processing (HIGH-005 fix)
   276→            val rateLimitInfo = toolPermissionService.checkRateLimitForApiKey(mcpApiKey.id, request.id)
   277→            if (rateLimitInfo.exceeded) {
   278→                auditService.logAuthenticationEvent(
   279→                    McpEventType.RATE_LIMITED,
   280→                    mcpApiKey.id,
   281→                    mcpApiKey.userId,
   282→                    success = false,
   283→                    errorCode = "RATE_LIMITED",
   284→                    errorMessage = "Rate limit exceeded: ${rateLimitInfo.remainingCalls} calls remaining, resets at ${rateLimitInfo.resetTime}",
   285→                    requestId = request.id
   286→                )
   287→
   288→                return HttpResponse.status<McpToolCallResponse>(HttpStatus.TOO_MANY_REQUESTS)
   289→                    .body(McpToolCallResponse(
   290→                        jsonrpc = request.jsonrpc,
   291→                        id = request.id,
   292→                        error = McpErrorResponse("RATE_LIMITED", "Rate limit exceeded. Resets at ${rateLimitInfo.resetTime}")
   293→                    ))
   294→            }
   295→
   296→            // Validate JSON-RPC format
   297→            if (request.jsonrpc != "2.0" || request.method != "tools/call") {
   298→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(
   299→                    McpToolCallResponse(
   300→                        jsonrpc = request.jsonrpc,
   301→                        id = request.id,
   302→                        error = McpErrorResponse("INVALID_REQUEST", "Invalid JSON-RPC request")
   303→                    )
   304→                )
   305→            }
   306→
   307→            // SECURITY: User delegation is mandatory
   308→            if (delegatedUserEmail.isNullOrBlank()) {
   309→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(
   310→                    McpToolCallResponse(
   311→                        jsonrpc = request.jsonrpc,
   312→                        id = request.id,
   313→                        error = McpErrorResponse(
   314→                            DelegationErrorCodes.DELEGATION_HEADER_REQUIRED,
   315→                            "X-MCP-User-Email header is required for all data-accessing endpoints"
   316→                        )
   317→                    )
   318→                )
   319→            }
   320→
   321→            if (!mcpApiKey.delegationEnabled) {
   322→                return HttpResponse.status<McpToolCallResponse>(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN)
   323→                    .body(McpToolCallResponse(
   324→                        jsonrpc = request.jsonrpc,
   325→                        id = request.id,
   326→                        error = McpErrorResponse(
   327→                            DelegationErrorCodes.DELEGATION_NOT_ENABLED,
   328→                            "User delegation is not enabled for this API key. Enable delegation in API key settings."
   329→                        )
   330→                    ))
   331→            }
   332→
   333→            val delegationValidation = delegationService.validateDelegation(mcpApiKey, delegatedUserEmail)
   334→            if (!delegationValidation.success) {
   335→                logger.warn(
   336→                    "Delegation failed for email={}, key={}, error={}",
   337→                    delegatedUserEmail, mcpApiKey.keyId, delegationValidation.errorCode
   338→                )
   339→                return HttpResponse.status<McpToolCallResponse>(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN)
   340→                    .body(McpToolCallResponse(
   341→                        jsonrpc = request.jsonrpc,
   342→                        id = request.id,
   343→                        error = McpErrorResponse(
   344→                            delegationValidation.errorCode ?: DelegationErrorCodes.DELEGATION_FAILED,
   345→                            delegationValidation.errorMessage ?: "Delegation validation failed"
   346→                        )
   347→                    ))
   348→            }
   349→
   350→            val delegation = DelegationContext(
   351→                delegatedUserEmail = delegatedUserEmail,
   352→                delegatedUserId = delegationValidation.user!!.id!!,
   353→                effectivePermissions = delegationValidation.effectivePermissions
   354→            )
   355→
   356→            val effectivePermissions = delegation.effectivePermissions
   357→
   358→            val toolName = request.params.name
   359→            val arguments = request.params.arguments
   360→
   361→            // Check tool permission using effective permissions
   362→            val permissionCheck = toolPermissionService.hasPermissionWithSet(
   363→                toolName,
   364→                effectivePermissions,
   365→                request.id
   366→            )
   367→
   368→            if (!permissionCheck.granted) {
   369→                auditService.logAuthenticationEvent(
   370→                    McpEventType.PERMISSION_DENIED,
   371→                    mcpApiKey.id,
   372→                    mcpApiKey.userId,
   373→                    success = false,
   374→                    errorCode = "PERMISSION_DENIED",
   375→                    errorMessage = permissionCheck.reason,
   376→                    requestId = request.id
   377→                )
   378→
   379→                return HttpResponse.status<McpToolCallResponse>(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN)
   380→                    .body(McpToolCallResponse(
   381→                        jsonrpc = request.jsonrpc,
   382→                        id = request.id,
   383→                        error = McpErrorResponse("PERMISSION_DENIED", permissionCheck.reason)
   384→                    ))
   385→            }
   386→
   387→            // Get and validate tool
   388→            val tool = toolRegistry.getTool(toolName)
   389→            if (tool == null) {
   390→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(
   391→                    McpToolCallResponse(
   392→                        jsonrpc = request.jsonrpc,
   393→                        id = request.id,
   394→                        error = McpErrorResponse("TOOL_NOT_FOUND", "Tool '$toolName' not found")
   395→                    )
   396→                )
   397→            }
   398→
   399→            // Validate arguments
   400→            val validationResult = toolRegistry.validateArguments(toolName, arguments)
   401→            if (!validationResult.isValid) {
   402→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(
   403→                    McpToolCallResponse(
   404→                        jsonrpc = request.jsonrpc,
   405→                        id = request.id,
   406→                        error = McpErrorResponse("INVALID_ARGUMENTS", validationResult.errorMessage!!)
   407→                    )
   408→                )
   409→            }
   410→
   411→            // Build execution context with access control data (Feature: 052-mcp-access-control)
   412→            val executionContext = accessControlService.buildExecutionContext(mcpApiKey, delegation)
   413→
   414→            // Execute tool with context
   415→            val toolResult = tool.execute(arguments, executionContext)
   416→            val duration = System.currentTimeMillis() - startTime
   417→
   418→            // Log tool execution with delegation info
   419→            auditService.logToolCall(
   420→                apiKeyId = mcpApiKey.id,
   421→                userId = mcpApiKey.userId,
   422→                sessionId = "http-${request.id}", // HTTP requests don't have sessions
   423→                toolName = toolName,
   424→                operation = tool.operation,
   425→                arguments = arguments,
   426→                success = !toolResult.isError,
   427→                durationMs = duration,
   428→                errorCode = if (toolResult.isError) (toolResult as com.secman.mcp.tools.McpToolResult.Error).code else null,
   429→                errorMessage = if (toolResult.isError) (toolResult as com.secman.mcp.tools.McpToolResult.Error).message else null,
   430→                requestId = request.id,
   431→                delegatedUserEmail = delegation.delegatedUserEmail,
   432→                delegatedUserId = delegation.delegatedUserId
   433→            )
   434→
   435→            val response = if (toolResult.isError) {
   436→                val error = toolResult as com.secman.mcp.tools.McpToolResult.Error
   437→                McpToolCallResponse(
   438→                    jsonrpc = request.jsonrpc,
   439→                    id = request.id,
   440→                    error = McpErrorResponse(error.code, error.message)
   441→                )
   442→            } else {
   443→                val success = toolResult as com.secman.mcp.tools.McpToolResult.Success
   444→                McpToolCallResponse(
   445→                    jsonrpc = request.jsonrpc,
   446→                    id = request.id,
   447→                    result = McpToolResult(
   448→                        content = success.content,
   449→                        isError = false,
   450→                        metadata = success.metadata
   451→                    )
   452→                )
   453→            }
   454→
   455→            HttpResponse.ok(response)
   456→
   457→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   458→            val duration = System.currentTimeMillis() - startTime
   459→            logger.error("Tool call failed: ${request.params.name}", e)
   460→
   461→            // Log the error
   462→            try {
   463→                if (apiKey != null) {
   464→                    val authResult = authService.authenticateApiKey(apiKey)
   465→                    if (authResult.success) {
   466→                        val apiKeyVal = authResult.apiKey!!
   467→                        auditService.logToolCall(
   468→                            apiKeyId = apiKeyVal.id,
   469→                            userId = apiKeyVal.userId,
   470→                            sessionId = "http-${request.id}",
   471→                            toolName = request.params.name,
   472→                            operation = McpOperation.READ, // Default
   473→                            arguments = request.params.arguments,
   474→                            success = false,
   475→                            durationMs = duration,
   476→                            errorCode = "SYSTEM_ERROR",
   477→                            errorMessage = "An internal error occurred",
   478→                            requestId = request.id
   479→                        )
   480→                    }
   481→                }
   482→            } catch (auditException: Exception) {
   483→                logger.error("Failed to log tool execution error", auditException)
   484→            }
   485→
   486→            HttpResponse.status<McpToolCallResponse>(HttpStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)
   487→                .body(McpToolCallResponse(
   488→                    jsonrpc = request.jsonrpc,
   489→                    id = request.id,
   490→                    error = McpErrorResponse("SYSTEM_ERROR", "Tool execution failed")
   491→                ))
   492→        }
   493→    }
   494→
   495→    private fun validateSessionRequest(request: McpSessionCreateRequest): String? {
   496→        if (request.clientInfo.name.isBlank()) {
   497→            return "Client name is required"
   498→        }
   499→
   500→        if (request.clientInfo.version.isBlank()) {
   501→            return "Client version is required"
   502→        }
   503→
   504→        return null
   505→    }

150→                return HttpResponse.redirect<Any>(URI.create("$frontendBaseUrl/login?error=${java.net.URLEncoder.encode("Invalid OAuth callback parameters", "UTF-8")}"))
   151→            }
   152→
   153→            logger.info("Looking up OAuth state: {}...", state.take(10))
   154→
   155→            // Find provider ID from state with retry mechanism
   156→            // Microsoft Azure with cached SSO can return in 100-500ms, potentially before
   157→            // the state-save transaction is fully visible. Retry handles this race condition.
   158→            val stateOpt = oauthService.findStateByValueWithRetry(state)
   159→            if (!stateOpt.isPresent) {
   160→                logger.error("Invalid OAuth state: state token not found in database after retries (first 10 chars: {})", state.take(10) + "...")
   161→                logger.error("Possible causes: state never saved, state expired (>10min), or state already consumed by another callback")
   162→                return HttpResponse.redirect<Any>(URI.create("$frontendBaseUrl/login?error=${java.net.URLEncoder.encode("Invalid or expired state parameter. Please try logging in again.", "UTF-8")}"))
   163→            }
   164→
   165→            val oauthState = stateOpt.get()
   166→            logger.info("OAuth state validated: providerId={}, createdAt={}, expiresAt={}",
   167→                oauthState.providerId, oauthState.createdAt, oauthState.expiresAt)
   168→
   169→            // Process OAuth callback with the already-validated state
   170→            val result = oauthService.handleCallback(oauthState, code)
   171→
   172→            when (result) {
   173→                is OAuthService.CallbackResult.Success -> {
   174→                    logger.info("OAuth login successful: user={}, email={}, roles={}",
   175→                        result.user.username, result.user.email, result.user.roles)
   176→
   177→                    // Create user info JSON using ObjectMapper for proper escaping (prevents JSON injection)
   178→                    val userInfoJson = objectMapper.writeValueAsString(mapOf(
   179→                        "id" to result.user.id,
   180→                        "username" to result.user.username,
   181→                        "email" to result.user.email,
   182→                        "roles" to result.user.roles,
   183→                        "workgroupCount" to result.user.workgroupCount,
   184→                        "awsAccountCount" to result.user.awsAccountCount,
   185→                        "domainCount" to result.user.domainCount
   186→                    ))
   187→
   188→                    // Only pass user metadata in URL - JWT is delivered solely via HttpOnly cookie
   189→                    // This prevents token exposure in browser history, proxy logs, and referrer headers
   190→                    val encodedUser = java.net.URLEncoder.encode(userInfoJson, "UTF-8")
   191→                    val redirectUrl = "$frontendBaseUrl/login/success?user=$encodedUser"
   192→
   193→                    logger.info("Redirecting to success page with auth cookie")
   194→                    // Set HttpOnly cookie for authentication (same as local login)
   195→                    HttpResponse.redirect<Any>(URI.create(redirectUrl))
   196→                        .cookie(authCookieService.createAuthCookie(result.token))
   197→                }
   198→
   199→                is OAuthService.CallbackResult.Error -> {
   200→                    logger.error("OAuth callback failed: {}", result.message)
   201→                    HttpResponse.redirect<Any>(URI.create("$frontendBaseUrl/login?error=${java.net.URLEncoder.encode(result.message, "UTF-8")}"))
   202→                }
   203→            }
   204→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   205→            logger.error("OAuth callback processing exception: {} - {}", e.javaClass.simpleName, e.message, e)
   206→            // Walk the exception cause chain to reveal RollbackException -> PersistenceException etc.
   207→            var cause: Throwable? = e.cause
   208→            var depth = 1
   209→            while (cause != null && depth <= 5) {
   210→                logger.error("  Cause chain [{}]: {} - {}", depth, cause.javaClass.simpleName, cause.message)
   211→                cause = cause.cause
   212→                depth++
   213→            }
   214→            val errorMsg = "An unexpected error occurred during login. Please try again."
   215→            HttpResponse.redirect<Any>(URI.create("$frontendBaseUrl/login?error=${java.net.URLEncoder.encode(errorMsg, "UTF-8")}"))
   216→        }
   217→    }
   218→
   219→
   220→    /**
   221→     * API endpoint for OAuth callback (for AJAX requests)
   222→     */
   223→    @Post("/callback")
   224→    @ExecuteOn(TaskExecutors.BLOCKING)
   225→    open fun callbackApi(
   226→        @Valid @Body callbackRequest: CallbackRequest
   227→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
   228→        return try {
   229→            // Find provider ID from state with retry mechanism
   230→            val stateOpt = oauthService.findStateByValueWithRetry(callbackRequest.state)
   231→            if (!stateOpt.isPresent) {
   232→                logger.error("Invalid OAuth state in API callback: state token not found after retries (first 10 chars: {})",
   233→                    callbackRequest.state.take(10) + "...")
   234→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("Invalid or expired state parameter. Please try again."))
   235→            }
   236→
   237→            val oauthState = stateOpt.get()
   238→            val result = oauthService.handleCallback(oauthState, callbackRequest.code)
   239→            
   240→            when (result) {
   241→                is OAuthService.CallbackResult.Success -> {
   242→                    logger.info("OAuth API login successful for user: {}", result.user.username)
   243→
   244→                    val response = LoginResponse(
   245→                        id = result.user.id,
   246→                        username = result.user.username,
   247→                        email = result.user.email,
   248→                        roles = result.user.roles
   249→                    )
   250→
   251→                    HttpResponse.ok(response)
   252→                        .cookie(authCookieService.createAuthCookie(result.token))
   253→                }
   254→                
   255→                is OAuthService.CallbackResult.Error -> {
   256→                    logger.error("OAuth API callback error: {}", result.message)
   257→                    HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse(result.message))
   258→                }
   259→            }
   260→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   261→            logger.error("OAuth API callback processing error: {}", e.message, e)
   262→            HttpResponse.serverError(ErrorResponse("An unexpected error occurred during login. Please try again."))
   263→        }
   264→    }
   265→
   266→    @Serdeable
   267→    data class CallbackRequest(
   268→        val code: String,
   269→        val state: String
   270→    )
   271→
   272→}

1→package com.secman.controller
     2→
     3→import io.micronaut.management.endpoint.annotation.Endpoint
     4→import io.micronaut.management.endpoint.annotation.Read
     5→
     6→/**
     7→ * Management endpoint for JVM memory metrics
     8→ * Feature: 073-memory-optimization
     9→ *
    10→ * Exposes heap memory statistics for monitoring and validation
    11→ * of memory optimization targets:
    12→ * - SC-001: Query memory <50MB above baseline
    13→ * - SC-002: Export memory <100MB above baseline
    14→ *
    15→ * Access: Anonymous (management endpoint)
    16→ * Path: GET /memory
    17→ */
    18→@Endpoint(id = "memory")
    19→class MemoryController {
    20→
    21→    /**
    22→     * Get current JVM heap memory metrics
    23→     *
    24→     * @return Map containing:
    25→     *   - heap.used: Used heap memory in MB
    26→     *   - heap.max: Maximum heap memory in MB
    27→     *   - heap.free: Free heap memory in MB
    28→     *   - heap.total: Total allocated heap in MB
    29→     *   - timestamp: Unix timestamp in milliseconds
    30→     */
    31→    @Read
    32→    fun memory(): Map<String, Any> {
    33→        val runtime = Runtime.getRuntime()
    34→        val usedMemory = runtime.totalMemory() - runtime.freeMemory()
    35→
    36→        return mapOf(
    37→            "heap" to mapOf(
    38→                "used" to usedMemory / 1024 / 1024,
    39→                "max" to runtime.maxMemory() / 1024 / 1024,
    40→                "free" to runtime.freeMemory() / 1024 / 1024,
    41→                "total" to runtime.totalMemory() / 1024 / 1024
    42→            ),
    43→            "timestamp" to System.currentTimeMillis()
    44→        )
    45→    }
    46→}
    47→

    36→/**
    37→ * OAuth/OIDC Authentication Service
    38→ *
    39→ * Handles complete OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect (OIDC) authentication flows for external identity providers.
    40→ * This is the core authentication service for all single sign-on (SSO) integrations in Secman.
    41→ *
    42→ * ## Supported Identity Providers
    43→ * - **Microsoft Azure AD / Entra ID**: Full OIDC support with tenant validation, JWKS signature verification
    44→ * - **GitHub**: OAuth 2.0 with user info endpoint for profile data
    45→ * - **Generic OIDC**: Any standards-compliant OIDC provider via dynamic configuration
    46→ *
    47→ * ## OAuth Flow Architecture
    48→ *
    49→ * The service implements a standard OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code flow with OIDC extensions:
    50→ *
    51→ * ```
    52→ * ┌─────────┐     ┌─────────────┐     ┌────────────────┐     ┌─────────────┐
    53→ * │ Browser │────▶│ OAuthCtrl   │────▶│ OAuthService   │────▶│ IdP (Azure/ │
    54→ * │         │◀────│ /authorize  │◀────│ buildAuthUrl() │◀────│ GitHub/etc) │
    55→ * └─────────┘     └─────────────┘     └────────────────┘     └─────────────┘
    56→ *      │                                      │
    57→ *      │ 1. User clicks "Login with..."       │ 2. Generate state, save to DB
    58→ *      │                                      │
    59→ *      ▼                                      ▼
    60→ * ┌─────────┐     ┌─────────────┐     ┌────────────────┐     ┌─────────────┐
    61→ * │ Browser │────▶│ OAuthCtrl   │────▶│ OAuthService   │────▶│ IdP Token   │
    62→ * │         │◀────│ /callback   │◀────│ handleCallback │◀────│ Endpoint    │
    63→ * └─────────┘     └─────────────┘     └────────────────┘     └─────────────┘
    64→ *                                             │
    65→ *      3. IdP redirects with code ───────────▶│ 4. Exchange code for token
    66→ *                                             │ 5. Validate ID token (OIDC)
    67→ *      6. Return JWT for frontend ◀───────────│ 7. Find or create user
    68→ * ```
    69→ *
    70→ * ## Race Condition Handling (Microsoft Azure SSO)
    71→ *
    72→ * **Problem**: Microsoft Azure callbacks can arrive within 100-500ms when users have cached SSO sessions.
    73→ * This creates a race condition where the OAuth callback arrives before the state-save transaction
    74→ * is fully committed and visible to other database connections.
    75→ *
    76→ * **Solution**: This service implements two complementary strategies:
    77→ *
    78→ * 1. **Immediate State Commit**: [saveOAuthStateImmediately] uses `REQUIRES_NEW` transaction
    79→ *    with explicit `entityManager.flush()` to ensure the state is committed before redirect.
    80→ *
    81→ * 2. **Retry with Exponential Backoff**: [findStateByValueWithRetry] retries state lookups
    82→ *    with configurable backoff (default: 100ms → 150ms → 225ms → 337ms → 500ms).
    83→ *
    84→ * Configuration via environment variables or `application.yml`:
    85→ * ```yaml
    86→ * secman:
    87→ *   oauth:
    88→ *     state-retry:
    89→ *       max-attempts: 5           # OAUTH_STATE_RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS
    90→ *       initial-delay-ms: 100     # OAUTH_STATE_RETRY_INITIAL_DELAY
    91→ *       max-delay-ms: 500         # OAUTH_STATE_RETRY_MAX_DELAY
    92→ *       backoff-multiplier: 1.5   # OAUTH_STATE_RETRY_BACKOFF_MULTIPLIER
    93→ *     token-exchange:
    94→ *       max-retries: 2            # OAUTH_TOKEN_EXCHANGE_MAX_RETRIES
    95→ *       retry-delay-ms: 500       # OAUTH_TOKEN_EXCHANGE_RETRY_DELAY
    96→ * ```
    97→ *
    98→ * ## Security Features
    99→ *
   100→ * - **State Parameter**: CSRF protection via cryptographically random state tokens
