    17→/**
    18→ * Delegation context passed through the request when X-MCP-User-Email header is present.
    19→ * Feature: 050-mcp-user-delegation
    20→ */
    21→data class DelegationContext(
    22→    val delegatedUserEmail: String,
    23→    val delegatedUserId: Long,
    24→    val effectivePermissions: Set<McpPermission>
    25→)
    26→
    27→/**
    28→ * Main MCP protocol controller handling core MCP operations.
    29→ *
    30→ * Implements the Model Context Protocol for AI assistant integration.
    31→ */
    32→@Controller("/api/mcp")
    33→open class McpController(
    34→    @Inject private val authService: McpAuthenticationService,
    35→    @Inject private val sessionService: McpSessionService,
    36→    @Inject private val auditService: McpAuditService,
    37→    @Inject private val toolPermissionService: McpToolPermissionService,
    38→    @Inject private val toolRegistry: McpToolRegistry,
    39→    @Inject private val delegationService: McpDelegationService,
    40→    @Inject private val accessControlService: McpAccessControlService
    41→) {
    42→    private val logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(McpController::class.java)
    43→
    44→    companion object {
    45→        /** Header name for user delegation email. Feature: 050-mcp-user-delegation */
    46→        const val DELEGATION_HEADER = "X-MCP-User-Email"
    47→    }
    48→
    49→    /**
    50→     * Get MCP server capabilities.
    51→     * Returns available tools based on effective permissions (user roles ∩ API key permissions).
    52→     *
    53→     * SECURITY: User delegation via X-MCP-User-Email header is mandatory.
    54→     * Requests without this header are rejected with DELEGATION_HEADER_REQUIRED.
    55→     */
    56→    @Get("/capabilities")
    57→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_ANONYMOUS)
    58→    suspend fun getCapabilities(
    59→        @Header("X-MCP-API-Key") apiKey: String?,
    60→        @Header(DELEGATION_HEADER) delegatedUserEmail: String?
    61→    ): HttpResponse<McpCapabilitiesResponse> {
    62→        return try {
    63→            if (apiKey == null) {
    64→                return HttpResponse.status<McpCapabilitiesResponse>(HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED)
    65→                    .body(McpCapabilitiesResponse(error = McpErrorResponse("AUTH_REQUIRED", "API key required")))
    66→            }
    67→
    68→            val authResult = authService.authenticateApiKey(apiKey)
    69→            if (!authResult.success) {
    70→                return HttpResponse.status<McpCapabilitiesResponse>(HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED)
    71→                    .body(McpCapabilitiesResponse(error = McpErrorResponse("AUTH_FAILED", authResult.errorMessage ?: "Authentication failed")))
    72→            }
    73→
    74→            val mcpApiKey = authResult.apiKey!!
    75→
    76→            // SECURITY: User delegation is mandatory
    77→            if (delegatedUserEmail.isNullOrBlank()) {
    78→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(
    79→                    McpCapabilitiesResponse(error = McpErrorResponse(
    80→                        DelegationErrorCodes.DELEGATION_HEADER_REQUIRED,
    81→                        "X-MCP-User-Email header is required for all data-accessing endpoints"
    82→                    ))
    83→                )
    84→            }
    85→
    86→            if (!mcpApiKey.delegationEnabled) {
    87→                return HttpResponse.status<McpCapabilitiesResponse>(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN)
    88→                    .body(McpCapabilitiesResponse(error = McpErrorResponse(
    89→                        DelegationErrorCodes.DELEGATION_NOT_ENABLED,
    90→                        "User delegation is not enabled for this API key. Enable delegation in API key settings."
    91→                    )))
    92→            }
    93→
    94→            val validationResult = delegationService.validateDelegation(mcpApiKey, delegatedUserEmail)
    95→            if (!validationResult.success) {
    96→                return HttpResponse.status<McpCapabilitiesResponse>(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN)
    97→                    .body(McpCapabilitiesResponse(error = McpErrorResponse(
    98→                        validationResult.errorCode ?: DelegationErrorCodes.DELEGATION_FAILED,
    99→                        validationResult.errorMessage ?: "Delegation validation failed"
   100→                    )))
   101→            }
   102→
   103→            val effectivePermissions = validationResult.effectivePermissions
   104→            val toolCapabilities = toolRegistry.getToolCapabilities(effectivePermissions)
   105→
   106→            val serverInfo = mutableMapOf<String, Any>(
   107→                "name" to "Secman MCP Server",
   108→                "version" to "1.0.0",
   109→                "protocol" to "mcp/1.0",
   110→                "delegationActive" to true,
   111→                "delegatedUser" to delegatedUserEmail
   112→            )
   113→
   114→            val response = McpCapabilitiesResponse(
   115→                capabilities = mapOf(
   116→                    "tools" to (toolCapabilities["tools"] as? List<*> ?: emptyList<Any>()),
   117→                    "resources" to emptyMap<String, Any>(),
   118→                    "prompts" to emptyMap<String, Any>()
   119→                ),
   120→                serverInfo = serverInfo
   121→            )
   122→
   123→            HttpResponse.ok(response)
   124→
   125→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   126→            logger.error("Capabilities request failed", e)
   127→            HttpResponse.status<McpCapabilitiesResponse>(HttpStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)
   128→                .body(McpCapabilitiesResponse(error = McpErrorResponse("SYSTEM_ERROR", "Internal server error")))
   129→        }
   130→    }
   131→
   132→    /**
   133→     * Create a new MCP session.
   134→     */
   135→    @Post("/session")
   136→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_ANONYMOUS)
   137→    open suspend fun createSession(
   138→        @Header("X-MCP-API-Key") apiKey: String?,
   139→        @Valid @Body request: McpSessionCreateRequest
   140→    ): HttpResponse<McpSessionResponse> {
   141→        return try {
   142→            if (apiKey == null) {
   143→                return HttpResponse.status<McpSessionResponse>(HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED)
   144→                    .body(McpSessionResponse(error = McpErrorResponse("AUTH_REQUIRED", "API key required")))
   145→            }
   146→
   147→            val authResult = authService.authenticateApiKey(apiKey)
   148→            if (!authResult.success) {
   149→                return HttpResponse.status<McpSessionResponse>(HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED)
   150→                    .body(McpSessionResponse(error = McpErrorResponse("AUTH_FAILED", authResult.errorMessage ?: "Authentication failed")))
   151→            }
   152→
   153→            val mcpApiKey = authResult.apiKey!!
   154→
   155→            // Validate request
   156→            val validationError = validateSessionRequest(request)
   157→            if (validationError != null) {
   158→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(
   159→                    McpSessionResponse(error = McpErrorResponse("INVALID_REQUEST", validationError))
   160→                )
   161→            }
   162→
   163→            val sessionResult = sessionService.createSession(
   164→                apiKeyId = mcpApiKey.id,
   165→                userId = mcpApiKey.userId,
   166→                clientInfo = request.clientInfo.toJsonString(),
   167→                capabilities = request.capabilities.toJsonString(),
   168→                connectionType = McpConnectionType.HTTP
   169→            )
   170→
   171→            if (!sessionResult.success) {
   172→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(
   173→                    McpSessionResponse(error = McpErrorResponse(sessionResult.errorCode!!, sessionResult.errorMessage!!))
   174→                )
   175→            }
   176→
   177→            val response = McpSessionResponse(
   178→                sessionId = sessionResult.sessionId!!,
   179→                capabilities = request.capabilities,
   180→                serverInfo = mapOf(
   181→                    "name" to "Secman MCP Server",
   182→                    "version" to "1.0.0"
   183→                )
   184→            )
   185→
   186→            HttpResponse.status<McpSessionResponse>(HttpStatus.CREATED).body(response)
   187→
   188→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   189→            logger.error("Session creation failed", e)
   190→            HttpResponse.status<McpSessionResponse>(HttpStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)
   191→                .body(McpSessionResponse(error = McpErrorResponse("SYSTEM_ERROR", "Session creation failed")))
   192→        }
   193→    }
   194→
   195→    /**
   196→     * Close an MCP session.
   197→     */
   198→    @Delete("/session/{sessionId}")
   199→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_ANONYMOUS)
   200→    suspend fun closeSession(
   201→        @Header("X-MCP-API-Key") apiKey: String?,
   202→        sessionId: String
   203→    ): HttpResponse<Void> {
   204→        return try {
   205→            if (apiKey == null) {
   206→                return HttpResponse.status(HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED)
   207→            }
   208→
   209→            val authResult = authService.authenticateApiKey(apiKey)
   210→            if (!authResult.success) {
   211→                return HttpResponse.status(HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED)
   212→            }
   213→
   214→            // Validate session belongs to this API key
   215→            val sessionValidation = sessionService.validateSession(sessionId, false)
   216→            if (!sessionValidation.valid) {
   217→                return HttpResponse.status(HttpStatus.NOT_FOUND)
   218→            }
   219→
   220→            val session = sessionValidation.session!!
   221→            if (session.apiKeyId != authResult.apiKey!!.id) {
   222→                return HttpResponse.status(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN)
   223→            }
   224→
   225→            val closeResult = sessionService.closeSession(sessionId, "Client requested")
   226→            if (!closeResult.success) {
   227→                return HttpResponse.status(HttpStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)
   228→            }
   229→
   230→            HttpResponse.noContent()
   231→
   232→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   233→            logger.error("Session close failed for sessionId: $sessionId", e)
   234→            HttpResponse.status(HttpStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)
   235→        }
   236→    }
   237→
   238→    /**
   239→     * Execute an MCP tool.
   240→     *
   241→     * SECURITY: User delegation via X-MCP-User-Email header is mandatory.
   242→     * Computes effective permissions as intersection of user roles and API key permissions.
   243→     */
   244→    @Post("/tools/call")
   245→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_ANONYMOUS)
   246→    open suspend fun callTool(
   247→        @Header("X-MCP-API-Key") apiKey: String?,
   248→        @Header(DELEGATION_HEADER) delegatedUserEmail: String?,
   249→        @Valid @Body request: McpToolCallRequest
   250→    ): HttpResponse<McpToolCallResponse> {
   251→        val startTime = System.currentTimeMillis()
   252→
   253→        return try {
   254→            if (apiKey == null) {
   255→                return HttpResponse.status<McpToolCallResponse>(HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED)
   256→                    .body(McpToolCallResponse(
   257→                        jsonrpc = request.jsonrpc,
   258→                        id = request.id,
   259→                        error = McpErrorResponse("AUTH_REQUIRED", "API key required")
   260→                    ))
   261→            }
   262→
   263→            val authResult = authService.authenticateApiKey(apiKey)
   264→            if (!authResult.success) {
   265→                return HttpResponse.status<McpToolCallResponse>(HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED)
   266→                    .body(McpToolCallResponse(
   267→                        jsonrpc = request.jsonrpc,
   268→                        id = request.id,
   269→                        error = McpErrorResponse("AUTH_FAILED", authResult.errorMessage ?: "Authentication failed")
   270→                    ))
   271→            }
   272→
   273→            val mcpApiKey = authResult.apiKey!!
   274→
   275→            // Security: Check rate limits before processing (HIGH-005 fix)
   276→            val rateLimitInfo = toolPermissionService.checkRateLimitForApiKey(mcpApiKey.id, request.id)
   277→            if (rateLimitInfo.exceeded) {
   278→                auditService.logAuthenticationEvent(
   279→                    McpEventType.RATE_LIMITED,
   280→                    mcpApiKey.id,
   281→                    mcpApiKey.userId,
   282→                    success = false,
   283→                    errorCode = "RATE_LIMITED",
   284→                    errorMessage = "Rate limit exceeded: ${rateLimitInfo.remainingCalls} calls remaining, resets at ${rateLimitInfo.resetTime}",
   285→                    requestId = request.id
   286→                )
   287→
   288→                return HttpResponse.status<McpToolCallResponse>(HttpStatus.TOO_MANY_REQUESTS)
   289→                    .body(McpToolCallResponse(
   290→                        jsonrpc = request.jsonrpc,
   291→                        id = request.id,
   292→                        error = McpErrorResponse("RATE_LIMITED", "Rate limit exceeded. Resets at ${rateLimitInfo.resetTime}")
   293→                    ))
   294→            }
   295→
   296→            // Validate JSON-RPC format
   297→            if (request.jsonrpc != "2.0" || request.method != "tools/call") {
   298→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(
   299→                    McpToolCallResponse(
   300→                        jsonrpc = request.jsonrpc,
   301→                        id = request.id,
   302→                        error = McpErrorResponse("INVALID_REQUEST", "Invalid JSON-RPC request")
   303→                    )
   304→                )
   305→            }
   306→
   307→            // SECURITY: User delegation is mandatory
   308→            if (delegatedUserEmail.isNullOrBlank()) {
   309→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(
   310→                    McpToolCallResponse(
   311→                        jsonrpc = request.jsonrpc,
   312→                        id = request.id,
   313→                        error = McpErrorResponse(
   314→                            DelegationErrorCodes.DELEGATION_HEADER_REQUIRED,
   315→                            "X-MCP-User-Email header is required for all data-accessing endpoints"
   316→                        )
   317→                    )
   318→                )
   319→            }
   320→
   321→            if (!mcpApiKey.delegationEnabled) {
   322→                return HttpResponse.status<McpToolCallResponse>(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN)
   323→                    .body(McpToolCallResponse(
   324→                        jsonrpc = request.jsonrpc,
   325→                        id = request.id,
   326→                        error = McpErrorResponse(
   327→                            DelegationErrorCodes.DELEGATION_NOT_ENABLED,
   328→                            "User delegation is not enabled for this API key. Enable delegation in API key settings."
   329→                        )
   330→                    ))
   331→            }
   332→
   333→            val delegationValidation = delegationService.validateDelegation(mcpApiKey, delegatedUserEmail)
   334→            if (!delegationValidation.success) {
   335→                logger.warn(
   336→                    "Delegation failed for email={}, key={}, error={}",
   337→                    delegatedUserEmail, mcpApiKey.keyId, delegationValidation.errorCode
   338→                )
   339→                return HttpResponse.status<McpToolCallResponse>(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN)
   340→                    .body(McpToolCallResponse(
   341→                        jsonrpc = request.jsonrpc,
   342→                        id = request.id,
   343→                        error = McpErrorResponse(
   344→                            delegationValidation.errorCode ?: DelegationErrorCodes.DELEGATION_FAILED,
   345→                            delegationValidation.errorMessage ?: "Delegation validation failed"
   346→                        )
   347→                    ))
   348→            }
   349→
   350→            val delegation = DelegationContext(
   351→                delegatedUserEmail = delegatedUserEmail,
   352→                delegatedUserId = delegationValidation.user!!.id!!,
   353→                effectivePermissions = delegationValidation.effectivePermissions
   354→            )
   355→
   356→            val effectivePermissions = delegation.effectivePermissions
   357→
   358→            val toolName = request.params.name
   359→            val arguments = request.params.arguments
   360→
   361→            // Check tool permission using effective permissions
   362→            val permissionCheck = toolPermissionService.hasPermissionWithSet(
   363→                toolName,
   364→                effectivePermissions,
   365→                request.id
   366→            )
   367→
   368→            if (!permissionCheck.granted) {
   369→                auditService.logAuthenticationEvent(
   370→                    McpEventType.PERMISSION_DENIED,
   371→                    mcpApiKey.id,
   372→                    mcpApiKey.userId,
   373→                    success = false,
   374→                    errorCode = "PERMISSION_DENIED",
   375→                    errorMessage = permissionCheck.reason,
   376→                    requestId = request.id
   377→                )
   378→
   379→                return HttpResponse.status<McpToolCallResponse>(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN)
   380→                    .body(McpToolCallResponse(
   381→                        jsonrpc = request.jsonrpc,
   382→                        id = request.id,
   383→                        error = McpErrorResponse("PERMISSION_DENIED", permissionCheck.reason)
   384→                    ))
   385→            }
   386→
   387→            // Get and validate tool
   388→            val tool = toolRegistry.getTool(toolName)
   389→            if (tool == null) {
   390→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(
   391→                    McpToolCallResponse(
   392→                        jsonrpc = request.jsonrpc,
   393→                        id = request.id,
   394→                        error = McpErrorResponse("TOOL_NOT_FOUND", "Tool '$toolName' not found")
   395→                    )
   396→                )
   397→            }
   398→
   399→            // Validate arguments
   400→            val validationResult = toolRegistry.validateArguments(toolName, arguments)
   401→            if (!validationResult.isValid) {
   402→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(
   403→                    McpToolCallResponse(
   404→                        jsonrpc = request.jsonrpc,
   405→                        id = request.id,
   406→                        error = McpErrorResponse("INVALID_ARGUMENTS", validationResult.errorMessage!!)
   407→                    )
   408→                )
   409→            }
   410→
   411→            // Build execution context with access control data (Feature: 052-mcp-access-control)
   412→            val executionContext = accessControlService.buildExecutionContext(mcpApiKey, delegation)
   413→
   414→            // Execute tool with context
   415→            val toolResult = tool.execute(arguments, executionContext)
   416→            val duration = System.currentTimeMillis() - startTime
   417→
   418→            // Log tool execution with delegation info
   419→            auditService.logToolCall(
   420→                apiKeyId = mcpApiKey.id,
   421→                userId = mcpApiKey.userId,
   422→                sessionId = "http-${request.id}", // HTTP requests don't have sessions
   423→                toolName = toolName,
   424→                operation = tool.operation,
   425→                arguments = arguments,
   426→                success = !toolResult.isError,
   427→                durationMs = duration,
   428→                errorCode = if (toolResult.isError) (toolResult as com.secman.mcp.tools.McpToolResult.Error).code else null,
   429→                errorMessage = if (toolResult.isError) (toolResult as com.secman.mcp.tools.McpToolResult.Error).message else null,
   430→                requestId = request.id,
   431→                delegatedUserEmail = delegation.delegatedUserEmail,
   432→                delegatedUserId = delegation.delegatedUserId
   433→            )
   434→
   435→            val response = if (toolResult.isError) {
   436→                val error = toolResult as com.secman.mcp.tools.McpToolResult.Error
   437→                McpToolCallResponse(
   438→                    jsonrpc = request.jsonrpc,
   439→                    id = request.id,
   440→                    error = McpErrorResponse(error.code, error.message)
   441→                )
   442→            } else {
   443→                val success = toolResult as com.secman.mcp.tools.McpToolResult.Success
   444→                McpToolCallResponse(
   445→                    jsonrpc = request.jsonrpc,
   446→                    id = request.id,
   447→                    result = McpToolResult(
   448→                        content = success.content,
   449→                        isError = false,
   450→                        metadata = success.metadata
   451→                    )
   452→                )
   453→            }
   454→
   455→            HttpResponse.ok(response)
   456→
   457→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   458→            val duration = System.currentTimeMillis() - startTime
   459→            logger.error("Tool call failed: ${request.params.name}", e)
   460→
   461→            // Log the error
   462→            try {
   463→                if (apiKey != null) {
   464→                    val authResult = authService.authenticateApiKey(apiKey)
   465→                    if (authResult.success) {
   466→                        val apiKeyVal = authResult.apiKey!!
   467→                        auditService.logToolCall(
   468→                            apiKeyId = apiKeyVal.id,
   469→                            userId = apiKeyVal.userId,
   470→                            sessionId = "http-${request.id}",
   471→                            toolName = request.params.name,
   472→                            operation = McpOperation.READ, // Default
   473→                            arguments = request.params.arguments,
   474→                            success = false,
   475→                            durationMs = duration,
   476→                            errorCode = "SYSTEM_ERROR",
   477→                            errorMessage = "An internal error occurred",
   478→                            requestId = request.id
   479→                        )
   480→                    }
   481→                }
   482→            } catch (auditException: Exception) {
   483→                logger.error("Failed to log tool execution error", auditException)
   484→            }
   485→
   486→            HttpResponse.status<McpToolCallResponse>(HttpStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)
   487→                .body(McpToolCallResponse(
   488→                    jsonrpc = request.jsonrpc,
   489→                    id = request.id,
   490→                    error = McpErrorResponse("SYSTEM_ERROR", "Tool execution failed")
   491→                ))
   492→        }
   493→    }
   494→
   495→    private fun validateSessionRequest(request: McpSessionCreateRequest): String? {
   496→        if (request.clientInfo.name.isBlank()) {
   497→            return "Client name is required"
   498→        }
   499→
   500→        if (request.clientInfo.version.isBlank()) {
   501→            return "Client version is required"
   502→        }
   503→
   504→        return null
   505→    }
   506→
   507→    private fun McpClientInfo.toJsonString(): String {
   508→        return com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.ObjectMapper().writeValueAsString(
   509→            mapOf(
   510→                "name" to name,
   511→                "version" to version,
   512→                "additionalInfo" to (additionalInfo ?: emptyMap())
   513→            )
   514→        )
   515→    }
   516→
   517→    private fun Map<String, Any>.toJsonString(): String {
   518→        return com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.ObjectMapper().writeValueAsString(this)
   519→    }
   520→}

1→package com.secman.service
     2→
     3→import com.secman.dto.CveLookupDto
     4→import io.micronaut.cache.annotation.Cacheable
     5→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
     6→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
     7→import java.net.URI
     8→import java.net.http.HttpClient
     9→import java.net.http.HttpRequest
    10→import java.net.http.HttpResponse
    11→import java.time.Duration
    12→
    13→/**
    14→ * Service for looking up CVE details from NVD API v2.0
    15→ *
    16→ * Proxies requests to NVD to avoid CORS issues and centralize caching.
    17→ * Responses are cached for 24h via Micronaut's Caffeine cache.
    18→ *
    19→ * Feature: 072-cve-link-lookup
    20→ */
    21→@Singleton
    22→open class CveLookupService {
    23→
    24→    private val logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(CveLookupService::class.java)
    25→
    26→    private val httpClient: HttpClient = HttpClient.newBuilder()
    27→        .connectTimeout(Duration.ofSeconds(5))
    28→        .build()
    29→
    30→    private val cveIdPattern = Regex("^CVE-\\d{4}-\\d{4,}\$")
    31→
    32→    /**
    33→     * Look up CVE details from NVD API v2.0
    34→     *
    35→     * @param cveId CVE identifier (e.g., "CVE-2023-12345")
    36→     * @return CveLookupDto with CVE details, or null if not found or invalid
    37→     */
    38→    @Cacheable("cve_descriptions")
    39→    open fun lookupCve(cveId: String): CveLookupDto? {
    40→        if (!cveIdPattern.matches(cveId)) {
    41→            logger.debug("Invalid CVE ID format: {}", cveId)
    42→            return null
    43→        }
    44→
    45→        return try {
    46→            val url = "https://services.nvd.nist.gov/rest/json/cves/2.0?cveId=$cveId"
    47→            val request = HttpRequest.newBuilder()
    48→                .uri(URI.create(url))
    49→                .timeout(Duration.ofSeconds(10))
    50→                .header("Accept", "application/json")
    51→                .GET()
    52→                .build()
    53→
    54→            val response = httpClient.send(request, HttpResponse.BodyHandlers.ofString())
    55→
    56→            if (response.statusCode() != 200) {
    57→                logger.warn("NVD API returned status {} for CVE {}", response.statusCode(), cveId)
    58→                return null
    59→            }
    60→
    61→            parseNvdResponse(cveId, response.body())
    62→        } catch (e: Exception) {
    63→            logger.error("Error looking up CVE {}: {}", cveId, e.message)
    64→            null
    65→        }
    66→    }
    67→
    68→    private fun parseNvdResponse(cveId: String, responseBody: String): CveLookupDto? {
    69→        return try {
    70→            // Simple JSON parsing without Jackson dependency - parse NVD v2.0 response
    71→            val body = responseBody
    72→
    73→            // Extract description (English)
    74→            val description = extractEnglishDescription(body)
    75→
    76→            // Extract CVSS score and severity from v31 or v2
    77→            val cvssScore = extractCvssScore(body)
    78→            val severity = extractSeverity(body)
    79→
    80→            // Extract published date
    81→            val publishedDate = extractField(body, "published")
    82→
    83→            // Extract references (first 5)
    84→            val references = extractReferences(body)
    85→
    86→            CveLookupDto(
    87→                cveId = cveId,
    88→                description = description,
    89→                severity = severity,
    90→                cvssScore = cvssScore,
    91→                publishedDate = publishedDate,
    92→                references = references
    93→            )
    94→        } catch (e: Exception) {
    95→            logger.error("Error parsing NVD response for CVE {}: {}", cveId, e.message)
    96→            null
    97→        }
    98→    }
    99→
   100→    private fun extractEnglishDescription(json: String): String? {
   101→        // Find descriptions array and extract English description
   102→        val descriptionsIdx = json.indexOf("\"descriptions\"")
   103→        if (descriptionsIdx == -1) return null
   104→
   105→        // Find English description value
   106→        val langEnIdx = json.indexOf("\"lang\":\"en\"", descriptionsIdx)
   107→        if (langEnIdx == -1) return null
   108→
   109→        // Look for "value" near this lang entry (within the same object)
   110→        val valueIdx = json.indexOf("\"value\":\"", langEnIdx - 200)
   111→        if (valueIdx == -1 || valueIdx > langEnIdx + 200) {
   112→            // Try looking after lang
   113→            val valueAfterIdx = json.indexOf("\"value\":\"", langEnIdx)
   114→            if (valueAfterIdx == -1 || valueAfterIdx > langEnIdx + 200) return null
   115→            return extractJsonStringValue(json, valueAfterIdx)
   116→        }
   117→        return extractJsonStringValue(json, valueIdx)
   118→    }
   119→
   120→    private fun extractJsonStringValue(json: String, valueKeyIdx: Int): String? {
   121→        val valueStart = json.indexOf("\"value\":\"", valueKeyIdx)
   122→        if (valueStart == -1) return null
   123→        val contentStart = valueStart + "\"value\":\"".length
   124→        val contentEnd = findClosingQuote(json, contentStart)
   125→        if (contentEnd == -1) return null
   126→        return json.substring(contentStart, contentEnd).replace("\\\"", "\"").replace("\\n", " ")
   127→    }
   128→
   129→    private fun findClosingQuote(json: String, startIdx: Int): Int {
   130→        var i = startIdx
   131→        while (i < json.length) {
   132→            if (json[i] == '"' && (i == 0 || json[i - 1] != '\\')) {
   133→                return i
   134→            }
   135→            i++
   136→        }
   137→        return -1
   138→    }
   139→
   140→    private fun extractCvssScore(json: String): Double? {
   141→        // Try CVSS v3.1 first, then v3.0, then v2
   142→        for (key in listOf("\"cvssMetricV31\"", "\"cvssMetricV30\"", "\"cvssMetricV2\"")) {
   143→            val idx = json.indexOf(key)
   144→            if (idx != -1) {
   145→                val scoreIdx = json.indexOf("\"baseScore\":", idx)
   146→                if (scoreIdx != -1 && scoreIdx < idx + 500) {
   147→                    val numStart = scoreIdx + "\"baseScore\":".length
   148→                    val numEnd = json.indexOfAny(charArrayOf(',', '}', ' '), numStart)
   149→                    if (numEnd != -1) {
   150→                        return json.substring(numStart, numEnd).trim().toDoubleOrNull()

1→package com.secman.controller

    16→/**
    17→ * Controller for application-wide settings.
    18→ * Feature: 068-requirements-alignment-process
    19→ *
    20→ * Provides endpoints for ADMIN users to configure application settings
    21→ * such as the base URL used in email notifications.
    22→ *
    23→ * Access Control: ADMIN role required for all endpoints
    24→ */
    25→@Controller("/api/settings/app")
    26→@Secured("ADMIN")
    27→@ExecuteOn(TaskExecutors.BLOCKING)
    28→open class AppSettingsController(
    29→    private val appSettingsService: AppSettingsService
    30→) {
    31→    private val logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(AppSettingsController::class.java)
    32→
    33→    @Serdeable
    34→    data class ErrorResponse(
    35→        val status: Int,
    36→        val message: String,
    37→        val timestamp: String = java.time.Instant.now().toString()
    38→    )
    39→
    40→    @Serdeable
    41→    data class UpdateAppSettingsRequest(
    42→        @field:NotBlank(message = "Base URL is required")
    43→        val baseUrl: String,
    44→        val globalCveApprovalAdminOnly: Boolean = false
    45→    )
    46→
    47→    /**
    48→     * GET /api/settings/app
    49→     *
    50→     * Retrieve current application settings.
    51→     *
    52→     * Security: ADMIN role required
    53→     *
    54→     * Returns:
    55→     * - 200 OK: AppSettingsDto { baseUrl: String, updatedBy: String?, updatedAt: String? }
    56→     * - 401 Unauthorized: Missing or invalid JWT token
    57→     * - 403 Forbidden: User does not have ADMIN role
    58→     * - 500 Internal Server Error: Unexpected error
    59→     */
    60→    @Get
    61→    open fun getAppSettings(): HttpResponse<*> {
    62→        return try {
    63→            logger.debug("GET /api/settings/app - Fetching application settings")
    64→            val settings = appSettingsService.getSettings()
    65→            logger.debug("Application settings retrieved: baseUrl={}", settings.baseUrl)
    66→            HttpResponse.ok(settings)
    67→        } catch (e: Exception) {
    68→            logger.error("Failed to retrieve application settings", e)
    69→            HttpResponse.serverError(ErrorResponse(
    70→                status = HttpStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.code,
    71→                message = "Internal server error"
    72→            ))
    73→        }
    74→    }
    75→
    76→    /**
    77→     * PUT /api/settings/app
    78→     *
    79→     * Update application settings.
    80→     *
    81→     * Security: ADMIN role required
    82→     *
    83→     * Request Body: { baseUrl: String }
    84→     *
    85→     * Returns:
    86→     * - 200 OK: Updated AppSettingsDto
    87→     * - 400 Bad Request: Invalid base URL format
    88→     * - 401 Unauthorized: Missing or invalid JWT token
    89→     * - 403 Forbidden: User does not have ADMIN role
    90→     * - 500 Internal Server Error: Unexpected error
    91→     */
    92→    @Put
    93→    open fun updateAppSettings(
    94→        @Valid @Body request: UpdateAppSettingsRequest,
    95→        authentication: Authentication
    96→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
    97→        return try {
    98→            val username = authentication.name
    99→            logger.info("PUT /api/settings/app - User '{}' updating app settings: baseUrl={}",
   100→                username, request.baseUrl)
   101→
   102→            val updated = appSettingsService.updateSettings(request.baseUrl, username, request.globalCveApprovalAdminOnly)
   103→
   104→            logger.info("Application settings updated successfully by user '{}'", username)
   105→            HttpResponse.ok(updated)
   106→        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
   107→            logger.warn("Invalid app settings update request: {}", e.message)
   108→            HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse(
   109→                status = HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST.code,
   110→                message = e.message ?: "Invalid request"
   111→            ))
   112→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   113→            logger.error("Failed to update application settings", e)
   114→            HttpResponse.serverError(ErrorResponse(
   115→                status = HttpStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.code,
   116→                message = "Internal server error"
   117→            ))
   118→        }
   119→    }
   120→}

    38→/**
    39→ * Controller for vulnerability management and exception operations
    40→ *
    41→ * Endpoints:
    42→ * - GET /api/vulnerabilities/current - List current vulnerabilities with filters
    43→ * - GET /api/vulnerability-exceptions - List vulnerability exceptions
    44→ * - POST /api/vulnerability-exceptions - Create new exception
    45→ * - PUT /api/vulnerability-exceptions/{id} - Update exception
    46→ * - DELETE /api/vulnerability-exceptions/{id} - Delete exception
    47→ *
    48→ * Access: ADMIN, VULN, and SECCHAMPION roles only
    49→ * Feature: 025-role-based-access-control
    50→ *
    51→ * Related to: Feature 004-i-want-to (VULN Role & Vulnerability Management UI)
    52→ */
    53→@Controller
    54→@Secured("ADMIN", "VULN", "SECCHAMPION")
    55→@ExecuteOn(TaskExecutors.BLOCKING)
    56→open class VulnerabilityManagementController(
    57→    private val vulnerabilityService: VulnerabilityService,
    58→    private val vulnerabilityExceptionService: VulnerabilityExceptionService,
    59→    private val assetFilterService: AssetFilterService,
    60→    private val vulnerabilityTimestampMigrationService: com.secman.service.VulnerabilityTimestampMigrationService,
    61→    private val vulnerabilityExportService: VulnerabilityExportService,
    62→    private val exportJobService: ExportJobService
    63→) {
    64→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(VulnerabilityManagementController::class.java)
    65→
    66→    @Serdeable
    67→    data class ErrorResponse(val error: String)
    68→
    69→    /**
    70→     * Get current vulnerabilities (latest scan per asset) with exception status
    71→     * Feature: 008-create-an-additional (Workgroup-Based Access Control)
    72→     *
    73→     * GET /api/vulnerabilities/current?severity=Critical&system=server&exceptionStatus=not_excepted&product=zlib&adDomain=CONTOSO&cloudAccountId=123456789012&page=0&size=50
    74→     * Auth: ADMIN or VULN role
    75→     * Response: PaginatedVulnerabilitiesResponse
    76→     *
    77→     * FR-014, FR-016, FR-018: ADMIN sees all, VULN role respects workgroup restrictions
    78→     *
    79→     * Query Parameters:
    80→     * - severity: Optional CVSS severity filter (e.g., "Critical", "High", "Medium", "Low")
    81→     * - system: Optional system/asset name filter (substring match, case-insensitive)
    82→     * - exceptionStatus: Optional filter - "excepted", "not_excepted", or omit for all
    83→     * - product: Optional product/version filter (substring match, case-insensitive)
    84→     * - adDomain: Optional AD domain filter (substring match, case-insensitive)
    85→     * - cloudAccountId: Optional AWS cloud account ID filter (substring match, case-insensitive)
    86→     * - page: Page number (0-indexed, default: 0)
    87→     * - size: Page size (default: 50, max: 500)
    88→     * - sort: Sort field (e.g., "assetName", "cvssSeverity", "scanTimestamp", "vulnerabilityId", "vulnerableProductVersions", "daysOpen")
    89→     * - sortDir: Sort direction ("asc" or "desc", default: "desc")
    90→     */
    91→    @Get("/api/vulnerabilities/current")
    92→    @Secured("ADMIN", "VULN", "SECCHAMPION")
    93→    open fun getCurrentVulnerabilities(
    94→        authentication: Authentication,
    95→        @Nullable @QueryValue severity: String?,
    96→        @Nullable @QueryValue system: String?,
    97→        @Nullable @QueryValue exceptionStatus: String?,
    98→        @Nullable @QueryValue product: String?,
    99→        @Nullable @QueryValue adDomain: String?,
   100→        @Nullable @QueryValue cloudAccountId: String?,

     5→import com.secman.repository.UserRepository

     8→import io.micronaut.http.HttpResponse
     9→import io.micronaut.http.HttpStatus
    10→import io.micronaut.http.annotation.*
    11→import io.micronaut.security.annotation.Secured
    12→import io.micronaut.security.authentication.Authentication
    13→import io.micronaut.security.rules.SecurityRule

    20→/**
    21→ * Controller for user profile operations
    22→ * Feature 028: User Profile Page
    23→ * Feature 051: User Password Change
    24→ *
    25→ * Endpoints:
    26→ * - GET /api/users/profile: Returns current user's profile data
    27→ * - PUT /api/users/profile/change-password: Changes current user's password
    28→ *
    29→ * Security: All endpoints require authentication
    30→ */
    31→@Controller("/api/users")
    32→@Secured(SecurityRule.IS_AUTHENTICATED)
    33→open class UserProfileController(
    34→    private val userRepository: UserRepository,
    35→    private val webAuthnService: WebAuthnService,
    36→    private val auditLogService: AuditLogService
    37→) {
    38→    private val logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(UserProfileController::class.java)
    39→    private val passwordEncoder = BCryptPasswordEncoder()
    40→
    41→    @Serdeable
    42→    data class ErrorResponse(
    43→        val message: String
    44→    )
    45→
    46→    @Serdeable
    47→    data class MfaToggleRequest(
    48→        val enabled: Boolean
    49→    )
    50→
    51→    @Serdeable
    52→    data class MfaStatusResponse(
    53→        val enabled: Boolean,
    54→        val passkeyCount: Int,
    55→        val canDisable: Boolean,
    56→        val message: String? = null
    57→    )
    58→
    59→    /**
    60→     * Request DTO for password change
    61→     * Feature 051: User Password Change
    62→     */
    63→    @Serdeable
    64→    data class ChangePasswordRequest(
    65→        val currentPassword: String,
    66→        val newPassword: String,
    67→        val confirmPassword: String
    68→    )
    69→
    70→    /**
    71→     * Response DTO for password change
    72→     * Feature 051: User Password Change
    73→     */
    74→    @Serdeable
    75→    data class ChangePasswordResponse(
    76→        val success: Boolean,
    77→        val message: String
    78→    )
    79→
    80→    /**
    81→     * Get current user's profile
    82→     *
    83→     * Retrieves profile information (username, email, roles) for the authenticated user.
    84→     * User is identified from the JWT token in the Authorization header.
    85→     *
    86→     * @param authentication Micronaut Security authentication object
    87→     * @return HttpResponse with UserProfileDto or 404 error
    88→     */
    89→    @Get("/profile")
    90→    fun getCurrentUserProfile(authentication: Authentication): HttpResponse<*> {
    91→        val username = authentication.name
    92→        logger.debug("Fetching profile for user: {}", username)
    93→
    94→        val userOptional = userRepository.findByUsername(username)
    95→        if (userOptional.isEmpty) {
    96→            logger.warn("User not found: {}", username)
    97→            return HttpResponse.notFound(ErrorResponse("User not found"))
    98→        }
    99→
   100→        val user = userOptional.get()
   101→        logger.debug("Profile retrieved for user: {}", username)
   102→        return HttpResponse.ok(UserProfileDto.fromUser(user))
   103→    }
   104→
   105→    /**
   106→     * Get MFA status for current user
   107→     * Feature: Passkey MFA Support
   108→     *
   109→     * GET /api/users/profile/mfa-status
   110→     */
   111→    @Get("/profile/mfa-status")
   112→    fun getMfaStatus(authentication: Authentication): HttpResponse<*> {
   113→        try {
   114→            val username = authentication.name
   115→            val user = userRepository.findByUsername(username).orElseThrow {
   116→                IllegalArgumentException("User not found")
   117→            }
   118→
   119→            val passkeys = webAuthnService.getUserPasskeys(user)
   120→            val passkeyCount = passkeys.size
   121→
   122→            val response = MfaStatusResponse(
   123→                enabled = user.mfaEnabled,
   124→                passkeyCount = passkeyCount,
   125→                canDisable = passkeyCount == 0 || !user.mfaEnabled,
   126→                message = when {
   127→                    user.mfaEnabled && passkeyCount == 0 -> "MFA is enabled but no passkeys are registered. Please register a passkey."
   128→                    user.mfaEnabled && passkeyCount > 0 -> "MFA is enabled with $passkeyCount passkey(s) registered."
   129→                    !user.mfaEnabled && passkeyCount > 0 -> "MFA is disabled. You have $passkeyCount passkey(s) registered but not actively used."
   130→                    else -> "MFA is disabled. Register a passkey to enable MFA."
   131→                }
   132→            )
   133→
   134→            return HttpResponse.ok(response)
   135→
   136→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   137→            logger.error("Failed to get MFA status", e)
   138→            return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf("error" to "An internal error occurred"))
   139→        }
   140→    }
   141→
   142→    /**
   143→     * Toggle MFA on/off for current user
   144→     * Feature: Passkey MFA Support
   145→     *
   146→     * PUT /api/users/profile/mfa-toggle
   147→     */
   148→    @Put("/profile/mfa-toggle")
   149→    @Transactional
   150→    open fun toggleMfa(
   151→        @Valid @Body request: MfaToggleRequest,
   152→        authentication: Authentication
   153→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
   154→        try {
   155→            val username = authentication.name
   156→            val user = userRepository.findByUsername(username).orElseThrow {
   157→                IllegalArgumentException("User not found")
   158→            }
   159→
   160→            // Check if user has passkeys when enabling MFA
   161→            if (request.enabled) {
   162→                val passkeyCount = webAuthnService.getUserPasskeys(user).size
   163→                if (passkeyCount == 0) {
   164→                    return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf(
   165→                        "error" to "Cannot enable MFA without registering at least one passkey",
   166→                        "message" to "Please register a passkey before enabling MFA"
   167→                    ))
   168→                }
   169→            }
   170→
   171→            user.mfaEnabled = request.enabled
   172→            userRepository.update(user)
   173→
   174→            logger.info("User ${user.username} (ID: ${user.id}) ${if (request.enabled) "enabled" else "disabled"} MFA")
   175→
   176→            return HttpResponse.ok(mapOf(
   177→                "success" to true,
   178→                "mfaEnabled" to user.mfaEnabled,
   179→                "message" to "MFA ${if (request.enabled) "enabled" else "disabled"} successfully"
   180→            ))
   181→
   182→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   183→            logger.error("Failed to toggle MFA", e)
   184→            return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf("error" to "An internal error occurred"))
   185→        }
   186→    }
   187→
   188→    /**
   189→     * Change current user's password
   190→     * Feature 051: User Password Change
   191→     *
   192→     * PUT /api/users/profile/change-password
   193→     *
   194→     * Requirements:
   195→     * - FR-001: Accessible to authenticated users with local accounts
   196→     * - FR-002: Requires current password verification
   197→     * - FR-003, FR-004: Requires new password and confirmation to match
   198→     * - FR-005: Minimum 8 characters
   199→     * - FR-006: Must differ from current password
   200→     * - FR-007: Clear error messages
   201→     * - FR-008: Success message on completion
   202→     * - FR-009: OAuth users cannot change password
   203→     * - FR-010: Secure password hashing
   204→     */
   205→    @Put("/profile/change-password")
   206→    @Transactional
   207→    open fun changePassword(
   208→        @Valid @Body request: ChangePasswordRequest,
   209→        authentication: Authentication
   210→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
   211→        val username = authentication.name
   212→        logger.debug("Password change request for user: {}", username)
   213→
   214→        // Find user
   215→        val userOptional = userRepository.findByUsername(username)
   216→        if (userOptional.isEmpty) {
   217→            logger.warn("Password change attempted for non-existent user: {}", username)
   218→            return HttpResponse.notFound(ChangePasswordResponse(false, "User not found"))
   219→        }
   220→
   221→        val user = userOptional.get()
   222→
   223→        // FR-009: Check if user can change password (not OAuth-only)
   224→        if (user.authSource == User.AuthSource.OAUTH) {
   225→            logger.warn("OAuth user {} attempted to change password", username)
   226→            // FR-011: Audit log failed attempt
   227→            auditLogService.logAction(
   228→                authentication = authentication,
   229→                action = "PASSWORD_CHANGE_FAILED",
   230→                entityType = "User",
   231→                entityId = user.id,
   232→                details = "OAuth user attempted password change"
   233→            )
   234→            return HttpResponse.status<ChangePasswordResponse>(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN)
   235→                .body(ChangePasswordResponse(false, "Password change is not available for OAuth accounts"))
   236→        }
   237→
   238→        // FR-002: Verify current password
   239→        if (!passwordEncoder.matches(request.currentPassword, user.passwordHash)) {
   240→            logger.warn("Invalid current password for user: {}", username)
   241→            // FR-011: Audit log failed attempt
   242→            auditLogService.logAction(
   243→                authentication = authentication,
   244→                action = "PASSWORD_CHANGE_FAILED",
   245→                entityType = "User",
   246→                entityId = user.id,
   247→                details = "Invalid current password provided"
   248→            )
   249→            return HttpResponse.badRequest(ChangePasswordResponse(false, "Current password is incorrect"))
   250→        }
   251→
   252→        // FR-003, FR-004: Verify new password and confirmation match
   253→        if (request.newPassword != request.confirmPassword) {
   254→            return HttpResponse.badRequest(ChangePasswordResponse(false, "New password and confirmation do not match"))
   255→        }
   256→
   257→        // FR-005: Minimum length validation
   258→        if (request.newPassword.length < 8) {
   259→            return HttpResponse.badRequest(ChangePasswordResponse(false, "Password must be at least 8 characters"))
   260→        }
   261→
   262→        // FR-020 (from US2): Max length validation
   263→        if (request.newPassword.length > 200) {
   264→            return HttpResponse.badRequest(ChangePasswordResponse(false, "Password exceeds maximum length"))
   265→        }
   266→
   267→        // FR-006: Must differ from current password
   268→        if (passwordEncoder.matches(request.newPassword, user.passwordHash)) {
   269→            return HttpResponse.badRequest(ChangePasswordResponse(false, "New password must be different from current password"))
   270→        }
   271→
   272→        // FR-010: Update password with secure hashing
   273→        user.passwordHash = passwordEncoder.encode(request.newPassword)!!
   274→        userRepository.update(user)
   275→
   276→        logger.info("Password changed successfully for user: {} (ID: {})", username, user.id)
   277→
   278→        // FR-011: Audit log successful password change
   279→        auditLogService.logAction(
   280→            authentication = authentication,
   281→            action = "PASSWORD_CHANGED",
   282→            entityType = "User",
   283→            entityId = user.id,
   284→            details = "Password changed successfully via self-service"
   285→        )
   286→
   287→        // FR-008: Success message
   288→        return HttpResponse.ok(ChangePasswordResponse(true, "Password changed successfully"))
   289→    }
   290→}
   291→

     6→/**
     7→ * Data Transfer Object for user profile API responses
     8→ * Feature 028: User Profile Page
     9→ * Feature 051: User Password Change (added canChangePassword)
    10→ *
    11→ * Exposes only safe, user-visible fields:
    12→ * - username: User's display name
    13→ * - email: User's email address
    14→ * - roles: User's assigned roles
    15→ * - canChangePassword: Whether user can change their password (LOCAL/HYBRID users only)
    16→ *
    17→ * Security: Excludes passwordHash, id, timestamps, and workgroups
    18→ */
    19→@Serdeable
    20→data class UserProfileDto(
    21→    val username: String,
    22→    val email: String,
    23→    val roles: Set<String>,
    24→    val canChangePassword: Boolean = true
    25→) {
    26→    companion object {
    27→        /**
    28→         * Factory method to create UserProfileDto from User entity
    29→         * Implements defensive programming with null-safe email handling
    30→         *
    31→         * @param user The User entity to convert
    32→         * @return UserProfileDto with user data
    33→         */
    34→        fun fromUser(user: User): UserProfileDto {
    35→            return UserProfileDto(
    36→                username = user.username,
    37→                email = user.email,
    38→                roles = user.roles.map { it.name }.toSet(),
    39→                canChangePassword = user.authSource != User.AuthSource.OAUTH
    40→            )
    41→        }
    42→    }
    43→}

    16→/**
    17→ * REST controller for AWS Account Sharing management.
    18→ *
    19→ * Feature: AWS Account Sharing
    20→ *
    21→ * - ADMIN users: full access to all sharing rules (list all, create any, delete any)
    22→ * - Non-admin users with AWS accounts: scoped access to their own outgoing sharing rules
    23→ *   (list own, create where they are source, delete their own)
    24→ */
    25→@Controller("/api/aws-account-sharing")
    26→@Secured(SecurityRule.IS_AUTHENTICATED)
    27→open class AwsAccountSharingController(
    28→    private val awsAccountSharingService: AwsAccountSharingService,
    29→    private val userRepository: UserRepository
    30→) {
    31→    private val logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(AwsAccountSharingController::class.java)
    32→
    33→    /**
    34→     * GET /api/aws-account-sharing - List sharing rules.
    35→     * ADMINs see all rules; non-admins see only rules where they are the source user.
    36→     */
    37→    @Get
    38→    fun listSharingRules(
    39→        @QueryValue("page") page: Int?,
    40→        @QueryValue("size") size: Int?,
    41→        authentication: Authentication
    42→    ): HttpResponse<Map<String, Any>> {
    43→        logger.debug("Listing AWS account sharing rules: page=$page, size=$size")
    44→
    45→        return try {
    46→            val result = if (isAdmin(authentication)) {
    47→                awsAccountSharingService.listSharingRules(page ?: 0, size ?: 20)
    48→            } else {
    49→                val userId = getUserIdFromAuthentication(authentication)
    50→                awsAccountSharingService.listSharingRulesForSourceUser(userId, page ?: 0, size ?: 20)
    51→            }
    52→            HttpResponse.ok(result)
    53→        } catch (e: Exception) {
    54→            logger.error("Failed to list AWS account sharing rules", e)
    55→            HttpResponse.serverError(mapOf(
    56→                "error" to "Internal Server Error",
    57→                "message" to "Failed to list sharing rules"
    58→            ))
    59→        }
    60→    }
    61→
    62→    /**
    63→     * POST /api/aws-account-sharing - Create a new sharing rule.
    64→     * ADMINs can create any rule; non-admins can only create rules where they are the source.
    65→     */
    66→    @Post
    67→    open fun createSharingRule(
    68→        @Valid @Body request: CreateAwsAccountSharingRequest,
    69→        authentication: Authentication
    70→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
    71→        logger.info("Creating AWS account sharing rule: source=${request.sourceUserId}, target=${request.targetUserId}")
    72→
    73→        return try {
    74→            val currentUserId = getUserIdFromAuthentication(authentication)
    75→
    76→            // Non-admin users can only share their own accounts
    77→            if (!isAdmin(authentication) && request.sourceUserId != currentUserId) {
    78→                return HttpResponse.status<Any>(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN).body(mapOf(
    79→                    "error" to "Forbidden",
    80→                    "message" to "You can only share your own AWS accounts"
    81→                ))
    82→            }
    83→
    84→            val result = awsAccountSharingService.createSharingRule(request, currentUserId)
    85→            HttpResponse.status<Any>(HttpStatus.CREATED).body(result)
    86→        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
    87→            logger.warn("AWS account sharing creation failed: ${e.message}")
    88→            HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf(
    89→                "error" to "Validation Error",
    90→                "message" to (e.message ?: "Invalid request")
    91→            ))
    92→        } catch (e: IllegalStateException) {
    93→            logger.warn("AWS account sharing creation failed: ${e.message}")
    94→            HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf(
    95→                "error" to "Conflict",
    96→                "message" to (e.message ?: "Sharing rule already exists")
    97→            ))
    98→        } catch (e: NoSuchElementException) {
    99→            HttpResponse.notFound(mapOf(
   100→                "error" to "Not Found",
   101→                "message" to (e.message ?: "User not found")
   102→            ))
   103→        }
   104→    }
   105→
   106→    /**
   107→     * DELETE /api/aws-account-sharing/{id} - Delete a sharing rule.
   108→     * ADMINs can delete any rule; non-admins can only delete rules where they are the source.
   109→     */
   110→    @Delete("/{id}")
   111→    fun deleteSharingRule(
   112→        @PathVariable id: Long,
   113→        authentication: Authentication
   114→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
   115→        logger.info("Deleting AWS account sharing rule: id=$id")
   116→
   117→        return try {
   118→            // Non-admin users can only delete their own rules
   119→            if (!isAdmin(authentication)) {
   120→                val currentUserId = getUserIdFromAuthentication(authentication)
   121→                if (!awsAccountSharingService.isOwnedByUser(id, currentUserId)) {
   122→                    return HttpResponse.status<Any>(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN).body(mapOf(
   123→                        "error" to "Forbidden",
   124→                        "message" to "You can only delete your own sharing rules"
   125→                    ))
   126→                }
   127→            }
   128→
   129→            awsAccountSharingService.deleteSharingRule(id)
   130→            HttpResponse.noContent<Void>()
   131→        } catch (e: NoSuchElementException) {
   132→            logger.warn("AWS account sharing rule not found: $id")
   133→            HttpResponse.notFound<Any>()
   134→        }
   135→    }
   136→
   137→    private fun getUserIdFromAuthentication(authentication: Authentication): Long {
   138→        val userId = authentication.attributes["userId"]
   139→        return when (userId) {
   140→            is Long -> userId
   141→            is Int -> userId.toLong()
   142→            is String -> userId.toLong()
   143→            else -> throw IllegalStateException("Unable to determine user ID from authentication")
   144→        }
   145→    }
   146→
   147→    /**
   148→     * GET /api/aws-account-sharing/users - Lightweight user list for the sharing form.
   149→     * Returns minimal user info (id, username, email) for populating target user dropdowns.
   150→     * Available to all authenticated users so non-admins can select sharing targets.
   151→     */
   152→    @Get("/users")
   153→    fun listUsersForSharing(): HttpResponse<List<SharingUserResponse>> {
   154→        val users = userRepository.findAll().map { user ->
   155→            SharingUserResponse(
   156→                id = user.id!!,
   157→                username = user.username,
   158→                email = user.email
   159→            )
   160→        }
   161→        return HttpResponse.ok(users)
   162→    }
   163→
   164→    private fun isAdmin(authentication: Authentication): Boolean {
   165→        return authentication.roles.contains("ADMIN")
   166→    }
   167→
   168→    @Serdeable
   169→    data class SharingUserResponse(
   170→        val id: Long,
   171→        val username: String,
   172→        val email: String
   173→    )
   174→}

    35→    35→    35→
    36→    36→    36→/**
    37→    37→    37→ * OAuth/OIDC Authentication Service
    38→    38→    38→ *
    39→    39→    39→ * Handles complete OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect (OIDC) authentication flows for external identity providers.
    40→    40→    40→ * This is the core authentication service for all single sign-on (SSO) integrations in Secman.
    41→    41→    41→ *
    42→    42→    42→ * ## Supported Identity Providers
    43→    43→    43→ * - **Microsoft Azure AD / Entra ID**: Full OIDC support with tenant validation, JWKS signature verification
    44→    44→    44→ * - **GitHub**: OAuth 2.0 with user info endpoint for profile data
    45→    45→    45→ * - **Generic OIDC**: Any standards-compliant OIDC provider via dynamic configuration
    46→    46→    46→ *
    47→    47→    47→ * ## OAuth Flow Architecture
    48→    48→    48→ *
    49→    49→    49→ * The service implements a standard OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code flow with OIDC extensions:
    50→    50→    50→ *
    51→    51→    51→ * ```
    52→    52→    52→ * ┌─────────┐     ┌─────────────┐     ┌────────────────┐     ┌─────────────┐
    53→    53→    53→ * │ Browser │────▶│ OAuthCtrl   │────▶│ OAuthService   │────▶│ IdP (Azure/ │
    54→    54→    54→ * │         │◀────│ /authorize  │◀────│ buildAuthUrl() │◀────│ GitHub/etc) │
    55→    55→    55→ * └─────────┘     └─────────────┘     └────────────────┘     └─────────────┘
    56→    56→    56→ *      │                                      │
    57→    57→    57→ *      │ 1. User clicks "Login with..."       │ 2. Generate state, save to DB
    58→    58→    58→ *      │                                      │
    59→    59→    59→ *      ▼                                      ▼
    60→    60→    60→ * ┌─────────┐     ┌─────────────┐     ┌────────────────┐     ┌─────────────┐
    61→    61→    61→ * │ Browser │────▶│ OAuthCtrl   │────▶│ OAuthService   │────▶│ IdP Token   │
    62→    62→    62→ * │         │◀────│ /callback   │◀────│ handleCallback │◀────│ Endpoint    │
    63→    63→    63→ * └─────────┘     └─────────────┘     └────────────────┘     └─────────────┘
    64→    64→    64→ *                                             │
    65→    65→    65→ *      3. IdP redirects with code ───────────▶│ 4. Exchange code for token
    66→    66→    66→ *                                             │ 5. Validate ID token (OIDC)
    67→    67→    67→ *      6. Return JWT for frontend ◀───────────│ 7. Find or create user
    68→    68→    68→ * ```
    69→    69→    69→ *
    70→    70→    70→ * ## Race Condition Handling (Microsoft Azure SSO)
    71→    71→    71→ *
    72→    72→    72→ * **Problem**: Microsoft Azure callbacks can arrive within 100-500ms when users have cached SSO sessions.
    73→    73→    73→ * This creates a race condition where the OAuth callback arrives before the state-save transaction
    74→    74→    74→ * is fully committed and visible to other database connections.
    75→    75→    75→ *
    76→    76→    76→ * **Solution**: This service implements two complementary strategies:
    77→    77→    77→ *
    78→    78→    78→ * 1. **Immediate State Commit**: [saveOAuthStateImmediately] uses `REQUIRES_NEW` transaction
    79→    79→    79→ *    with explicit `entityManager.flush()` to ensure the state is committed before redirect.
    80→    80→    80→ *
    81→    81→    81→ * 2. **Retry with Exponential Backoff**: [findStateByValueWithRetry] retries state lookups
    82→    82→    82→ *    with configurable backoff (default: 100ms → 150ms → 225ms → 337ms → 500ms).
    83→    83→    83→ *
    84→    84→    84→ * Configuration via environment variables or `application.yml`:
    85→    85→    85→ * ```yaml
    86→    86→    86→ * secman:
    87→    87→    87→ *   oauth:
    88→    88→    88→ *     state-retry:
    89→    89→    89→ *       max-attempts: 5           # OAUTH_STATE_RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS
    90→    90→    90→ *       initial-delay-ms: 100     # OAUTH_STATE_RETRY_INITIAL_DELAY
    91→    91→    91→ *       max-delay-ms: 500         # OAUTH_STATE_RETRY_MAX_DELAY
    92→    92→    92→ *       backoff-multiplier: 1.5   # OAUTH_STATE_RETRY_BACKOFF_MULTIPLIER
    93→    93→    93→ *     token-exchange:
    94→    94→    94→ *       max-retries: 2            # OAUTH_TOKEN_EXCHANGE_MAX_RETRIES
    95→    95→    95→ *       retry-delay-ms: 500       # OAUTH_TOKEN_EXCHANGE_RETRY_DELAY
    96→    96→    96→ * ```
    97→    97→    97→ *
    98→    98→    98→ * ## Security Features
    99→    99→    99→ *
   100→   100→   100→ * - **State Parameter**: CSRF protection via cryptographically random state tokens
   101→   101→   101→ * - **JWKS Validation**: ID token signatures verified against provider's JWKS endpoint
   102→   102→   102→ * - **Tenant Validation**: Microsoft Azure tenant ID verified against configured tenant
   103→   103→   103→ * - **Token Expiry**: OAuth states auto-expire (default 10 minutes) with scheduled cleanup
   104→   104→   104→ * - **Audit Logging**: Security events logged to `security.audit` logger for compliance
   105→   105→   105→ *
   106→   106→   106→ * ## User Provisioning
   107→   107→   107→ *
   108→   108→   108→ * When a user authenticates via OAuth for the first time:
   109→   109→   109→ * 1. User is auto-created if `autoProvision` is enabled on the identity provider
   110→   110→   110→ * 2. Default roles (USER, VULN) are assigned per Feature 046
   111→   111→   111→ * 3. [UserCreatedEvent] is published for user mapping application (Feature 042)
   112→   112→   112→ * 4. Admin notifications sent asynchronously (Feature 027)
   113→   113→   113→ * 5. Existing users preserve their roles on subsequent logins (FR-006)
   114→   114→   114→ *
   115→   115→   115→ * ## Error Handling
   116→   116→   116→ *
   117→   117→   117→ * User-friendly error messages are provided via [OAuthErrorCode] enum:
   118→   118→   118→ * - State validation errors (not found, expired, mismatch)
   119→   119→   119→ * - Token exchange failures (timeout, server error)
   120→   120→   120→ * - User provisioning failures
   121→   121→   121→ *
   122→   122→   122→ * Microsoft-specific errors are mapped via [MicrosoftErrorMapper] for clearer diagnostics.
   123→   123→   123→ *
   124→   124→   124→ * ## Related Components
   125→   125→   125→ *
   126→   126→   126→ * - [OAuthController]: REST endpoints for OAuth flow (`/oauth/authorize`, `/oauth/callback`)
   127→   127→   127→ * - [OAuthConfig]: Retry and timeout configuration
   128→   128→   128→ * - [JwksValidationService]: JWT signature validation using JWKS
   129→   129→   129→ * - [IdentityProvider]: Database entity for provider configuration
   130→   130→   130→ * - [OAuthState]: Database entity for state parameter storage
   131→   131→   131→ * - [MicrosoftErrorMapper]: Azure AD error code translation
   132→   132→   132→ *
   133→   133→   133→ * ## Logging
   134→   134→   134→ *
   135→   135→   135→ * - Standard logs: `com.secman.service.OAuthService`
   136→   136→   136→ * - Security audit: `security.audit` (for compliance/SIEM integration)
   137→   137→   137→ * - Correlation IDs: Each OAuth flow gets a unique correlation ID for tracing
   138→   138→   138→ *
   139→   139→   139→ * @see OAuthController
   140→   140→   140→ * @see OAuthConfig
   141→   141→   141→ * @see JwksValidationService
   142→   142→   142→ * @see IdentityProvider
   143→   143→   143→ * @see MicrosoftErrorMapper
   144→   144→   144→ * @since 1.0
   145→   145→   145→ * @author Secman Development Team
   146→   146→   146→ */
   147→   147→   147→@Singleton
   148→   148→   148→open class OAuthService(
   149→   149→   149→    private val identityProviderRepository: IdentityProviderRepository,
   150→   150→   150→    private val oauthStateRepository: OAuthStateRepository,

    73→            logger.info("Backend Base URL (from config): {}", backendBaseUrl)
    74→            logger.info("Request URI: {}", request.uri)

    77→            // Log whether a stale auth cookie is present (helps diagnose new-user failures)
    78→            val hasExistingCookie = request.cookies.get(AuthCookieService.AUTH_COOKIE_NAME) != null
    79→            if (hasExistingCookie) {

    98→                HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("Failed to build authorization URL"))
    99→            }

1→package com.secman.util
     2→
     3→import jakarta.persistence.AttributeConverter
     4→import jakarta.persistence.Converter
     5→import org.springframework.security.crypto.encrypt.Encryptors
     6→import org.springframework.security.crypto.encrypt.TextEncryptor
     7→import java.security.SecureRandom
     8→import java.util.*
     9→
    10→@Converter
    11→class EncryptedStringConverter : AttributeConverter<String?, String?> {
    12→
    13→    companion object {
    14→        private const val ENCRYPTION_PASSWORD_PROPERTY = "secman.encryption.password"
    15→        private const val ENCRYPTION_SALT_PROPERTY = "secman.encryption.salt"
    16→
    17→        // Default values for development - should be overridden in production
    18→        private const val DEFAULT_PASSWORD = "SecManDefaultEncryptionPassword2024"
    19→        private const val DEFAULT_SALT = "5c0744940b5c369b"
    20→
    21→        private val textEncryptor: TextEncryptor by lazy {
    22→            val password = System.getProperty(ENCRYPTION_PASSWORD_PROPERTY)
    23→                ?: System.getenv("SECMAN_ENCRYPTION_PASSWORD")
    24→                ?: DEFAULT_PASSWORD
    25→
    26→            val salt = System.getProperty(ENCRYPTION_SALT_PROPERTY)
    27→                ?: System.getenv("SECMAN_ENCRYPTION_SALT")
    28→                ?: DEFAULT_SALT
    29→
    30→            Encryptors.text(password, salt)
    31→        }
    32→
    33→        /**
    34→         * Generate a random salt for production use
    35→         */
    36→        fun generateSalt(): String {
    37→            val bytes = ByteArray(8)
    38→            SecureRandom().nextBytes(bytes)
    39→            return bytes.joinToString("") { String.format("%02x", it) }
    40→        }
    41→
    42→        /**
    43→         * Generate a random password for production use
    44→         */
    45→        fun generatePassword(): String {
    46→            val bytes = ByteArray(32)
    47→            SecureRandom().nextBytes(bytes)
    48→            return Base64.getEncoder().encodeToString(bytes)
    49→        }
    50→    }
    51→
    52→    /**
    53→     * Convert the entity attribute to a database column value.
    54→     * Encrypts the plaintext string for storage.
    55→     */
    56→    override fun convertToDatabaseColumn(attribute: String?): String? {
    57→        return attribute?.let { plaintext ->
    58→            try {
    59→                textEncryptor.encrypt(plaintext)
    60→            } catch (e: Exception) {
    61→                throw RuntimeException("Failed to encrypt sensitive data", e)
    62→            }
    63→        }
    64→    }
    65→
    66→    /**
    67→     * Convert the database column value to an entity attribute.
    68→     * Decrypts the encrypted string for use in the application.
    69→     */
    70→    override fun convertToEntityAttribute(dbData: String?): String? {
    71→        return dbData?.let { encrypted ->
    72→            try {
    73→                textEncryptor.decrypt(encrypted)
    74→            } catch (e: Exception) {
    75→                throw RuntimeException("Failed to decrypt sensitive data. " +
    76→                    "This may indicate corrupted data or mismatched encryption keys.", e)
    77→            }
    78→        }
    79→    }
    80→}
